tasks.title: Reconcile verify_uniqueness warn test block: stale comment, verdict-policy consistency, non-numeric coverage
tasks.description:
Three interrelated follow-up suggestions from reviewer_comprehensive on Task 1249 (which itself folded Task 1234's review). All three address the same test block in crates/reify-constraints/src/solver.rs (roughly lines 1141-1372) and should be done together to avoid merge conflicts and to pick a coherent verdict-coverage policy in one pass.

(S1 — stale comment in happy-path test) In `verify_uniqueness_no_warn_when_param_has_valid_f64` (~line 1214), the NB comment block (~lines 1247-1252) still says `` `_unique` keeps captured-value symmetry with the sibling tests `` but Task 1249 already renamed the binding from `_unique` to `_`. The sibling warn tests were also renamed from `let unique = ...` to `let _ = ...` in that same diff, so the "symmetry" clause is stale in two ways: the name doesn't exist, and the "symmetry" it claims is now "all bindings are `_`". Rewrite the comment to drop the `_unique`/symmetry framing and keep only the substantive point: the test's contract is the *absence* of the fallback warn on the happy-path `as_f64() == Some` branch, not any uniqueness verdict. (Do NOT restore the `_unique` binding — `let _ = expr` is idiomatic per Task 1249's S3 rationale.)

(S2 — verdict-policy consistency) `verify_uniqueness_aggregates_warn_for_multiple_missing_params` (~line 1261) still has `assert!(!unique, ...)` at ~line 1297, even though Task 1249 removed the equivalent assertion from the two single-param warn tests on the grounds that the verdict is implicitly coupled to `solve_core` converging on the perturbed point. The exact same fragility applies to the aggregation test (trivial no-constraint problem, perturbation anchor falls back to midpoint for both params). The current mix is inconsistent. Pick one policy and apply it uniformly:
  (a) Preferred: also drop `assert!(!unique, ...)` from the aggregation test and change `let unique = ...` to `let _ = ...` there, so all three warn-focused tests share a single "verdict is out-of-contract" posture. Verdict coverage for the missing-param case is already provided deterministically by `verify_uniqueness_skips_solve_core_when_param_missing` (~line 1314), which uses a `CountingSubscriberBuilder` to prove the early-return via DEBUG==0 and asserts `!unique`.
  (b) Alternative: restore the assertions in the two single-param tests. Only pick this if review concludes the fragility concern is hypothetical and convergence on the trivial problem is guaranteed. This is NOT the recommended path because it undoes Task 1249 S1.

(S3 — non-numeric verdict coverage gap) The single-param **non-numeric** (`Value::Undef` → `as_f64() == None`) path has no verdict assertion anywhere in the test suite after Task 1249. The single-param **missing** path is still covered by `verify_uniqueness_skips_solve_core_when_param_missing`, and the multi-missing path is covered by the aggregation test (in option 2a above, only until we drop the verdict there — at which point it'd have to also move to a deterministic test). Add a dedicated `verify_uniqueness_skips_solve_core_when_param_non_numeric` test modeled on `verify_uniqueness_skips_solve_core_when_param_missing` (~line 1314): same CountingSubscriberBuilder setup counting WARN+DEBUG with target_prefix "reify_constraints", same WARN==1 / DEBUG==0 / !unique assertions, but with `solved_values` containing `Value::Undef` for the single param instead of being empty. This closes the non-numeric verdict-coverage gap and proves the early-return path fires deterministically for that branch too.

All three suggestions are test-only, single-file (`crates/reify-constraints/src/solver.rs`), and small. Expected net diff: rewrite a ~6-line comment block, delete one `assert!(!unique)` line + rename one `let` binding, add one ~60-line test function.

Parent: Task 1249 (which itself is the follow-up to Task 1234's review). Reviewer: reviewer_comprehensive (S1 + S2 + S3 on Task 1249's diff).

tasks.title: Kind-aware seen_defaults dedup in collect_all_requirements
tasks.description:
The `seen_defaults` dedup in `collect_all_requirements` (lib.rs ~line 7892) compares only by type, not by kind (Param vs Let). When TraitA provides `param x : Real = 1` and TraitB provides `let x : Real = 42`, the Let default is silently discarded because it matches the existing Param entry on type. A third trait requiring `let x : Real` then sees only the Param default, fails kind-matching, and reports a spurious "missing member" error.

Fix: treat same-name, same-type, different-kind defaults as a conflict in the dedup logic, or store both kinds in `available_defaults` (e.g., keyed by (name, kind) instead of just name) so both remain available for requirement matching.

Add assertions like `assert_eq!(x_cells[0].cell_type, Type::Length, ...)` and `assert!(x_cells[0].default_expr.is_some(), ...)` to catch regressions where type or expression is lost during injection.

2. **Diamond inheritance test**: No test covers the diamond pattern where a param requirement from a grandparent trait is satisfied by a same-kind default from a sibling trait through refinement. Example: `trait Base { param x : Real }`, `trait Left : Base { }`, `trait Right : Base { param x : Real = 1.0 }`, `structure S : Left + Right {}` — verify no error and correct cell materialization. This exercises the `visited` dedup and kind-matching interaction together.

tasks.title: Improve guard compilation failure handling: distinguish no-guard from broken-guard, filter by severity
tasks.description:
Two issues in the guard compilation fallback at lib.rs:5761-5772:

1. **Misleading cascade**: Setting `guard_expr = None` when guard compilation fails prevents the old "guard references no Int/Bool param" cascade, but introduces a new one — the termination check sees `guard_expr: None` and emits "recursive sub has no termination condition: add a where clause" even though the user DID write a where clause. Fix: add a `guard_compile_failed: bool` field to `SubComponentDecl` (or use a sentinel like `guard_expr: Some(POISON)`) so the termination check can distinguish "no guard written" from "guard written but broken" and skip checking entirely for the latter.

2. **Severity filtering**: The `diagnostics.len()` delta approach counts ALL diagnostic severities. If `compile_expr` ever emits a warning for a valid guard, the guard would be incorrectly dropped. Fix: filter by `Severity::Error` instead of comparing total lengths: `diagnostics[diag_count_before..].iter().any(|d| d.severity == Severity::Error)`.

Also fix while in the area:
- Update test file line 10 doc comment: "undef in recursive sub args" → "undef in guard-referenced recursive sub args"
- Update test comment at line 428 to say "Must NOT have the specific guard-references-no-param cascading error" instead of claiming exactly one error

tasks.title: Refactor verify_uniqueness early-return tests: extract helper, fix warn-message wording, restore multi-missing verdict coverage
tasks.description:
Three interrelated follow-up suggestions from reviewer_comprehensive on Task 1283's diff. All three touch the same test block in `crates/reify-constraints/src/solver.rs` (roughly lines 1160-1350, the `verify_uniqueness_*` integration tests) and should be done in one pass to avoid merge churn and pick a single coherent policy.

(S1 — extract early-return test helper) `verify_uniqueness_skips_solve_core_when_param_missing` (~line 1216) and `verify_uniqueness_skips_solve_core_when_param_non_numeric` (~line 1290) are ~95% identical: same `ResolutionProblem` setup (single length param with bounds (0.0, 1.0), no constraints, no objective), same `CountingSubscriberBuilder` wiring counting WARN+DEBUG at target_prefix "reify_constraints", same `warn==1 / debug==0 / !unique` contract. Only the `solved_values` HashMap contents differ (empty vs `Value::Undef`) plus a few assertion-message strings. Extract a private helper inside the test module along the lines of:

```rust
fn assert_verify_uniqueness_early_returns(
    problem: &ResolutionProblem,
    solved_values: &HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>,
    branch_label: &str,
)
```

that builds the counting subscriber, runs `verify_uniqueness`, and asserts `!unique && warn==1 && debug==0` with messages templated on `branch_label`. Each call site then becomes ~10 lines of setup + one helper call, and adding a third early-return branch (e.g. NaN) is a one-liner.

(S2 — assertion-message wording) In the new non-numeric test's WARN assertion (~line 1337), the message currently reads `"expected exactly 1 WARN (the aggregated non-numeric-param early-return warn)"`. The actual warn string emitted by `solver.rs:826` is `"{N} solved value(s) missing or non-numeric"` — a single aggregated warn covering both the missing and non-numeric branches, not a distinct non-numeric warn. Change the message to `"the aggregated missing-or-non-numeric early-return warn"` (matching how the sibling missing-param test should also be worded if it diverges). Ideally the helper from S1 produces a single canonical wording so this cannot drift again.

(S3 — restore verdict coverage for the multi-missing aggregation path) Task 1283's S2 intentionally dropped `assert!(!unique, ...)` from `verify_uniqueness_aggregates_warn_for_multiple_missing_params` (~line 1164) on the grounds that a single-param early-return skip test already proves the verdict contract. But the multi-missing aggregation path now has no verdict assertion at all — a future refactor that changes how multi-missing is aggregated could silently ship a wrong verdict as long as exactly one WARN still fires. Pick one of:
  (a) Preferred: add a dedicated `verify_uniqueness_skips_solve_core_when_multiple_params_missing` test, modeled on `_when_param_missing`, that exercises the two-missing-param case with the CountingSubscriberBuilder and asserts `!unique && warn==1 && debug==0`. This keeps `aggregates_warn_for_multiple_missing_params` focused on warn-message text and the new test focused on control-flow contract. Once the S1 helper lands this is ~15 lines of setup + one helper call.
  (b) Alternative: restore the one-line `assert!(!unique, ...)` in the existing aggregation test. Cheaper but undoes Task 1283 S2's "verdict is out-of-contract here" framing — NOT recommended.

tasks.title: Replace wildcard catch-all with explicit match arms in compile_guarded_members
tasks.description:
In crates/reify-compiler/src/guards.rs (line 421-424), the `_ => {}` catch-all silently drops 6 MemberDecl variants (Port, Connect, Chain, AssociatedType, MetaBlock, ConstraintInst) with no diagnostic. A new variant added to MemberDecl in the future would also be silently swallowed without a compiler warning.

Replace the `_ => {}` wildcard with explicit empty arms: `MemberDecl::Port(_) | MemberDecl::Connect(_) | MemberDecl::Chain(_) | MemberDecl::AssociatedType(_) | MemberDecl::MetaBlock(_) | MemberDecl::ConstraintInst(_) => {}`. This ensures adding a new MemberDecl variant produces a non-exhaustive match compiler error, forcing a conscious decision.

The MemberDecl enum is defined in crates/reify-syntax/src/lib.rs (lines 77-91) with 13 variants total. Param, Let, Constraint, and GuardedGroup are handled by earlier arms; Sub, Minimize, and Maximize emit diagnostics (added in task 490).

tasks.title: Tighten run_tests_propagates_violation_diagnostics assertions (Task 1276 follow-up)
tasks.description:
Three test-quality suggestions from Task 1276 review (crates/reify-eval/tests/test_runner.rs:148-170). The test was strengthened in commit e80c9f094 but the new assertions can be made less brittle and more aligned with the sibling pattern used in `violated_constraint_def_produces_labeled_diagnostic` (constraint_def_eval.rs:85).

S1 (line 164, test_brittleness): The `d.message.contains("violated")` clause couples the test to a specific word in SimpleConstraintChecker's output ("constraint {id} violated"). If the wording is ever localized or reworded (e.g. "not satisfied", "failed"), this assertion breaks without a real regression. Drop the "violated" substring clause and rely on `severity == Severity::Error` + label-substring as the meaningful signal.

S2 (line 155, redundant_assertion): The `e.id.entity == "TestA"` filter adds no signal — the test source has only TestA, so every constraint result is by construction from TestA. The sibling test `run_tests_ignores_nontest_template_constraint_failures` (test_runner.rs:108) already covers entity isolation. Simplify to `results[0].constraint_results.iter().any(|e| e.satisfaction == Satisfaction::Violated)`.

S3 (line 165, test_logic): The `contains("Positive") || contains("TestA")` OR branch is dead — the label is enriched to "Positive[0]" by `labeled_diagnostics` (crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs:2261), so the "TestA" branch will never fire today. Tighten to `d.message.contains("Positive")` (or better, match `"Positive[0]"` to align with the sibling pattern at constraint_def_eval.rs:85 which uses `"MinWall[0]"`).

Net effect: the assertions become:
  1. `constraint_results.any(|e| e.satisfaction == Violated)`
  2. `diagnostics.any(|d| d.severity == Error && d.message.contains("Positive"))` (or "Positive[0]")

tasks.title: Dedupe find_bare_*_violations helpers and close raw-string gap
tasks.description:
Review suggestions S1+S6 from task 1039 review. In tree-sitter-reify/tests/build_logic_tests.rs, `find_bare_self_path_violations` (lines 897-908) is an almost-verbatim copy of `find_bare_build_rs_violations` (lines 867-877); the only difference is the needle strings, and the doc-comments explicitly acknowledge the parallel. Any future change (multi-line awareness, comment-stripping refinement, raw-string handling) must be made in two places.

Additionally, both helpers currently miss raw-string literals — `r"tests/build_logic_tests.rs"` or `r#"tests/build_logic_tests.rs"#` would compile, perform a bare relative read, and escape the guard. `test_find_bare_self_path_violations` has no coverage for the raw-string case.

Required changes:
1. Extract a shared helper `fn find_bare_path_violations<'a>(source: &'a str, needles: &[&str]) -> Vec<(usize, &'a str)>`; rewrite the two existing functions as thin wrappers that pass their needle arrays. Keep the public call sites unchanged.
2. Either (a) extend the helper to also match `r"..."` / `r#"..."#` raw-string forms for each needle, OR (b) add an explicit negative test in `test_find_bare_self_path_violations` that documents the known raw-string gap so future contributors know it is out of scope. Prefer (a) if the unit-test cost is small.
3. Verify: `cargo test -p tree-sitter-reify test_find_bare_self_path_violations test_find_bare_build_rs_violations test_self_read_paths_use_manifest_dir`.

tasks.title: Polish pub+modifier accept/reject coverage and labels in test_sync_comments_grep.sh
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1279 review (esc-1279-4, S1 + S2 + S4). Three related test-quality improvements to tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh, all touching the pub+modifier assertion block near lines 92-102.

**S1 — Disambiguate duplicate label (line 101-102):**
Task 1279 added an assertion for `pub(super) const fn sanitize_value(` with the label "(pub+const combination)", which is identical to the existing `pub(crate) const fn` label at line 92. Two distinct assertions with the same parenthetical label make it harder to distinguish failures in CI output.

Fix: Rename the line 101 label to "(pub(super)+const combination)" to differentiate from the existing "pub(crate)+const" assertion at line 92.

**S2 — Mirror new accept cases with _raw reject cases (after line 102):**
The new accept cases at lines 98-102 exercise `pub(crate) async fn` and `pub(super) const fn`, but there is no matching reject-case coverage for the same prefixes with a suffixed identifier. The existing reject cases only cover bare `fn sanitize_value_raw(`. A bug in the modifier chain could regress prefix+suffix interactions without this being caught.

Add reject-case mirrors near the existing reject block (~line 106+):
- `rejects: pub(crate) async fn sanitize_value_raw(`
- `rejects: pub(super) const fn sanitize_value_raw(`

**S4 — Optional: fill remaining symmetry gaps (after line 102):**
The underlying pattern supports `pub(in path::to::mod) fn ...` and several uncovered modifier combinations. This is explicitly flagged by the reviewer as "optional" and "a minor symmetry gap rather than a bug." Decide during implementation whether to include:
- `accepts: pub(in crate::foo) fn sanitize_value(`
- `accepts: pub(super) unsafe fn sanitize_value(`
- `accepts: unsafe async fn sanitize_value(`

tasks.title: Split CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR precision guard into its own test and drop review-thread comment

Bundling both in one test muddies failure messages — a future reader won't immediately know whether the constants' definition broke or a specific test function lacks `(THIS_FILE)`.

S5 (style, line 1546): The comment `// dot-slash prefix variant (reviewer S1: helper must also detect \"./tests/build_logic_tests.rs\")` bakes a pointer to a review suggestion ("reviewer S1") into committed source. Future readers without the review thread will find this reference meaningless.

tasks.title: Harden bash -c PATTERN interpolation in test_sync_comments_grep.sh
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1279 review (esc-1279-4, S3). Structural robustness improvement to tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh.

The new "extracted pattern contains expected fn name" assertion added by Task 1279 (lines 48-49) interpolates $PATTERN unquoted into a double-quoted `bash -c` argument via string concatenation:

```bash
assert "extracted pattern contains expected fn name" \
    bash -c "[[ \"$PATTERN\" == *sanitize_value* ]]"
```

Because PATTERN contains regex metacharacters (parens, backslashes, brackets, quotes), any future extraction bug that leaves an unbalanced quote would cause this assertion to fail in a confusing way — a shell parse error rather than a clean "pattern missing fn name" diagnostic.

The existing accept/reject assertions below (lines 53+) have the same structural risk (they all interpolate $PATTERN inside `bash -c "... grep -qE '$PATTERN' ..."`), so the current new assertion is merely consistent with the file's prevailing style. The reviewer flagged it as "a natural place to harden" the pattern.

**Options to consider (evaluate during implementation):**

1. **Narrow fix:** Replace just the line 48-49 assertion with a plain bash `[[ ]]` check that does not go through `bash -c`:
   ```bash
   if [[ "$PATTERN" != *sanitize_value* ]]; then
       echo "  FAIL: extracted pattern missing expected fn name" >&2
       exit 1
   fi
   ```
   (Or wire it into the `assert` helper without the second layer of quoting.)

2. **Broader fix:** Export PATTERN and have the `bash -c` subshells read it from the environment (`$PATTERN` inside the subshell's own context, no interpolation), eliminating the shell-injection risk for ALL existing accept/reject assertions in the file in a single pass.

Option 2 is the more principled fix but is a wider refactor; option 1 addresses only the new assertion. Implementer should choose based on scope preference.

Verify with: `bash tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh`

Source: esc-1279-4 (steward-triage from Task 1279 post-merge review)

tasks.title: Harden test_portable_timeout.sh Test 21b pre-cleanup and poll loop
tasks.description:
Review suggestions S3+S4 from task 1039 review, both in tests/infra/test_portable_timeout.sh.

S4 (robustness, lines 541-547): The pre-cleanup kills every host process whose args end in `sleep 31339`, regardless of owner. On a shared CI host or dev workstation this can race with another concurrent run of this same test (e.g., parallel worktrees running `cargo test`) — one run's pre-cleanup can kill the orphan the other run is about to assert on, producing cross-test flakes. It also silently masks the case where a real regression in Test 21a leaves an orphan behind.

S3 (performance, lines 558-563): The new poll loop runs `ps | grep` with no initial wait, so on the common path where the orphan is reaped in ~50-500ms the test now incurs a full 1s sleep before the second check (rather than the old 0.5s). After the 5th iteration fails the test still executes `sleep 1` that is never followed by another check — ~1s of pure waste when the test is about to fail anyway.

Required changes:
1. Scope the pre-cleanup scan to the current user: `ps -o pid,user,args` filtered by `$(id -un)`, and/or bump the sentinel to include `$$` (per-run uniqueness). Preferred: fix Test 21a at the source so Test 21b does not need defensive pre-cleanup. Document whichever path is taken in an inline comment.
2. Short-circuit the poll loop to avoid the trailing wasted sleep: `[ "$_try" -lt 5 ] && "$_abs_sleep" 1` (minimal change, preserves the ~5s upper bound the comment advertises). Optionally back off (0.2/0.4/0.8/1.6/2.0) if the author prefers faster common-path.
3. Verify: run `tests/infra/test_portable_timeout.sh` and ensure Tests 21a/21b pass; confirm no stray `sleep 31339` processes remain after completion.

tasks.title: Tighten completion_items test assertions and cover missing-items-field fallthrough

Under the current code this falls through to the outer `CompletionResponse::Array` panic, which is the right behavior but is not asserted. Add a `#[should_panic(expected = "CompletionResponse::Array")]` test to lock in the fallthrough contract.

tasks.title: Close remaining pub/unsafe symmetry gaps in test_sync_comments_grep.sh
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1320 review (esc-1320-8). Two small symmetry gaps in tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh, both explicitly flagged as "suggestion" severity by the comprehensive reviewer.

**S1 — Add accept case for alternate qualifier ordering (near line 110-111):**
The new accept case at line 110 covers `unsafe async fn sanitize_value(`, but the alternate common ordering `const unsafe fn sanitize_value(` is not explicitly exercised. The current regex structure places const and unsafe in separate optional groups, so it would match, but the ordering is not validated by a test. Adding this case makes the qualifier-ordering guarantee explicit.

Fix: After line 111, add:
```
assert "accepts: const unsafe fn sanitize_value( (const+unsafe combination)" \
    bash -c "printf '%s\n' 'const unsafe fn sanitize_value(v: Value) -> Value {' | grep -qE '$PATTERN'"
```

**S2 — Add reject mirror for pub(in path) suffix false-positive (near line 118-122):**

tasks.title: Polish whitespace labels and trap comment in test_test_helpers robustness section
tasks.description:
Two small polish items in the robustness section of tests/infra/test_test_helpers.sh (lines ~440-455) from Task 1240 review:

1. **Readable multi-space labels (line 451).** The `_ws_label` currently sets `(empty)` for the empty string and `(tab)` for `$'\t'`, but single-space (`' '`) and double-space (`'  '`) variants render as literal invisible whitespace in test output, making a failure for one of those cases ambiguous in logs. Apply a readable label for every variant, e.g.

```bash
case "$ws" in
    '')    _ws_label='(empty)' ;;
    ' ')   _ws_label='(1 space)' ;;
    '  ')  _ws_label='(2 spaces)' ;;
    $'\t') _ws_label='(tab)' ;;
    *)     _ws_label="(${#ws} chars)" ;;
esac
```

2. **Defensive comment on trap cleanup_robust EXIT (line 442).** Today the `trap cleanup_robust EXIT` is the only main-shell EXIT trap in this file — earlier EXIT traps are inside `bash -c` subshells so there is no conflict. A future edit that adds another main-shell EXIT trap before this line would silently overwrite the previous trap. Add a short comment above line 442 noting the invariant, e.g.

```bash
# NOTE: This is the only main-shell EXIT trap in this file. Any future
# main-shell EXIT trap added above would silently overwrite this one
# (and vice versa). Stack via `trap -p EXIT` if that ever changes.
```

Both items are 1–3 line polish edits in the same ~15-line region. Verify with `bash tests/infra/test_test_helpers.sh` — all existing tests (plus the whitespace loop's readable labels) should still pass.

tasks.title: Centralize parse_and_compile / make_engine test helpers in reify-test-support
tasks.description:
The `parse_and_compile(source: &str) -> reify_compiler::CompiledModule` and `make_engine() -> reify_eval::Engine` helpers are duplicated across 9+ test files in crates/reify-eval/tests: e2e_meta.rs, test_runner.rs, m5_integration.rs, m9_constraint_def.rs, m9_trait_conformance.rs, purpose_eval.rs, m10_geometric_types.rs, m11_field_calculus.rs, determinacy_predicates.rs. Each copy does: parse → assert no parse errors → compile → assert no error-severity diagnostics → return CompiledModule. The make_engine helper wraps `Engine::new(Box::new(MockConstraintChecker::new()), None)`.

There is existing precedent (completed task 2) that moved `compile_source`/`eval_source` helpers into reify-test-support. This follow-up would extend the same consolidation to these two helpers.

**Fix:**
1. Add `parse_and_compile` and `make_engine` as pub fns in reify-test-support (likely in a new `helpers.rs` or `pipeline.rs` module).
2. Consider also including `parse_compile_expect_err(source, needle)` — introduced in task 1043's e2e_meta.rs — since the error-diagnostic assertion pattern is also useful across files.
3. Replace the per-file copies in the 9 reify-eval test files with `use reify_test_support::...;`.
4. Audit for subtle behavior differences: e2e_meta.rs uses `.any()` + lazy format args (no allocation on success path); m5_integration.rs uses pre-collected `errors: Vec<_>`. Choose one as the canonical form. The `.any()` variant is marginally more efficient on success (no Vec alloc) but the pre-collect variant is simpler — pick whichever the team prefers and apply uniformly.

**Priority:** low. Reviewer (task 1043) flagged this as "low priority" because the current per-file pattern is an established convention; this is a maintainability / drift-prevention improvement.

**Source:** review-suggestion triage for task 1043 (esc-1043-12, suggestion 3: code_reuse_cross_file).

tasks.title: Harden portable_timeout post-wait cleanup: quote PGID, fix comment, address early-exit semantics
tasks.description:
Three consistency/correctness follow-ups to scripts/lib_portable.sh's post-wait orphan-cleanup block (lines 145-152), added in Task 1214 (S2).

**S1: Quote the PGID argument on line 152 for consistency with timer subshells.**
Lines 124 and 134 now use `kill -9 -- "-$cmd_pid" 2>/dev/null` (quoted, per Task 1214's S1 fix), but the new post-wait cleanup on line 152 uses `kill -9 -- -$cmd_pid 2>/dev/null || true` (unquoted). If defensive quoting is worth applying in the timer subshells, it should apply here too. Change line 152 to `kill -9 -- "-$cmd_pid" 2>/dev/null || true`. This will break Test 23 (currently asserts exactly 2 quoted occurrences) — update it to 3, or rewrite as an invariant (no unquoted `kill -9 -- -$cmd_pid` occurrences anywhere in the file).

**S2: Fix the comment justifying SIGKILL-over-SIGTERM on the post-wait cleanup (lines 149-150).**
The current comment says "if we reached this point the command already ignored SIGTERM, so re-sending SIGTERM here would be ineffective." That's only true for the timeout-escalation path — line 152 also runs when the command exited naturally before the timeout fired, in which case the command did NOT ignore SIGTERM and the premise is false. Rewrite to cover both paths, e.g. "Use SIGKILL rather than SIGTERM: in the timeout path, the command already ignored SIGTERM, so SIGTERM would be ineffective; in the early-exit path, any surviving process-group members are orphans with no opportunity for graceful shutdown anyway."

**S3: Address or document the early-exit behavioral change.**
Previously, orphans in a normally-exiting command's process group received SIGTERM (giving them a grace window); they now receive SIGKILL unconditionally. This is a silent behavioral change for commands that background children in the same PGID and exit successfully. No existing test exercises the early-exit orphan-cleanup path (Test 16 only checks the timer's inner `sleep 31337`, which is the timeout-escalation path). Two options:
  (a) Add a behavioral test that launches a command which forks a child in the same PGID, exits fast, and verifies the child is reaped post-call.
  (b) Document the semantics change in the portable_timeout doc comment so callers know orphaned PGID members no longer get a SIGTERM grace window on early exit.

All three suggestions come from reviewer_comprehensive's post-merge review of Task 1214. They're small and closely related — one task covers them efficiently.

Memory hints: search "portable_timeout POSIX fallback SIGKILL orphan cleanup" for context on the design; Task 1214 established the baseline.

tasks.title: Strengthen test_portable_timeout Task 1214 tests: behavioral grace-period check, invariant-based regex
tasks.description:
Two test-quality follow-ups to tests/infra/test_portable_timeout.sh tests added in Task 1214 (Tests 22, 23, 24).

**S4 (test_coverage): Tests 22/23/24 are purely structural.**
All four new assertions grep lib_portable.sh for specific strings and line-count invariants — they verify the source code *looks* a certain way but do not verify any runtime behavior. Test 24 in particular could pass even if `_pt_kill_grace` were assigned a non-integer or hoisted outside the POSIX-fallback branch (declared on code paths that never use it). Add at least one behavioral assertion — either:
  (a) override `_pt_kill_grace` to a distinct value (e.g. 4), trigger the escalation scenario (command ignores SIGTERM), and measure the gap between timer-fire and SIGKILL; OR
  (b) use `declare -f portable_timeout` to confirm `_pt_kill_grace` is declared inside the POSIX fallback branch and nowhere else in the function body.

**S5 (test_robustness): Test 22's second assertion is too narrow.**
tests/infra/test_portable_timeout.sh:582-583 currently greps for the exact string `kill -- -$cmd_pid 2>/dev/null || true`. A regression that reintroduces SIGTERM in a slightly different form (without `|| true`, different redirection, or the quoted form `kill -- "-$cmd_pid"`) would bypass this guard. The comment even advertises the fragility ("The '|| true' suffix is unique to this line") as intentional — but this makes the assertion a bug-specific test rather than an invariant test.

Replace the fixed-string check with a regex that rules out *any* `kill` of `$cmd_pid`'s process group without `-9`/`-KILL`, for example:
  `! grep -qE '(^|[^-9])kill[[:space:]]+--[[:space:]]+"?-\$cmd_pid' "$LIB_PORTABLE"`
so any future variant of plain SIGTERM to that PGID is caught.

Both suggestions come from reviewer_comprehensive's post-merge review of Task 1214. If the sibling task updates line 152 to use quoted PGID form, Test 23's count invariant may also need updating — coordinate test changes.

Memory hints: search "test_portable_timeout structural assertions" for context; tests 22-24 were added in Task 1214 commits d9de6afb0, 62196f293, and the S2 commit.

tasks.title: Strengthen Section 1 regression guards in test_sync_comments_grep.sh
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1322 review (esc-1322-29, suggestions S1/S2/S3/S6). Improve the hardening self-checks added in Task 1322 to tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh so they are more robust and self-documenting.

Four related improvements in Section 1 (lines ~46-59):

1. **S1 (readability, line 53):** The `_CHECK='bash'; _CHECK+=' -c ".*\$PATTERN'` split-concatenation trick prevents the self-check regex from matching its own definition line. This is opaque on first read. Add an inline comment directly above line 53 explaining *why* it is split — something like `# Split assignment so the literal 'bash -c ".*$PATTERN' does not appear contiguously in this file and trip its own self-check below.`

2. **S2 (test_coverage, lines 53-54):** `_no_unhardened_pattern_interp` matches only the exact form `bash -c "...$PATTERN...`. It does NOT catch the equivalent `${PATTERN}` form (`\$PATTERN` in the regex requires a literal `$` directly followed by `P`, not `$` followed by `{`), nor variations like `bash -c "...\\"$PATTERN\\"..."`. Since this IS the regression trap for the whole refactor, its recall matters. Broaden the regex to at least accept an optional `{` after `$` (e.g. `bash -c ".*\$\\{?PATTERN`). A stronger fix would be to reject any `bash -c "` line that mentions PATTERN at all in Section 1.

3. **S3 (test_quality, lines 56-57):** The `exports PATTERN` assertion is a purely textual grep for `^[[:space:]]*export[[:space:]]+PATTERN`. An `export PATTERN` inside an `if false; then ... fi` block would pass the textual check while leaving PATTERN unexported. Add (or replace with) a behavioral check: `assert "PATTERN is actually exported" bash -c '[ -n "${PATTERN+x}" ] && env | grep -q "^PATTERN="'`. This validates the invariant the textual check is trying to enforce.

4. **S6 (diagnostics, line 54):** When `_no_unhardened_pattern_interp` fails, grep's matched lines are printed but the user may not immediately understand they are the cause of the failing assert. Add a louder header, e.g.:
```bash
_no_unhardened_pattern_interp() {
    local m
    m=$(grep -nE "$_CHECK" "$THIS_SCRIPT") || return 0
    echo "UNHARDENED PATTERN INTERPOLATION FOUND:" >&2
    echo "$m" >&2
    return 1
}
```

Verify with: `bash tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh` — all assertions should still pass (≥40), and the braced-form negative case should also be caught if you add one.

Source: esc-1322-29 (steward-triage from Task 1322 post-merge review)

tasks.title: Extend bash -c env-passing hardening to Sections 2 & 3 of test_sync_comments_grep.sh
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1322 review (esc-1322-29, suggestions S4/S5). Task 1322 hardened only Section 1 of tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh. Sections 2 (lines 167, 170) and Section 3 (lines 182-220) still use the fragile `bash -c "..."` form with parent-shell interpolation of `$SYNC_TEST`, `$_SECT3_HELPER`, and heredoc-style embedded scripts that must escape every `\\$`.

While these interpolated values are trusted *paths* (not extracted regexes) and so are not currently broken, the file now has two conventions side-by-side, which undermines the "hardened-everywhere" story and makes it easy to regress Section 1 by copying from Sections 2/3.

**Work:**

1. **S5 (consistency):** Apply the same env-passing pattern used in Section 1 to Sections 2 and 3. Export `SYNC_TEST` and `_SECT3_HELPER` (or equivalent scoped names) once, and switch the outer quotes of each `bash -c` to single-quotes so the child shell reads the variables from its own environment instead of parent-shell interpolation. For Section 3, the heredoc-style embedded scripts should likewise stop using `\\$`-escapes by running inside a single-quoted outer.

2. **S4 (scoping):** While touching these sections, consider the naming/scope of `PATTERN`. `export PATTERN` promotes a very generic name to the environment for the remainder of the script, including Section 3 subshells that `source sync_comments_test.sh`. If that file (now or in future) references a local `PATTERN` variable, the exported one will shadow it. Either (a) rename to `FN_EXISTENCE_PATTERN` and update Section 1 assertions accordingly, or (b) `unset PATTERN` at the end of Section 1 (before the Section 2 header) so it does not leak into sourced scripts downstream. Option (a) is cleaner; option (b) is a narrower cleanup.

Verify with: `bash tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh` (all existing assertions must still pass) and `bash tests/infra/run_all.sh` (broader infra suite).

tasks.title: Restore error_prefix constants as source of truth for parse_params labels
tasks.description:
In bridge.rs (Task 983's refactor), `parse_params` call sites now use hardcoded string labels ("initialize", "didOpen", etc.) while `error_prefix::*` constants duplicate the full prefixes as parallel string literals. The original invariant — "editing a constant changes the runtime error" — has been inverted into "a test keeps two parallel strings in sync". The doc comment on `parse_params` (lines 16-22) also still claims the label format is "the canonical prefix asserted by integration tests via error_prefix constants", which is misleading after the refactor.

Fix: thread `error_prefix::*` constants through as labels by (a) making each constant the exact runtime prefix (e.g. `INITIALIZE_PARAMS = "initialize params error"`) and changing `parse_params` to format as `format!("{label}: {e}")`, or (b) adopting an equivalent approach that restores the constants as the literal source of truth. Update call sites to `parse_params::<InitializeParams>(params, error_prefix::INITIALIZE_PARAMS)`. Update the doc comment on `parse_params` to accurately describe the relationship.

Verify by running the existing `error_prefix_constants_match_actual_errors` integration test in `crates/reify-lsp/tests/in_process_bridge.rs`, which should continue to pass once constants and runtime strings are unified.

tasks.title: Consolidate or remove redundant parse_params unit tests in bridge.rs
tasks.description:
The three new unit tests added in Task 983 at crates/reify-lsp/src/bridge.rs:237-262 duplicate existing coverage and have inconsistent match strictness:

- `parse_params_returns_ok_for_valid_input` is implicitly exercised by every happy-path integration test in `tests/in_process_bridge.rs`.
- `parse_params_returns_err_with_label_prefix_on_invalid_input` and `parse_params_substitutes_provided_label` both feed `json!(42)` and check for a label-derived prefix — redundant with each other and with `error_prefix_constants_match_actual_errors`, which already pins the prefix format for every method arm.
- One asserts `starts_with("initialize params error: ")` while the other uses `contains("didOpen params error")` — inconsistent strictness for the same property.

Fix: either delete the three unit tests (the integration tests already cover observable behavior) or consolidate into a single table-driven test iterating `[("initialize", InitializeParams), ("didOpen", DidOpenTextDocumentParams), ...]` with consistent `starts_with` assertions. Prefer deletion unless helper coverage must be isolated from dispatcher coverage.

tasks.title: Centralize serde in [workspace.dependencies] in root Cargo.toml
tasks.description:
`serde` is currently declared as a direct dependency in three crates (reify-lsp, reify-mcp, gui/src-tauri), each re-declaring its version independently. Task 983 added `serde = "1"` to `crates/reify-lsp/Cargo.toml:16` without features, matching the existing pattern but missing an opportunity to centralize.

Fix: add `serde = { version = "1", default-features = true }` to `[workspace.dependencies]` in the root `Cargo.toml`, then update all three crates to use `serde.workspace = true` (with `features = ["derive"]` where the derive macro is needed). This centralizes version management across the workspace.

Verify with `cargo check --workspace` and the standard verify pipeline (fmt/clippy/test).

tasks.title: Consolidate redundant structured_field_emission tests in concurrent_eval
tasks.description: Deduplicate and harden the per-site structured_field_emission tests added in Task 600, which now overlap with the migrated poison_recovery / poison_recovery_extended / poison_shared_fallback / poison_evaluate suites that already call assert_poison_recovers(..., expected_fields).
tasks.details:

The per-site module was the TDD-failing scaffold from commit 1faade68a/a471aac41; its role as a contract guard is now redundant.

Actions:

1. Delete the entire `structured_field_emission` module (crates/reify-runtime/tests/concurrent_eval.rs:2386-2672). Rationale: the migrated poison_* suites already cover every site and also assert on data content, so they subsume the per-site checks and cost less to maintain.

In particular: add at least one assertion per family (helper recovery, into_result inline recovery, shared_fallback inline recovery) that `capture.fields_by_event().iter().any(|m| m.contains_key("error"))` so the `error = %e` field cannot silently disappear. Reviewer suggestion #1.

Centralize the fixed message check in `assert_poison_recovers` (single `message == "lock poisoned, recovering"` check) or add `capture.assert_any_message_equals(&str)` in reify-test-support so the exact-equality check lives in one place instead of 11 per-site sites. This addresses reviewer suggestion #6 (brittleness from repeated string literals).

4. After deletion, verify the remaining tests still compile and pass:

5. Ensure the field contract (lock/access/path/error/message) is still enforced end-to-end by the migrated suites. If any site loses coverage, add it back inside the relevant existing module (poison_recovery / poison_shared_fallback / poison_evaluate) instead of recreating structured_field_emission.

tasks.title: Introduce compile-time constants for ConcurrentEvalAdapter poison-field schema
tasks.description: Replace free-form string literals in the 11 tracing::warn! sites in crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent_eval.rs with `const &str` values so typos like `access = "exlusive"` become compile errors.
tasks.details:
Background: Task 600 established a structured-field schema for lock poisoning recovery warnings (see the module doc comment at crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent_eval.rs:30-36). The schema is currently documented only in the doc comment; each of the 11 warn sites and the corresponding test assertions hand-repeat the string literals `"values"`, `"snapshot_values"`, `"results"`, `"read"`, `"write"`, `"exclusive"`, `"into_inner"`, `"shared_fallback"`. A typo in any of these compiles and silently breaks the field contract (Datadog/Jaeger filters would drop the warning). Reviewer suggestion #7.

Actions:

1. Introduce a small private module inside concurrent_eval.rs (e.g. `mod poison_fields { ... }`) defining:
   - `pub(super) const LOCK_VALUES: &str = "values";`
   - `pub(super) const LOCK_SNAPSHOT_VALUES: &str = "snapshot_values";`
   - `pub(super) const LOCK_RESULTS: &str = "results";`
   - `pub(super) const ACCESS_READ: &str = "read";`
   - `pub(super) const ACCESS_WRITE: &str = "write";`
   - `pub(super) const ACCESS_EXCLUSIVE: &str = "exclusive";`
   - `pub(super) const PATH_INTO_INNER: &str = "into_inner";`
   - `pub(super) const PATH_SHARED_FALLBACK: &str = "shared_fallback";`
   - `pub(super) const MSG_LOCK_POISONED: &str = "lock poisoned, recovering";`

2. Update all 11 `tracing::warn!` call sites in concurrent_eval.rs (5 helper methods in the lines 108-150 range + 6 into_result paths in the lines 225-263 range) to use the constants:
   ```rust
   tracing::warn!(lock = poison_fields::LOCK_VALUES, access = poison_fields::ACCESS_READ, error = %e, "{}", poison_fields::MSG_LOCK_POISONED);
   ```
   Note: tracing::warn! accepts expressions as field values, so `lock = poison_fields::LOCK_VALUES` is fine.

3. Export the constants with `pub(crate)` visibility (or via a `#[cfg(feature = "test-utils")]` re-export) so the test file can reference them in assertions — so the tests stop repeating literals too.

4. Update the migrated tests in crates/reify-runtime/tests/concurrent_eval.rs to call `assert_poison_recovers(..., &[("lock", poison_fields::LOCK_VALUES), ("access", poison_fields::ACCESS_READ)])` instead of string literals. This converts any future rename into a compile error.

5. Update the module doc comment at crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent_eval.rs:30-36 to point at the `poison_fields` module as the source of truth instead of enumerating values inline.

6. Verify:
   - cd /home/leo/src/reify && cargo test -p reify-runtime --test concurrent_eval --features test-utils
   - cd /home/leo/src/reify && cargo clippy -p reify-runtime -- -D warnings

tasks.title: Document or harden MessageVisitor::record_debug field capture in reify-test-support
tasks.description: The WarnCapture field-matching API in crates/reify-test-support/src/tracing_support.rs stores non-message fields via `format!("{value:?}")`, which is reliable only for `&'static str` literals and for `%Display` fields. For `?Debug` fields on types like PoisonError, the stored form includes Debug decoration and silent breakage is possible.
tasks.details:
Background: Task 600 extended WarnCapture in crates/reify-test-support/src/tracing_support.rs:480-491 to capture structured fields for testing. `MessageVisitor::record_debug` writes non-message fields as `format!("{value:?}")`. This happens to work for the current callers (which use `&'static str` literals for lock/access/path — intercepted by `record_str` — and `error = %e` — where Display is wrapped such that Debug delegates to Display). But `assert_any_event_has_fields` doing value matching against this output is a non-obvious invariant: a future caller using `error = ?e` for a PoisonError would get `"PoisonError { .. }"` stored verbatim, and assertions would silently rely on the Debug format being stable. Reviewer suggestion #5.

Actions (pick one — prefer option A):

A) **Document the invariant on the public API**: Add a rustdoc warning to `WarnCapture::assert_any_event_has_fields` (and any other field-value assertion helpers) explicitly stating that value matching is reliable only for `&str`-typed fields and for `%Display` fields where Debug delegates to Display. Point callers at option B for Debug/Display fields. Minimal change.

B) **Add `assert_any_event_field_contains(key, substring)`**: A separate helper that does substring matching against the stored (possibly Debug-decorated) value. Useful for `?e`-style fields. Implement alongside option A.

C) **Normalize record_debug output**: Strip surrounding Debug quotes for single-string Debug outputs so `%e` values and `?literal` look alike. Higher-risk — may break existing callers. Do NOT take this option without a broader survey.

Preferred: A + B. Update the existing `assert_any_event_has_fields` doc to explain the contract, and add `assert_any_event_field_contains` for Debug/Display use.

Verify:
- cd /home/leo/src/reify && cargo test -p reify-test-support
- cd /home/leo/src/reify && cargo test -p reify-runtime --test concurrent_eval --features test-utils

tasks.description: Reduce the two near-identical 11-line Err-arm scaffolding blocks in the downstream_ops_after_malformed_initialize_without_initialized test (crates/reify-lsp/tests/in_process_bridge.rs lines 944-954 and 976-989). tasks.details:
Options (reviewer-suggested):
(a) Remove the Err arms entirely and replace with `.expect("didOpen should succeed pre-handshake (bridge has no init-state guard)")`, asserting on the Value directly. This is the simpler approach — if a future change adds a guard, whoever does so can add back the Err handling then.
(b) Extract a single helper `assert_ok_null_or_nonempty_err(label, result)` at the top of the test module and call it from both sites, eliminating the duplicated 11-line match+comment pattern.

Weigh (a) vs (b): (a) trades scaffolding-intent for simpler code; (b) preserves scaffolding-intent while removing duplication. Source: esc-1338-39 suggestion from reviewer_comprehensive (test_quality_dead_code). Task 1338 explicitly chose the "documented scaffolding" path originally, so this follow-up is a re-balancing rather than a bug fix. Low priority.

Other pre-handshake tests in the same file (e.g., the malformed-params tests that exercise a fresh `InProcessLsp` before handshake) share the same hang exposure but have no guard, making the current guard read as ad-hoc.
tasks.details:
Options (reviewer-suggested):
(a) Factor a `with_hang_guard<F>(seconds: u64, f: F)` helper at the top of the test module and apply it uniformly to all pre-handshake tests in the file.
(b) Add a one-line comment on the downstream_ops test documenting why it is uniquely at hang risk (e.g., "exercises compile path where a real stall was historically observed") and leave siblings ungated.

Prefer (a) unless investigation shows the downstream_ops test is genuinely uniquely hang-prone. Source: esc-1338-39 suggestion from reviewer_comprehensive (test_robustness). Note: reify-lsp Cargo.toml already includes `tokio = { workspace = true, features = ["time"] }` in dev-dependencies, so adding more timeouts requires no dependency changes. Low priority.

tasks.title: Clean up sync_ref_helpers.sh: unreachable display_fn and sourceable error exit
tasks.description: Two small hygiene fixes in tests/infra/sync_ref_helpers.sh from Task 1260 review (S1 + S5). Both are non-regressions carried over from the pre-move version, but are appropriate to address now that the file has its own home and is explicitly intended to be sourced from multiple places.
tasks.details:
Follow-up to Task 1260. Two review suggestions for tests/infra/sync_ref_helpers.sh.

**S1 — Unreachable display_fn fallback (sync_ref_helpers.sh:32)**
`local display_fn="${ref_fn:-<none>}"` is dead in its fallback form. The early-fail guard on line 31 returns when ref_fn is empty, so ${ref_fn:-<none>} always evaluates to $ref_fn and the `<none>` branch can never fire. Carried over verbatim from sync_comments_test.sh, not a regression.
Fix: either drop display_fn entirely and use ${ref_fn} in the assert description, or drop the early-fail guard and let display_fn legitimately fall back to `<none>`. Keeping both is contradictory. Recommended: drop display_fn (the early-fail guard is the valuable part).

**S5 — exit 1 in sourceable helper can kill caller (sync_ref_helpers.sh:20)**
`[ -f ... ] || { echo ERROR ...; exit 1; }` inside a file deliberately intended to be sourced from multiple places will terminate the caller's shell on failure. Not a regression (matches pattern in other consumers) but especially worth hardening here because this helper's whole purpose is sourcing.
Fix: `return 1 2>/dev/null || exit 1` — unwinds cleanly when sourced, still errors hard when (improbably) executed directly. Mirrors the `return 0 2>/dev/null || true` idiom already used in the source guard a few lines above.

**Note on structural meta-test coupling**
test_test_helpers.sh:396-400 includes a structural check "sync_ref_helpers.sh uses display_fn fallback variable". When S1 removes display_fn, that meta-test must be deleted (not updated to some other indicator).

tasks.title: POSIX-portability hygiene in tests/infra meta-tests: drop \s, extend sync_comments_test.sh coverage
tasks.description: Follow-up to Task 1260. Two portability-hygiene fixes in tests/infra meta-tests (S2 + S6). Both restore the POSIX-portable-regex spirit of the sync_comments grep subsystem that Task 1260's refactor slightly eroded.
tasks.details:
Follow-up to Task 1260. Two review suggestions targeting POSIX-portable regex hygiene in the tests/infra subsystem.

**S6 — `\s+` used in tests/infra/test_test_helpers.sh:308,368**
Both lines use `grep -qE '(source|\s+).*helper_file'` patterns. `\s` is a GNU/PCRE extension; BSD grep (macOS default, some CI runners) does not support it in ERE. The project already uses this pattern elsewhere in the same file so not newly broken, but the whole point of test_sync_comments_grep.sh / sync_ref_helpers.sh is to enforce POSIX-portable regex hygiene, so shipping `\s` in the sibling meta-test is inconsistent with the subsystem's own rules.
Fix: replace `\s+` with `[[:space:]]+` at test_test_helpers.sh:308 and :368. Check the rest of test_test_helpers.sh for other `\s` uses and convert them as well for consistency.

**S2 — sync_comments_test.sh no longer covered by POSIX-portability assertions (test_sync_comments_grep.sh:146-161)**
After Task 1260 moved assert_sync_ref_exists out, Section 2 of test_sync_comments_grep.sh rewrote its "no \b / no grep -P" scoped assertions to target $SYNC_REF_HELPERS instead of $SYNC_TEST. But sync_comments_test.sh still hosts extract_fn, which contains `/^[^/]*fn '"$fn_name"'[(<]/` and an awk pattern — exactly the kind of thing those scoped assertions were designed to guard. A future regression adding `\b` or `grep -P` in or near extract_fn would now slip past CI.
Fix: duplicate the no-\b and no-grep-P scoped assertions at lines 157-161 so each runs twice — once against $SYNC_REF_HELPERS and once against $SYNC_TEST. Keep the existing `[[:space:](<]` positive assertion scoped only to $SYNC_REF_HELPERS (since that pattern literally lives there now). The invariant "POSIX-portable regex only" belongs on any shell file in this subsystem that contains grep/awk patterns, not just on the one that houses assert_sync_ref_exists.

tasks.title: Extend assert_sync_ref_exists behavioral coverage; tighten or drop rc check
tasks.description: Follow-up to Task 1260. Review flagged that the file-move refactor reduced behavioral coverage of assert_sync_ref_exists (only the empty-ref_fn guard path is tested) and that the rc -eq 0 subshell check is redundant/misleading. S3 + S4 from esc-1260-38.
tasks.details:
Follow-up to Task 1260. Two review suggestions for tests/infra/test_test_helpers.sh in the assert_sync_ref_exists behavioral test region (lines 412-433).

**S3 — rc -eq 0 subshell check is redundant/misleading (test_test_helpers.sh:412-433)**
The test asserts `_src_beh_rc -eq 0` on the subshell that runs assert_sync_ref_exists. But assert() in test_helpers.sh is guaranteed to return 0 — both PASS and FAIL branches end with an arithmetic expansion that yields 0 — so this rc check cannot fail for any reason related to assert_sync_ref_exists. Combined with the FAIL-grep on the same output, it advertises robustness it does not provide.
Fix options:
  (a) Drop the rc check entirely and rely on the anchored `^  FAIL:` grep.
  (b) Run the subshell under `bash -eu` so a genuine runtime error (unset var, missing command) in assert_sync_ref_exists would change rc and be caught.
  (c) Capture rc from assert_sync_ref_exists directly (not from the subshell as a whole).
Recommended: (b) — gives the rc check actual meaning by making it detect unset-var or missing-command regressions in the helper.

**S4 — Behavioral coverage reduction after file-move refactor (test_test_helpers.sh:412-421)**
The refactor removed the sed-extraction robustness test (good — the extraction is gone) but did not add back behavioral tests for the happy path or the mismatch path. Net result: only the empty-ref_fn guard is covered behaviorally. The happy-path case in particular protects against future regression of the fn-existence regex itself.
Fix: add two behavioral subshells alongside the existing guard test:
  1. SYNC marker + matching `pub fn <name>` in target → expect `^  PASS:` anchored.
  2. SYNC marker + non-matching target fn → expect `^  FAIL:` anchored on the fn-existence assertion (not on the guard line). Verify by grepping for the fn name in the FAIL message or by checking it is not the guard wording.
Apply S3's rc-hardening (bash -eu) uniformly to all three behavioral subshells.

**Suggested order:** Implement S4 (add happy + mismatch subshells) first, then apply S3 uniformly (bash -eu across all three) so the tightened rc check gates the new tests from day one.

tasks.title: e2e_meta.rs polish: cardinality guards, drop vestigial binding, extract find_template helper
tasks.description:
Follow-up polish for `crates/reify-eval/tests/e2e_meta.rs` from review of task 1084. Four small, related improvements — do them together to minimize churn on the file.

1. **Add cardinality guard to `e2e_meta_access_on_occurrence`** (~lines 192-205). The `compiled.templates.iter().find(|t| t.name == "Welding")` lookup is robust to extra templates being emitted, but the previous code's implicit `templates[0]` check caught duplicate emissions. Add an explicit `assert_eq!(compiled.templates.len(), 1, "expected exactly one template for single occurrence def")` before the find to restore that coverage. Source: reviewer_comprehensive suggestion on esc-1084-44.

2. **Add matching cardinality guard to `e2e_meta_access_let_binding`** (~lines 88-101). Same pattern as #1 but for the Widget Structure sanity check. `assert_eq!(compiled.templates.len(), 1)` before the `find(|t| t.name == "Widget")`. Source: same review.

3. **Drop vestigial `_part` binding in `e2e_meta_sub_structure_child_meta`** (~lines 248-252). The `let _part = compiled.templates.iter().find(...).expect(...)` binds a value that is never read; the sole purpose is the `.expect()` side effect. Drop the binding entirely: `compiled.templates.iter().find(|t| t.name == "Part").expect("Part template should be present in compiled output");`. Source: same review (code_style).

4. **Extract `find_template` helper**. The `compiled.templates.iter().find(|t| t.name == X).expect(Y)` pattern is repeated four times in the file (Widget, Welding, Part, Assembly). Add a helper alongside the existing `parse_and_compile`, `make_engine`, `parse_compile_expect_err` helpers:

   ```rust
   /// Look up a compiled template by name, panicking with a helpful message if absent.
   fn find_template<'a>(
       compiled: &'a reify_compiler::CompiledModule,
       name: &str,
   ) -> &'a reify_compiler::CompiledTemplate {
       compiled
           .templates
           .iter()
           .find(|t| t.name == name)
           .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("{} template should be present in compiled output", name))
   }
   ```

   Use it in all four call sites. Source: same review (code_reuse).

Verification: run `cargo test -p reify-eval --test e2e_meta` from the worktree root after the changes; all meta tests should continue to pass.

Note: the cross-crate duplication concern on sub-component wiring assertions (S3 in the review) was intentionally NOT included — the reviewer explicitly said "no change required if the intent is e2e-boundary early failure", and that is the intent.

tasks.title: Strengthen error assertions in self_inside_lambda_in_fn_body_errors test
tasks.description:
The `self_inside_lambda_in_fn_body_errors` test in `self_keyword_tests.rs` has two assertion quality issues:

1. **Tautological else branch (lines 636-640):** The else branch is entered only when `parsed.errors.is_empty()` is false, so `assert!(!parsed.errors.is_empty(), ...)` can never fail — it's dead validation code. Replace with an assertion on error content, e.g. check that at least one error message mentions `self`.

2. **Weak if-branch assertion (lines 617-635):** The if branch only asserts that at least one Error diagnostic exists, without checking that the error is specifically about `self` usage. A future regression introducing a different compile error (e.g. type mismatch) would still pass. Assert on the diagnostic message content.

Fix: In both branches, assert that error messages mention `self` or `unresolved`, not just that errors exist.

tasks.title: Eliminate magic number '3' in Check 2 of check-pm-standardization.sh
tasks.description:
In scripts/check-pm-standardization.sh line 52, the Check 2 assertion hardcodes `[ "$total" = '3' ]` where '3' must match the number of entries in PKG_FILES. If a fourth package.json is added to PKG_FILES, this magic number must be updated separately — a maintenance trap. Replace the hardcoded '3' with a dynamic count derived from PKG_FILES (e.g. convert PKG_FILES to an array and use `${#files[@]}`, or count words with `set -- $PKG_FILES; echo $#`). The test in test_npm_ci_hardening.sh Test 19 line 212 also asserts `total.* = '3'` and will need updating to match whatever approach is chosen.

tasks.title: Fix misleading conformance diagnostic + add let-trait coverage test
tasks.description:
Two related issues in conformance.rs from task 823 review:

1. **Misleading diagnostic message (conformance.rs:48):** The diagnostic says "trait member '{}' has no type annotation" but `p` is iterated from `structure.members`, not the trait's member list. Users see "trait member X has no type annotation" and look at the trait definition instead of the structure. Change message to "structure member '{}' has no type annotation; cannot infer type for trait conformance check" or similar wording.

2. **Behavioral change test gap (conformance.rs:58-83):** The Let branch now uses `?` to skip let bindings without type annotations from `structure_members`. Previously `let y = expr` (no annotation) would contribute `(y, Type::Real)` and potentially satisfy a trait requirement. Now it is excluded, so a trait requiring that member gets "missing required member" instead. The test `trait_let_no_type_annotation_compiles_clean` only covers the case where the trait does NOT require that let member. Add a test where the trait DOES require member `y` and the structure provides `let y = expr` without a type annotation, asserting the "missing required member" diagnostic fires.

tasks.title: Extract ICE diagnostic helper to reduce duplication in entity.rs/guards.rs
tasks.description:
The ICE diagnostic pattern (format ICE message + DiagnosticLabel + push + fallback to Type::Real) is repeated nearly identically across four sites:
- entity.rs:534-546 (structure param pass 2 resolution)
- entity.rs:877-889 (port param pass 2 resolution)
- guards.rs:269-281 (guarded member pass 2 resolution)

All share the same structure: "internal compiler error: unresolved name '...' in pass 2" with label "ICE: name should have been registered in pass 1".

Extract a helper like `fn emit_ice_unresolved(diagnostics: &mut Vec<Diagnostic>, name: &str, span: Span, context: &str) -> Type` that pushes the ICE diagnostic and returns `Type::Real`. This reduces ~12 lines per site to 1 and centralizes the ICE format.

tasks.title: Improve trust_the_declaration_tests: fix redundant characterization test and add ungated coverage
tasks.description:
Two improvements to `trust_the_declaration_tests` in `crates/reify-expr/tests/gradient_tests.rs`:

1. **Fix redundant characterization test (from review S1):** `test_make_dimensioned_domain_mismatch_gradient` currently asserts (a) Value is a Field (already checked inside helper), (b) domain_type == Type::length() (trivially true by construction), (c) grad_codomain_type == Scalar{MASS/LENGTH} (trivially true by construction). Either remove the test entirely (the two consumer tests exercise the helper), or make it useful by asserting on the *gradient field's actual codomain_type* — i.e., match on `grad_result` to extract `codomain_type` and assert it equals `grad_codomain_type`. This validates the helper's evaluated result, not just its return-value types.

2. **Add ungated release-mode coverage (from review S3):** `gradient_codomain_mismatch_dimensioned_domain_no_panic` is gated with `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]`. The debug_assert is trivially absent in release mode, but the *numerical result* (derivative ≈ 2.0, dimension MASS/LENGTH) is meaningful in all build profiles. Consider adding an ungated version of the sampling test, or removing the cfg gate, so the derivative value and dimension are validated in release builds too.

tasks.title: Restore defensive error behavior in FailingMockGeometryKernel::tessellate
tasks.description:
The old per-file `FailingMockGeometryKernel` in tessellation.rs had `tessellate()` return `Err(TessError::TessellationFailed("should not reach".into()))` — a deliberate fail-loud guard against unexpected downstream calls after `execute()` fails. The consolidated version in reify-test-support returns `Ok(empty mesh)`, silently masking any regression where `tessellate` is erroneously invoked on a handle that was never created.

Fix: Change `FailingMockGeometryKernel::tessellate()` in `crates/reify-test-support/src/mocks.rs` to return `Err(TessError::TessellationFailed("should not reach: execute always fails".into()))`. Update the corresponding unit test `failing_kernel_tessellate_returns_ok_empty_mesh` to assert the error case instead. Optionally, consider whether `export()` should also return a defensive error rather than writing `b"BOGUS_EXPORT"`.

tasks.title: Extend Test 22 with independent Check 1 and Check 3 failure fixtures
tasks.description:
Test 22 in tests/infra/test_npm_ci_hardening.sh currently covers the Check 2 happy path (22a) and version-mismatch path (22b), but does not independently exercise Check 1 (packageManager field present / npm@ prefix) or Check 3 (npm lockfiles must not be gitignored). Add fixture-based tests that independently fault-detect each check:

- 22c: fixture where one package.json has no packageManager field (or a non-npm@ value) — assert script exits non-zero (Check 1 failure).
- 22d: fixture where an npm lockfile path (e.g. gui/package-lock.json) IS listed in .gitignore — assert script exits non-zero (Check 3 failure).

This gives independent fault-detection coverage for each of the script's four checks, so a regression in any single check is caught by at least one behavioral test. Keep the existing 22a/22b cases. Follow the same fixture pattern: mktemp dir, copy scripts/check-pm-standardization.sh in, initialize git, write synthetic package.json files and .gitignore.

Source: review suggestion on task 1328 (reviewer_comprehensive).

tasks.title: Add `||` compound-form fixture to _has_if_n_guard robustness suite
tasks.description:
The `_has_if_n_guard` regex in tests/infra/test_test_helpers.sh:273 supports the `||` (OR) compound operator via the `(if|&&|\|\|)` alternation, but the robustness suite only exercises the `&&` form (fixture_compound_and at ~line 563). If the pipe escaping in the regex were broken, the `||` path would silently fail to match.

Add a fixture for the `||` form alongside fixture_compound_and, e.g.:

```sh
fixture_compound_or=$(mk_fixture)
printf 'something || [ -n "$var" ] && do_work\n' > "$fixture_compound_or"
if _has_if_n_guard "$fixture_compound_or" 2>/dev/null; then ok=true; else ok=false; fi
check "_has_if_n_guard detects compound || guard: || [ -n" "$ok"
```

Verification: `bash tests/infra/test_test_helpers.sh` passes with the new fixture and reports the added check.

Source: reviewer_comprehensive suggestion on Task 1267 review.

tasks.title: Strengthen mcp_passthrough_types_satisfy_ipc_contract test — wire-format assertions and Option coverage
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 985 review. The current mcp_passthrough_types_satisfy_ipc_contract test (gui/src-tauri/src/tests/types_tests.rs:515) only proves Rust-to-Rust round-trip self-consistency via serde_json::to_value -> from_value. That does NOT guard against a future `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]` or field rename on DiagnosticInfo/SelectionInfo silently breaking the GUI<->frontend IPC wire contract.

Scope:
1. After each round-trip in the test, add explicit assertions against the serde_json::Value keys — matching the style at line 84-89 of source_location_serializes_with_expected_fields. E.g. `assert_eq!(v["file_path"], json!("src/main.ri"))`, `assert!(v.get("filePath").is_none())`, `assert_eq!(v["selected_entity"], ...)`.
2. Cover both Option variants for DiagnosticInfo.code (Some and None) and SelectionInfo.hovered_entity (Some and None) — currently only one variant of each is exercised, so any future `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = ...)]` change would slip through.
3. Hoist `use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize}` (or `serde::de::DeserializeOwned`) to the top of types_tests.rs alongside the other imports, since the inner `use` inside the test body is only there for the local helper bound.
4. Soften the "~50 lines of boilerplate" estimate in gui/src-tauri/src/types.rs:21 — closer reality is 15-25 lines per type. Rewording to something like "a struct definition plus a From impl per type with zero behavioral benefit" is more accurate.

tasks.title: Reconcile doc-vs-code mismatch in downstream_ops_after_malformed_initialize_without_initialized
tasks.description: The doc comment for `downstream_ops_after_malformed_initialize_without_initialized` in crates/reify-lsp/tests/in_process_bridge.rs (lines ~962-978) explicitly lists two permitted outcomes — (a) downstream calls succeed, or (b) they return a well-defined non-empty Err — but the code uses `.expect(...)` which unconditionally panics on Err. The second documented outcome is therefore treated as a test failure. Decide policy: either (1) relax `.expect(...)` to a `match` that accepts `Ok(v)` and `Err(e)` when `!e.is_empty()`, honoring both documented outcomes, or (2) tighten the doc comment to say only success is acceptable today and note that relaxing it to allow Err is a future change. Surfaced by reviewer_comprehensive during task 1373 review; pre-existing mismatch that task 1373's wording change ("should succeed") further entrenched.

Preferred resolution likely depends on whether the bridge is expected to add a pre-handshake guard in the near future — if yes, option (1) relaxes the test now so the future change won't need to rewrite this test; if no, option (2) is simpler and removes the dead documentation.

tasks.title: Tidy stress_sweep_degenerate negative-case tests (numbering, placeholder, SI constant)
tasks.description:
Three small hygiene improvements to the negative_extrude_distance_is_valid and negative_revolve_angle_is_valid tests added by Task 1181 in crates/reify-eval/tests/stress_sweep_degenerate.rs:

1. Step numbering: the new tests are tagged 'step-1'/'step-2' (lines 221-222, 309-311) while the surrounding tests use 'step-13' through 'step-24'. Readers will assume the numbering is monotonic. Renumber as 'step-25'/'step-26' to continue the file's sequence, or use a namespaced tag like 'task-1181-step-1' so provenance is obvious.

2. Unused 'profile' placeholder arg: both tests pass a 'profile' named arg (lines 255, 347) that is never read by the Extrude/Revolve compile paths (lib.rs:3633-3652 and 3653-3677 only read 'distance' / 'ox..angle'). This mirrors the existing zero_extrude_distance / revolve_zero_angle pattern, but the inline comment 'placeholder expr' under-explains why an unused arg is present. Add a one-line comment at the top of each args vec, e.g. `// 'profile' is an ignored placeholder — Extrude/Revolve compile paths only read 'distance'/'angle'.`

3. Hardcoded SI constant: line 300 compares dist_si against the literal `-0.01` next to the literal `mm(-10.0)`. A refactor of the `mm` helper (unit-factor change) would silently desync literal and assertion. Replace with `let expected_si = mm(-10.0).as_f64().unwrap();` then `(dist_si - expected_si).abs() < 1e-9`.

All three are non-blocking review suggestions from Task 1181's reviewer_comprehensive pass (esc-1181-108).

tasks.title: Pin negative-near-zero rejection symmetry for extrude distance and revolve angle
tasks.description:
Add companion unit tests in crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs (next to compile_geometry_op_extrude_near_zero_distance_returns_none at ~line 4481 and compile_geometry_op_revolve_near_zero_angle_returns_none at ~line 4551) that assert compile_geometry_op returns None for negative-near-zero inputs (e.g. literal_length(-1e-15) and literal_f64(-1e-15)).

Rationale: the current near-zero unit tests only use positive 1e-15, so the `.abs() >= 1e-12` / `.abs() < 1e-12` symmetry on the rejection side is not pinned by any test. A refactor to an asymmetric check (e.g. `v >= -1e-12 || v.abs() >= 1e-12`) would silently pass. Task 1181's new characterization tests cover the positive-of-the-negative-path (finite, magnitude ≥ 1e-12, negative sign), but not the negative-near-zero boundary.

Reviewer explicitly called this a pre-existing coverage gap that belongs in follow-up. From reviewer_comprehensive on esc-1181-108.

tasks.title: Extract prelude-collection helper in module_dag.rs and avoid cloning in compile_project
tasks.description: The prelude-collection loop is duplicated verbatim in module_dag.rs between compile_module (lines 168-178) and compile_project (lines 241-251), differing only in self.modules vs dag.modules. Extract a private helper fn collect_import_preludes(parsed, modules) so future changes (transitive re-exports, de-duplication, filtering) live in one place. Additionally, compile_project clones every imported CompiledModule (templates, functions, fields, constraint_defs) and then immediately drains dag.modules via remove() — the clones are pure waste. Consider changing compile_with_prelude's signature to &[&CompiledModule] so compile_project can pass references, or build the owned Vec<CompiledModule> only after draining dag.modules in the right order. Apply both the deduplication and the clone-avoidance as a single refactor. Files: crates/reify-compiler/src/module_dag.rs, crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs (compile_with_prelude signature).

searches: prelude-collection module_dag compile_project duplicate clone refactor

tasks.title: Strengthen user_unit_in_let_binding test to verify computed value
tasks.description: The test user_unit_in_let_binding in crates/reify-compiler/tests/user_defined_unit_tests.rs:57-85 only asserts w_thou.default_expr.is_some(). A regression that silently mis-compiled '5thou' to the hardcoded 5mm fallback (si_value 0.005 instead of 0.000127) would still produce Some(...) and pass. The test docstring claims to verify 'QuantityLiteral resolution works in all expression contexts', but the assertion does not verify the actual factor is applied. Either (a) walk w_thou.default_expr (BinOp → right) to find the 5thou literal and assert si_value ≈ 5 * 0.0000254, or (b) simplify to a single let binding with a known scalar literal like user_unit_overrides_hardcoded_fallback does, so the test directly inspects si_value. Files: crates/reify-compiler/tests/user_defined_unit_tests.rs.

tasks.title: Fix misleading 'stdlib prelude' wording in duplicate-unit diagnostic
tasks.description: In crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:272-286, the duplicate-unit diagnostic hardcodes 'already defined in stdlib prelude' whenever the original entry has span SourceSpan::empty(0). Post-task-209, prelude units flow in from user imports via compile_project / compile_module as well, so a collision with an imported pub unit (e.g. the local_unit_duplicating_imported_pub_unit_produces_error test) emits a message that misattributes the conflict to stdlib. Fix options: (a) track the source of seeded prelude units (UnitEntry::source: Stdlib | Imported(module_path)) and select wording accordingly — 'already defined in stdlib prelude' vs 'already imported from module X'; (b) minimally, soften wording to 'already defined in imported prelude' so it reads correctly in both cases. The existing test only asserts 'duplicate' and 'mil' appear, so tighten the assertion to cover the new wording. Files: crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs, crates/reify-compiler/src/units.rs (UnitRegistry/UnitEntry if tracking source), crates/reify-compiler/tests/user_defined_unit_tests.rs.

tasks.title: Add compile_project negative test for imported private unit invisibility
tasks.description: The existing tests cross_module_pub_unit_visible_via_module_dag and cross_module_private_unit_not_visible_via_module_dag (crates/reify-compiler/tests/user_defined_unit_tests.rs:208-240) exercise ModuleDag::compile_module, which was already calling compile_with_prelude before task 209 (module_dag.rs:168-181). Only compile_project_entry_sees_imported_pub_unit covers the new compile_project prelude-seeding branch; there is no negative test that compile_project enforces privacy. Add compile_project_entry_does_not_see_imported_private_unit: mirror the positive test but declare 'unit privmil' (no pub) in the library module, invoke compile_project on the entry file, and assert the entry module reports a semantic error mentioning 'unknown' or 'privmil'. Keeps positive/negative coverage symmetric at the compile_project layer so regressions in either branch are caught. Files: crates/reify-compiler/tests/user_defined_unit_tests.rs.

tasks.title: Pin or fix one-hop limitation for transitive pub-unit propagation
tasks.description: No test covers transitive propagation of pub units: if module A declares 'pub unit mil', and module B has 'pub import a', does module C (which imports B) see 'mil'? Under the current implementation it does NOT — B's compiled_units only stores locally-declared units, so the prelude-seeding loop in lib.rs:237-251 finds nothing in B and mil is not re-exported. This matches the current treatment of constraint_defs (one-hop only), but it's undocumented and untested for units — a cross-module integration gap for task 209's scope. Prefer option (a): add a test that pins the current one-hop limitation (A→B→C where C cannot see mil) so any future fix is deliberate. Option (b) would extend scope: propagate pub-seeded prelude units into the current module's compiled_units when is_pub so they re-export transitively, plus a positive A→B→C test. Go with option (a) for this task; option (b) is a separate design decision. Files: crates/reify-compiler/tests/user_defined_unit_tests.rs.

tasks.title: Polish conjugate review round-2: misleading sign-of-zero comment, transient refs, and coverage-grid note
tasks.description:
Follow-up review polish for task 1201 (Polish conjugate sanitization tests and comment in reify-expr). Three suggestions from reviewer_comprehensive in `crates/reify-expr/src/complex.rs`, all localized to the conjugate method and its NaN/Inf test block. Apply all three:

1. **Misleading sign-of-zero comment and weak assertion (complex.rs:~743-766, `conjugate_pure_imaginary_correct`)** — The test comment claims it "exercises IEEE-754 sign-of-zero preservation" on `re`, but (a) conjugate copies `re` unchanged, so sign-of-zero on re isn't "preserved" in any interesting sense, and (b) the assertion `(re - 0.0).abs() < 1e-12` treats +0.0 and -0.0 as equal and would never catch a regression. Pick ONE of:
   - (a) Drop the "sign-of-zero preservation" phrase from the doc comment and describe only what the test actually exercises (pure-imaginary re=0.0 input under DIMENSIONLESS).
   - (b) Actually test sign-of-zero — most meaningfully on `im`, the field conjugate transforms. Use bit-pattern equality: `assert_eq!(im.to_bits(), (-5.0f64).to_bits())`, and add a second case with `im=+0.0` asserting the output `im.to_bits() == (-0.0f64).to_bits()` to pin negation-of-zero semantics. If you go this route, rename the test to reflect what it actually proves.
   
   Prefer (a) for minimal change; prefer (b) if you want the coverage. Don't do both half-measures.

2. **Transient task/commit references in source comment (complex.rs:63-66)** — The pre-guard comment embeds "task 860 history, restored by task 903 / commit 173921547". Task IDs are opaque to future readers and will rot if the task store is pruned; even the commit hash loses context without the task trail. The forward-compatibility rationale itself is valuable — keep that. Trim to something like: "The pre-guard is preferred for stylistic parity with `phase` and for forward-compatibility: if the Complex arm of `sanitize_value` is ever removed, conjugate stays safe." The task/commit provenance belongs in the commit message and in memory, not in source.

3. **Coverage-grid intent comment (complex.rs:606 block header)** — Optional, low-priority. Add a one-line annotation to the `// ── method: conjugate (NaN/Inf sanitization) ──` block header stating what the matrix covers (e.g., "NaN/±Inf × {re, im}, DIMENSIONLESS") so future contributors can see at a glance what's exercised and what's deliberately omitted (signed-zero, both-non-finite). Note: do NOT add tests for the omitted cases in this task — that's strictly out of scope.

Verification:
- `cargo test -p reify-expr --lib conjugate`
- `cargo test -p reify-expr --test complex_eval_tests method_conjugate` (if still present — confirm layout)
