- search "task 1201 conjugate polish"
- search "task 903 conjugate sanitization"
- convention: "don't embed transient task/commit refs in source comments"
- entities: conjugate, sanitize_value

tasks.title: Harden write-path poison-recovery coverage + reword assert_poison_recovers doc
tasks.description: Close the `access = "write"` coverage gap for `write_values` and `write_snapshot_values` that was dropped when `structured_field_emission` was deleted in Task 1350, and fix the now-overstated `assert_poison_recovers` doc comment.
tasks.details:
Background — Task 1350 deleted the per-site `structured_field_emission` module (crates/reify-runtime/tests/concurrent_eval.rs:2386-2672), which was the only place asserting `access = "write"` for the two write-path warn sites `write_values` (concurrent_eval.rs:119) and `write_snapshot_values` (concurrent_eval.rs:139). The surviving `tracing_warn_emitted_on_poison_evaluate` test (concurrent_eval.rs:2558-2581) only asserts `assert_any_event_has_fields(&[("lock", "values")])` and never touches the snapshot_values write path at all. A typo changing `access="write"` to `"writte"` or a dropped field on the write helpers would slip past every surviving test.

Separately, the doc comment on `assert_poison_recovers` (concurrent_eval.rs:1606-1623) claims the canonical-message and `error`-field invariants fire "for every warn site across all 3 families" and "collapses 11 per-site string literals into one authoritative check." In reality, the helper only fires when a test routes through it. The evaluate-based tests (`tracing_warn_emitted_on_poison_evaluate` and the three `evaluate_recovers_poisoned_*`) bypass the helper, and `all_three_locks_poisoned_shared_fallback_recovers_with_three_warns` wires the subscriber manually. So the contract covers 9 of 11 sites, not all 11.

Actions:

1. Extend `tracing_warn_emitted_on_poison_evaluate` (concurrent_eval.rs:2558-2581) to assert the specific pairs `[("lock", "values"), ("access", "write")]` via `capture.assert_any_event_has_fields(...)`.

2. Add a mirror test `tracing_warn_emitted_on_poison_evaluate_snapshot_values` that calls `adapter.poison_snapshot_values()` and asserts `[("lock", "snapshot_values"), ("access", "write")]` on at least one captured event. Verifies the write path on snapshot_values is still observable.

3. Reword the `assert_poison_recovers` doc comment (concurrent_eval.rs:1606-1623) to say "applied on every callsite that uses this helper" and explicitly list which warn sites fall outside the helper (write_values, write_snapshot_values at concurrent_eval.rs:119/139, plus the bulk shared-fallback test). Better alternative: route the evaluate-based and triple-fallback tests through the helper (or a sibling helper) so the claim becomes true — but this is optional; reword-only is acceptable.

4. Verify: `cd /home/leo/src/reify && cargo test -p reify-runtime --test concurrent_eval --features test-utils` — all poison_* tests must still pass.

Derived from Task 1350 reviewer suggestion #1 (test_coverage_regression) + #2 (misleading_documentation), reviewer_comprehensive.

tasks.title: Pin expected-panic prefix on warn_capture_assert_any_message_equals partial-substring test
tasks.description: The `warn_capture_assert_any_message_equals_panics_on_partial_substring` test uses a bare `#[should_panic]`, so any unrelated panic (mutex poisoning, subscriber panic-on-emit, format overflow) would silently keep it green. Pin the expected prefix.
tasks.details:
Location: crates/reify-test-support/src/tracing_support.rs:1579-1593 (`warn_capture_assert_any_message_equals_panics_on_partial_substring`).

Current:
```rust
#[test]
#[should_panic]
fn warn_capture_assert_any_message_equals_panics_on_partial_substring() {
    ...
    capture.assert_any_message_equals("lock poisoned, recovering");
}
```

Fix: Change to
```rust
#[test]
#[should_panic(expected = "no WARN message equaled")]
fn warn_capture_assert_any_message_equals_panics_on_partial_substring() {
    ...
}
```
matching the sibling test `warn_capture_assert_any_message_equals_panics_on_missing` at line 1559-1571. This ensures the panic originates from the strict-equality check, not from an unrelated failure.

Verify: `cd /home/leo/src/reify && cargo test -p reify-test-support warn_capture_assert_any_message_equals_panics_on_partial_substring -- --exact`

tasks.title: Refine build_modify_missing_arg_no_kernel_error test (naming, docstring, assertions)
tasks.description:
Four in-place refinements to `build_modify_missing_arg_no_kernel_error` in `crates/reify-eval/tests/geometry_error_handling.rs` (Task 1286 review):

1. **Rename (S1)**: The function is named `build_modify_missing_arg_no_kernel_error`, but the kernel IS called once for the preceding Box. The intent is 'no kernel call for the Modify op specifically'. Rename to `build_modify_missing_arg_no_modify_kernel_call` (or `..._skips_modify_kernel`) so the name matches the assertion. See section header on line 1073 ('no Modify kernel call') for the correct phrasing.

2. **Drop hardcoded line range in docstring (S2)**: Line 1096 references `compile_geometry_op_modify_missing_arg_returns_none` in `lib.rs:4955-5007`. Hardcoded line numbers go stale on every refactor. Reference by symbol only.

3. **Add 'all geometry operations failed' assertion (S3)**: The docstring item (2) says 'compile failure short-circuits the realization', but `geometry_output=None` actually arises from TWO causes: Fillet compile failure truncates realization handles (lib.rs:2929-2931), then the post-loop check at lib.rs:3015 fires the 'all geometry operations failed; no geometry output produced' diagnostic. A future regression that produced `geometry_output=None` for a different reason (e.g. accidental early return) would still pass. Add an assertion that diagnostics contain 'all geometry operations failed', mirroring the primitive test lines 950-958, to pin both halves of the contract.

4. **Tighten kernel-op assertion (S5)**: Line 1153-1157 only checks `matches!(ops[0].op, GeometryOp::Box { .. })` — it does not pin dimensions. A regression that recorded zeros or wrong arg order would still pass. Tighten to `assert!(matches!(&ops[0].op, GeometryOp::Box { width, height, depth } if *width == mm(80.0) && *height == mm(100.0) && *depth == mm(5.0)))`, or extract the dimensions into a shared constant so they don't drift between Box and Fillet setup.

**Source:** Review suggestions S1, S2, S3, S5 from Task 1286 (esc-1286-133).

tasks.title: Run cargo test --release in CI to exercise release-only invariant tests
tasks.description:
Several engine.rs invariant tests are gated behind `#[cfg(not(debug_assertions))]` and only run under `cargo test --release`. The current `orchestrator.yaml` test_command runs `cargo test --workspace -- --test-threads=1` (debug only), so the release-mode graceful-fallback path documented in the debug_assert comments is not exercised in the default verify pipeline.

Affected tests in `gui/src-tauri/src/tests/engine_tests.rs`:
- `get_diagnostics_returns_empty_when_module_name_broken`
- `get_diagnostics_returns_empty_when_source_map_broken`

- `get_source_location_returns_none_when_module_name_broken` (new in task 1377)
- `get_source_location_returns_none_when_source_map_broken` (new in task 1377)

Options:
1. Add a second `cargo test --release` pass to `orchestrator.yaml`'s test_command (cost: ~2x compile + test time).
2. Add a release-only CI job that runs only the affected test names via filter.
3. Drop the cfg gate and use runtime `if cfg!(debug_assertions)` branching inside the tests instead — single pass covers both modes.

Decide which option fits the verify-pipeline budget and apply it.

tasks.title: Strengthen fn-regex meta-tests: add pub+triple-qualifier combos and symmetric reject case
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1278 review (esc-1278-130, S1+S2). Strengthen the meta-test coverage of the fn-existence regex in `tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh` so it exercises both halves of the pattern (visibility group + qualifier group) simultaneously and locks down the keyword ordering symmetrically.

Required additions in `tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh`:

Accept assertions (catch regressions where the visibility group accidentally consumes a qualifier slot):
- `pub const unsafe fn sanitize_value(`
- `pub(crate) async unsafe fn sanitize_value(`
- `pub(crate) const unsafe fn sanitize_value(`

Reject assertion (symmetric to the existing `unsafe async fn` reject):
- `unsafe const fn sanitize_value(` — invalid Rust grammar order (Rust requires `const unsafe`, not `unsafe const`).

Documentation comment near the reject block explaining the intentional gap: `const async fn` and `const async unsafe fn` are NOT asserted-against because the regex (correctly) permits them even though they are semantically impossible (`const fn` cannot be `async`). The regex validates grammar order, not semantics.

tasks.title: Extend extract_fn in sync_comments_test.sh to strip const/async/unsafe qualifiers
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1278 review (esc-1278-130, S3). Pre-existing latent bug made more reachable by Task 1278's broadened accept set.

Location: `tests/sync_comments_test.sh:47-55`

The `extract_fn` helper currently strips only `pub`/`pub(...)` from the captured signature line:
```sh
extract_fn() {
    local fn_name="$1" file="$2"
    awk '/^[^/]*fn '"$fn_name"'[(<]/,/^}/' "$file" |
        sed 's/^pub([^)]*) *//' |
        sed 's/^pub //'
}
```

If either copy of `sanitize_value` ever picks up a `const`/`async`/`unsafe` qualifier (which the regex now correctly admits after Task 1278), the body diff would falsely fail because one side would carry the qualifier in the captured first line and the other would not. This is not currently a bug because neither copy uses qualifiers, but the escalation explicitly notes it is "more reachable as a result of the broader accept set."

Fix: extend the `sed` chain in `extract_fn` to also strip leading `const `, `async `, and `unsafe ` in any valid combination from the captured signature line, or normalise both signatures before diffing. Verify that order-tolerant stripping works:
- `const unsafe fn sanitize_value(` → `fn sanitize_value(`
- `async unsafe fn sanitize_value(` → `fn sanitize_value(`
- `pub(crate) const unsafe fn sanitize_value(` → `fn sanitize_value(`

Add corresponding Section 3 extract_fn fixture tests in `tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh` exercising the qualifier-stripping path.

Verify with:
- `bash tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh`
- `bash tests/sync_comments_test.sh`

Source: esc-1278-130 S3 (steward-triage from Task 1278 post-merge review)

tasks.title: Document or extend sync_ref_helpers regex support for extern and default fn forms
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1278 review (esc-1278-130, S4). Future-proofing for fn forms not currently used anywhere in the SYNC-tracked codebase.

Location: `tests/infra/sync_ref_helpers.sh:35`

The fn-existence regex in `assert_sync_ref_exists` does not handle:
- `extern "C" fn foo(...)` (FFI declarations)
- `default fn foo(...)` (specialised trait impls)

Neither form appears in any current SYNC-tracked function, but if a future `// SYNC:` pair ever points at such a function, `assert_sync_ref_exists` will silently fail with a confusing "fn does not exist" message.

Two fix options:
1. **Extend the regex**: add `(default[[:space:]]+)?` and `(extern([[:space:]]+\"[^\"]*\")?[[:space:]]+)?` alternation groups. Add matching accept assertions to `tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh`.
2. **Document the limitation**: add a comment in the helper noting that `extern` and `default` fn forms are unsupported, and that SYNC pairs should avoid pointing at such functions (or be upgraded when first needed).

Option 2 is preferred for now — this is purely speculative future work. Revisit if a SYNC pair ever needs it.

Verify (option 2) with: `bash tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh` still passes.

Source: esc-1278-130 S4 (steward-triage from Task 1278 post-merge review)

tasks.title: Slim redundant explicit assertions in value_ord_real_negative_nan / signaling_nan tests

- value_ord_real_negative_nan (around lines 2725, 2728, 2731): drop the explicit neg_qnan.cmp(&pos_qnan)==Less, assert_ne!(neg_qnan, pos_qnan), and antisymmetry-reverse lines for the (neg_qnan, pos_qnan) pair; the (neg_qnan, neg_inf) explicit Less at 2722 is still needed only if not already covered by the (e) helper on that pair — which it is (line 2734), so drop it too.
- value_ord_real_signaling_nan (around lines 2761, 2762, 2766, 2771, 2772): drop the explicit Less/Greater, assert_ne, and antisymmetry-reverse lines for the (neg_snan, neg_inf), (neg_snan, neg_qnan), (pos_snan, pos_qnan) pairs that are now covered by assert_ord_consistent calls at lines 2774-2781.

Keep any explicit lines whose pair is NOT covered by a helper call, and keep the overall test docstring intact. Verify with `cargo test -p reify-types value_ord_real_`.

Source: Task 1390 review suggestion S2 from reviewer_comprehensive (esc-1390-135).

tasks.title: Fix vacuous regression guard in test_self_path_constants_use_manifest_dir
tasks.description:
Review suggestion from task 1321 review (reviewer_comprehensive, category: vacuous_regression_guard).

Location: tree-sitter-reify/tests/build_logic_tests.rs:1378-1400 (the new test introduced by task 1321).

Problem: The new `test_self_path_constants_use_manifest_dir` asserts `source.contains("env!(\"CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR\")")` against the file's own contents. But the test's own doc-comment at line 1381 contains the literal text `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` inside backticks. Verified: exactly 3 occurrences of that string exist in the file — lines 56, 60 (the real THIS_FILE / BUILD_RS constants) and line 1381 (the comment). If a future change replaces BOTH constants with hard-coded relative paths, the test would still pass because the comment alone would satisfy the contains-check. The regression guard is therefore vacuous — it cannot fail in the way its prose claims.

Fix (reviewer-recommended, option b is strictly stronger): change the assertion to require `source.matches("env!(\"CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR\")").count() >= 2`. This enforces that BOTH THIS_FILE and BUILD_RS use the macro, catches a regression in either constant individually, and makes the comment self-referential text a non-issue. Alternatively, match the more specific pattern `concat!(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` which only appears in the const declarations (not the comment).

While fixing: also rephrase the doc comment to refer to the macro indirectly — e.g., "the env! macro on CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR" — so any future blanket-text scan cannot be tripped by the doc.

Verify: `cargo test -p tree-sitter-reify test_self_path_constants_use_manifest_dir` passes, and mutation-check by temporarily replacing one of the two const definitions with a bare relative path and confirming the test now fails.

tasks.title: Introduce read_self_source() helper to dedupe THIS_FILE reads in build_logic_tests.rs
tasks.description:
Review suggestion from task 1321 review (reviewer_comprehensive, category: minor_duplication, explicitly flagged non-blocking cleanup).

Location: tree-sitter-reify/tests/build_logic_tests.rs — after the task 1321 split, both `test_self_read_paths_use_manifest_dir` (line 1341) and `test_self_path_constants_use_manifest_dir` (line 1391) independently call `std::fs::read_to_string(THIS_FILE).expect("should be able to read this test file via THIS_FILE")` with the same expect message. Grep confirms the file has ~6 self-reading call sites total: lines 934, 975, 1043, 1341, 1391, 1409.

Fix: introduce a tiny helper near the THIS_FILE const:

```rust
fn read_self_source() -> String {
    std::fs::read_to_string(THIS_FILE)
        .expect("should be able to read this test file via THIS_FILE")
}
```

Replace the six call sites. Keeps the expect message in one place and reduces noise.

Verify: `cargo test -p tree-sitter-reify --tests` all pass.

Priority: low — reviewer explicitly flagged this as acceptable duplication, non-blocking cleanup.

tasks.title: Refactor Task 1238 dimensional-correctness tests: helper extraction, let-else, doc fixes
tasks.description:
Task 1238 added 10 new dimensional-correctness tests (~765 lines) to crates/reify-expr/tests/field_calculus_tests.rs:3477-4240. Each test is ~70 lines of near-identical boilerplate (build ids, build lambda body, build Field, build Type::Field, make_function_call, eval, assert matches Field, then if-let for codomain assertion). The file already has the helper pattern via `run_divergence_identity_test` (line 81) and `run_curl_rotation_test` (line 170) — the new code regressed on that convention.

Consolidated fixes (from Task 1238 review suggestions S1, S2, S3, S4, S5, S6):

1. **Factor a helper** `fn run_dim_metadata_test(op: &str, domain: Type, codomain: Type, source: FieldSourceKind, expected_codomain: Type, label: &str)` that takes the operation name ("laplacian" or "divergence"), domain/codomain types, source kind, and expected result codomain, and contains the scaffolding. Body lambda content is metadata-only and can be a dummy parameterized by domain arity. Reduces each test to ~10 lines.

2. **Replace assertion pattern** — change each `assert!(matches!(&result, Value::Field { .. })); if let Value::Field { .. } = &result { ... }` to a let-else: `let Value::Field { codomain_type, .. } = &result else { panic!("..."); };` so inner assertions are not gated by an outer match guard.

3. **Fix doc-comment inaccuracies**:
   - `laplacian_dimensional_correctness_2d_point_domain` (line 3548): comment claims it exercises the `Type::Point { n: 2, .. }` arm, but `compute_laplacian` does NOT pattern-match on `n`. Rephrase to clarify it confirms dimension-agnostic behavior for n=2.
   - `divergence_dimensional_correctness_1d` (line 3933) / `_2d` (line 4006): same issue — `compute_divergence` has no n-discriminating arm. Rephrase to remove the "n=1/n=2 arm" wording.

4. **Review `laplacian_dimensional_correctness_int_domain_int_codomain`** (line 3700): largely redundant with `laplacian_dimensional_correctness_int_codomain` — `dim_quotient_type`'s outer guard `cd != DIMENSIONLESS && dd != DIMENSIONLESS` short-circuits on `cd == DIMENSIONLESS`, so the domain dimension is never evaluated once codomain is Int. Either drop it or correct the doc to explain it covers the Int-domain input-value case, not a distinct branch.

5. **Remove hard-coded source line numbers** from doc comments at lines 3483, 3548, 3776, 3852, 4092, 4166 (e.g. `calculus.rs:386`, `calculus.rs:69-71`, `calculus.rs:375`, `calculus.rs:183`). Reference function names and branch descriptions instead — e.g. "the `_ if scalar_dimension(domain_type).is_some()` first arm of `compute_laplacian`".

Scope: test file only. No production code changes. Do not drop coverage — after the helper refactor there should still be 10 test cases exercising (a) 1D/2D fields (b) Int domains/codomains (c) dimensionless-result branch (d) Composed source kind.

Memory hints:
- search "field_calculus_tests run_divergence_identity_test helper pattern" — existing helper convention
- search "task 1238 dimensional correctness new tests" — what was added

tasks.title: Polish behavioral Test 24 in test_portable_timeout.sh (naming, regex scope, robustness, cleanup)
tasks.description:
Five post-merge review polish suggestions targeting the behavioral Test 24 block added in Task 1343 (tests/infra/test_portable_timeout.sh:614-720). All five touch the same ~80-line region and should be applied as a single coherent polish pass.

**1. Test naming/header clarity (line 614)**
Two distinct tests share the number "Test 24" — the structural one at line 600 and the new behavioral one at line 663 — with no suffix to disambiguate and no `echo "--- Test 24 ..."` header for the behavioral block (inconsistent with every other test in the file). Rename to `Test 24b` (matching the existing 18b/18c convention) or Test 25, and add a header line: `echo ""; echo "--- Test 24b (behavioral): _pt_kill_grace override flows to timer ---"`.

**2. Doc overstatement on Test 22 regex (lines 578-582)**
The comment claims the regex covers SIGTERM "in any syntactic form — quoted or unquoted, with or without '|| true', any redirection." That overstates: the regex `(^|[^-9])kill[[:space:]]+--[[:space:]]+"?-\$cmd_pid` requires the literal `kill ... --` token sequence and would NOT catch forms like `kill -s TERM -- "-$cmd_pid"`, `kill -TERM -- ...`, or `kill --signal=TERM -- ...`. Either tighten the regex to also match `kill[[:space:]]+(-s[[:space:]]+TERM|-TERM|--signal=TERM)[[:space:]]+--`, or trim the comment to say "no line uses the canonical `kill -- -$cmd_pid` form (quoted or unquoted, with optional `|| true` or redirection)". Trimming the comment is simpler since the codebase only uses the canonical form.

**3. Behavioral Test 24 margin is too tight (lines 660-666)**
The behavioral test measures elapsed time with `date +%s` (1-second integer resolution) and asserts `gap >= 4`. Expected wall-clock is ~5s (1s timer + 4s grace); integer truncation and scheduler jitter can legitimately produce gap=4 with zero margin. The only safe regression bucket is gap==3; single-bucket margins are fragile under CI load. Fix: override `_pt_kill_grace=5` instead of 4 — expected ~6s, lower bound 5 still excludes the 2s regression. Minimal change, preserves POSIX portability.

**4. Substitution anchor robustness (lines 651-657)**
`${func_text/_pt_kill_grace=2/_pt_kill_grace=4}` replaces only the first occurrence. If a future change adds an unrelated `_pt_kill_grace=2` token earlier in the function body (comment, heredoc, string literal), the substitution silently rewrites the wrong site and the test passes vacuously. Fix: anchor on the `local` keyword: `${func_text/local _pt_kill_grace=2/local _pt_kill_grace=4}`. This matches the structural Test 24 invariant on line 608 (`local[[:space:]]+_pt_kill_grace=2`).

**5. rescue_dir leak on sanity-fail path (lines 640-671)**
The new test cleans up `rescue_dir` with `rm -rf` on line 666, but ONLY on the success path. If the SANITY FAIL branch (line 654) fires after `eval "$POSIX_FALLBACK_SETUP_NO_TRAP"` succeeds, the test exits 1 and `rescue_dir` leaks under `$TMPDIR`. Fix: move cleanup to a `trap 'rm -rf "$rescue_dir"' EXIT` set immediately after the eval succeeds. This is safe inside the outer `bash -c` because the timer subshell has already terminated by the time the trap fires (the helper's "no EXIT trap" concern applies to portable_timeout's internal subshells, not to this outer shell).

**Context:** All five suggestions come from reviewer_comprehensive's post-merge review of Task 1343 (esc-1343-138). Task 1343 itself fixed an unbound-variable bug in the behavioral Test 24 block (commit 5c5801029) — these are follow-up polish items, not the same class of bug.

**Memory hints:** search "test_portable_timeout behavioral Test 24" for Task 1343 context; the structural Test 24 at line 600 is the existing reference for suffix naming conventions (18b/18c).

A third file, `unit_registry_tests.rs`, has similar boilerplate. Move these helpers into a shared `crates/reify-compiler/tests/common/mod.rs` (the standard Cargo integration-test shared-code pattern), and update all three consumers to `mod common; use common::{parse_and_compile, compile_with_prelude_helper, errors_only};`. tasks.title: Consolidate overlapping unit tests and tighten weak assertions
tasks.description:
The new `crates/reify-compiler/tests/unit_declaration_tests.rs` (task 210) duplicates coverage already present in `user_defined_unit_tests.rs` and `unit_registry_tests.rs`. Consolidate and fix test-quality issues in one pass.

**Redundant coverage to remove or consolidate:**
- `cross_module_pub_unit_visible_via_prelude` (unit_declaration_tests.rs:217) duplicates `cross_module_pub_unit_visible_via_compile_with_prelude` (user_defined_unit_tests.rs:132).
- `cross_module_private_unit_not_visible_via_prelude` (unit_declaration_tests.rs:262) duplicates `cross_module_private_unit_not_visible_via_compile_with_prelude` (user_defined_unit_tests.rs:180).
- `duplicate_unit_name_shadowing_prelude_is_error` (unit_declaration_tests.rs:315) duplicates `local_unit_duplicating_imported_pub_unit_produces_error` in user_defined_unit_tests.rs and `local_unit_duplicate_of_prelude_emits_error` (unit_registry_tests.rs:1382).
- `duplicate_unit_name_in_same_module_is_error` (unit_declaration_tests.rs:293) duplicates `duplicate_unit_name_emits_error` (unit_registry_tests.rs:606).
- `user_unit_in_quantity_literal_evaluates_to_si_value_length` (unit_declaration_tests.rs:342) duplicates `quantity_literal_uses_registry_unit` (unit_registry_tests.rs:510).
- `custom_unit_declaration_via_cross_ref_quantity_literal` (unit_declaration_tests.rs:71) overlaps `unit_cross_ref_in_conversion_expr` (unit_registry_tests.rs:622).
- `offset_unit_degc_in_quantity_literal_converts_to_kelvin` (unit_declaration_tests.rs:139) overlaps `offset_unit_quantity_literal_applies_offset` (unit_registry_tests.rs:749).

**Resolution:** Either (a) delete the duplicates from `unit_declaration_tests.rs` and add 2-3 tests for genuinely new behavior (compound ENERGY/PRESSURE dimension, negative-factor unit in quantity literal, user-defined angle unit interacting with stdlib `deg`), or (b) keep `unit_declaration_tests.rs` as the single acceptance suite and delete the overlapping tests elsewhere. Task 210's minimum of ≥12 tests is met either way.

**Test-quality fixes to apply in the same pass:**
1. **Weak unknown-unit assertion** — `unit_declaration_tests.rs:282-287` uses `d.message.contains("unknown") || d.message.contains("hidden_mil")`, which matches any diagnostic mentioning `hidden_mil`. Tighten to `d.message.to_lowercase().contains("unknown unit") && d.message.contains("hidden_mil")`, or match a stable diagnostic code. Apply the same fix to the upstream clone at `user_defined_unit_tests.rs:199-205`.
2. **Missing dimension assertion in compound-dimension test** — `custom_unit_with_compound_dimension_area_in_quantity_literal` (`unit_declaration_tests.rs:474`) only destructures `Value::Scalar { si_value, .. }` and never verifies `dimension == DimensionVector::AREA`. A bug producing the right number with the wrong dimension would pass. Destructure `Value::Scalar { si_value, dimension, .. }` and assert `*dimension == DimensionVector::AREA`. Extend the `custom_unit_with_compound_dimension_force` test similarly with a quantity-literal-in-param variant asserting `*dimension == reify_types::dimension::FORCE`.
3. **Test clarity — cross-ref quantity literal** — `custom_unit_declaration_via_cross_ref_quantity_literal` (`unit_declaration_tests.rs:71`) declares `unit mm : Length = 0.001`, which matches the hardcoded fallback factor. The expected `thou.factor = 0.0000254` would hold even if the compiler silently fell back to the hardcoded `mm`. Change the declared factor to a distinctive value (e.g. `unit mm : Length = 0.005`, expected thou.factor = 0.000127), or rename the unit (`unit mymm : Length = 0.001`).

If duplicates are deleted (option a), items 1 and 3 may become moot; resolve accordingly.

Scope: tests only.

tasks.title: Fix dead self-check and complete if/else → unified-form refactor in test_test_helpers.sh
tasks.description:
Task 1269 added a self-check at tests/infra/test_test_helpers.sh:598-606 intended to pin the `fixture_clean` check to the unified single-line form. Reviewer_comprehensive flagged three related issues:

S1 (test_quality_dead_check): The self-check is a no-op. It greps for the OLD description text 'if-guard pattern returns true for clean file', but that string was removed by the same diff — the live description (line 504) is '_has_if_n_guard reports no-guard for clean file (no false positive)'. The only line in the file containing the old substring is line 601 itself, so `grep -c` returns 1 and the `-le 1` predicate is always true. The assertion provides no actual protection: a future change reverting the if/else form using the NEW description string would not be caught.

S2 (logic_off_by_one): Even if the grep pattern were updated, `-le 1` is too lax. It passes when the description is missing entirely (count == 0), silently masking deletion of the test itself. Should be `-eq 1` (or equivalent exact-count semantics).

S3 (refactor_incompleteness): The PR message states the motivation is that the if/else form 'placed the same description on both branches, which is ambiguous in CI output', but the unified form was applied to exactly one site (line 503). ~10+ other call sites in the same file still emit identical descriptions on both branches — e.g. lines 32-36, 42-46, 63-67, 73-77, 91-95, 121-125, 131-143, 167-171, plus checks inside the consumer loop (lines 204-247). Intent and implementation are inconsistent.

Recommended fix (folds all three): Replace the narrow pin at lines 598-606 with a generic file-wide invariant — scan the file for any `check "<desc>"` description that appears on more than one distinct line, and fail the self-check if any description is duplicated. This both protects every check (not just `fixture_clean`) and naturally drives completion of the refactor: the invariant will fail until the remaining ~10+ if/else duplicate-description sites are migrated to the unified `ok=true/false; check ... "$ok"` form. Migration is low-risk and mechanical.

Suggested implementation sketch for the invariant:
```
# Extract all `check "DESC" ...` DESC values, find duplicates.
dup_count=$(grep -oE 'check "[^"]+"' "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" \
    | sort | uniq -d | wc -l)
if [ "$dup_count" -eq 0 ]; then ok=true; else ok=false; fi
check "no duplicate check descriptions in this file" "$ok"
```
(Note: this is a single invariant that supersedes both the current dead `fixture_clean` pin and the need for per-site pins.)

Verification: `bash tests/infra/test_test_helpers.sh` must still pass after migration; the new invariant should report 0 duplicates.

tasks.title: Suppress redundant generic error when compile_geometry_op emits a specific diagnostic
tasks.description:
In crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs, when `compile_geometry_op` returns `None` after emitting a specific Warning diagnostic (e.g. "extrude dropped: distance=... is degenerate"), all three caller sites (lines ~2920, ~3000, ~3121) unconditionally push a redundant generic `Diagnostic::error("failed to compile geometry operation")`. Users see two messages for the same root cause: the helpful warning plus a redundant generic error.

Task 1187 addressed this pattern for Primitive/Modify branches, but the new degenerate-geometry warnings added by Task 1199 in the Sweep branches reintroduce the issue.

**Approach options:**
1. Return `Result<GeometryOp, SkipReason>` where `SkipReason::Explained` suppresses the caller's generic error and `SkipReason::Unexplained` still produces it.
2. Have callers check `diagnostics.len()` before/after the call and skip the generic error if a diagnostic was already emitted by the function.

Option 1 is cleaner but a larger refactor; Option 2 is pragmatic.

tasks.title: Extract degenerate-geometry epsilon constants and use scientific notation in warnings
tasks.description:
In crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs, the epsilon `1e-12` is repeated three times in the degenerate-geometry guards (lines ~3653, ~3683, ~3695) and echoed in warning strings. Two improvements:

1. **Named constants:** Extract `const DEGENERATE_LENGTH_EPSILON_M: f64 = 1e-12;` and `const DEGENERATE_ANGLE_EPSILON_RAD: f64 = 1e-12;`. Reference them in both the guard conditions and warning format strings to prevent drift.

2. **Scientific notation:** Change the format strings from `{}` (Display) to `{:e}` for f64 values. Near 1e-12/1e-15, Display renders as long decimal strings like `0.000000000000001` instead of `1e-15`, making the diagnostic harder to read.

tasks.title: Add end-to-end test asserting degenerate-geometry warnings reach BuildResult.diagnostics
tasks.description:
The new degenerate-geometry warnings (extrude dropped, revolve dropped) are verified by unit tests that call `compile_geometry_op` directly. However, nothing verifies the warnings actually propagate through `Engine::build` / `Engine::check` / `Engine::tessellate_realizations` to `BuildResult.diagnostics`.

The existing end-to-end tests in `crates/reify-eval/tests/stress_sweep_degenerate.rs` (`zero_extrude_distance`, `revolve_zero_angle`) assert only that the mock kernel receives no op — they do not assert the new warnings are surfaced. A regression that drops the warning on its way out would not be caught.

**Fix:** Extend `zero_extrude_distance` and `revolve_zero_angle` in `stress_sweep_degenerate.rs` to assert that `result.diagnostics` contains a Warning whose message contains `extrude dropped` / `revolve dropped`.

tasks.title: Audit compile_geometry_op for silent return-None branches missing diagnostics
tasks.description:
The new warn-and-drop pattern added by Task 1199 applies only to Extrude distance, Revolve axis, and Revolve angle. Several sibling branches in `compile_geometry_op` (crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs, lines ~3404-3605) still silently return `None` on degenerate/invalid input, leaving the user with only the caller's generic "failed to compile geometry operation" error:

- `PrimitiveKind::Cylinder` / `Sphere` with missing args

tasks.title: Emit diagnostic when scale rejected for negative factor
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs:3548-3552`, the negative scale factor check returns None without emitting a diagnostic. Update the test `compile_geometry_op_scale_negative_factor_returns_none` (line 4737) to also assert a diagnostic was emitted, matching the pattern of `compile_geometry_op_scale_nan_factor_returns_none` (line 4774).

tasks.title: Extract IEEE 754 totalOrder test helper and simplify round-trip tests
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-types/src/value.rs`, the IEEE 754 totalOrder golden-ordering assertion block (7 position lookups + 6 chain assertions on boundary floats) is copy-pasted across 5 tests (~150 lines total): `value_btreeset_boundary_real_iteration_order` (line 2595), `value_set_real_boundary_iteration_order_through_variant` (line 3082), `value_map_real_boundary_key_iteration_order_through_variant` (line 3144), `value_set_round_trip_preserves_iteration_order` (line 3209), and `value_map_round_trip_preserves_key_iteration_order` (line 3291). The boundary float array `[0.0, -0.0, INFINITY, NEG_INFINITY, NAN, -1.0, 1.0]` is also repeated.

**What to do:**
1. Extract a helper like `fn assert_ieee754_total_order_real(floats: &[f64])` that encapsulates the 7-position lookup and 6-assertion chain.
2. Define a `const BOUNDARY_REALS: &[f64]` in the test module.
3. While refactoring, simplify the round-trip tests (`value_set_round_trip_preserves_iteration_order` and `value_map_round_trip_preserves_key_iteration_order`): their structural-equality and content-hash assertions (parts a-c) are tautological given BTreeSet/BTreeMap stdlib guarantees (Ord alone determines order, not insertion order). Either add a comment noting the real value is the golden-ordering assertion (part d), or fold the hash check into the ordering tests as a one-liner and drop the standalone round-trip tests.

tasks.title: Add cross-variant float boundary ordering tests for Scalar/Complex/Orientation
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-types/src/value.rs`, the task-974 float-boundary iteration-order tests (lines 3082-3369) only exercise `Value::Real`. The doc comments on `Value::Set` and `Value::Map` explicitly call out `Value::Scalar`, `Value::Complex`, and `Value::Orientation` as also using `total_cmp` in their `Ord` impls. A regression in one of those arms (e.g. Scalar comparing dimension strings before the float, or Complex comparing real-then-imaginary) would not be caught.

**What to do:**
Add at least one cross-variant boundary test for each:
- `Value::Scalar` entries with the same dimension but boundary float values (NEG_INFINITY, -0.0, +0.0, NAN, etc.) — verify total_cmp ordering through the Scalar Ord path.
- `Value::Complex` entries with identical real parts but boundary-valued imaginary parts — verify total_cmp is exercised on the imaginary component.
- `Value::Orientation` entries with boundary float components — verify total_cmp through the quaternion comparison path.

These can be Set-based tests using the assert helper from the dedup task, or standalone.

**memory_hints:** search "Value Ord total_cmp implementation Scalar Complex Orientation"

tasks.title: test_npm_ci_hardening: add negative DIAGNOSTIC test and harden Test 24 output capture
tasks.description:

Memory hints: search "DIAGNOSTIC check-pm-standardization test_npm_ci_hardening" for prior context.
tasks.details:
Two improvements from review of task 1360:

1. **Negative DIAGNOSTIC test (new Test 22a-bis or extension to Test 22a):** Test 22a runs the script in a fixture where no lockfiles are gitignored and asserts exit 0, but does NOT explicitly verify `DIAGNOSTIC:` is absent in stdout. Test 24 verifies DIAGNOSTIC: IS emitted when a lockfile is gitignored. Without the negative test, a regression that always emits DIAGNOSTIC: (e.g. removing the `if` guard in Check 3) would still pass both tests — a false green. Fix: capture Test 22a stdout and assert `! grep -q DIAGNOSTIC:`.

2. **Harden Test 24 output capture:** Test 24 currently runs `bash script 2>&1 | grep -q 'DIAGNOSTIC:'`. This depends on (a) assert() not short-circuiting the script, and (b) no pipefail in the outer bash -c. Make explicit: `out=$(bash script 2>&1 || true); echo "$out" | grep -q 'DIAGNOSTIC:'`. Add a short comment documenting the dependency.

tasks.title: Polish assert_extraction helper: multi-line formatting and &Value signature
tasks.description:
Two follow-up improvements to the `assert_extraction` test helper in `crates/reify-stdlib/src/helpers.rs` (introduced by Task 1453):

1. **Line formatting (readability):** The 6 success-path test calls (lines ~337-363) are 200-280 characters wide single lines. Break each call across multiple lines or extract a `let v = Value::…` binding before calling `assert_extraction(v, …)` — keeps lines under ~100-120 chars while preserving the deduplication win.

2. **Signature consistency (test_quality):** `assert_extraction` takes `input: Value` by move (line 164), then immediately borrows it to call `tensor_components_f64(&input)`. Change the signature to `fn assert_extraction(input: &Value, …)` and update callers to pass `&Value::…` or bind-and-reference. This matches the function-under-test's `&Value` parameter and allows callers to reuse the input for Debug prints on failure.

Memory hints: search "tensor_components_f64 helpers", get_entity "helpers.rs"

tasks.title: Harden field_calculus_tests helpers with explicit panics for unsupported inputs
tasks.description:
Replace silent wildcard fallbacks in `make_sample_point` and `eval_field_op` test helpers with explicit panics so future misuse fails loudly.

Three changes from Task 1291 review (S1, S5, S6):

1. **Line 546 / line 590 — dimension cap**: `make_sample_point` uses a fixed `[1.0, 2.0, 3.0]` array and `eval_field_op` uses `["x", "y", "z"]`, both capping at 3 dimensions. Add `assert!(*n <= 3, "...only supports up to 3 dimensions")` at the top of the Point arm in both functions.

2. **Line 554 — inner wildcard**: The `_ => Value::Real(v)` arm inside the `Type::Point` match of `make_sample_point` silently falls back for unexpected quantity types (e.g., Type::Vector, Type::Field). Replace with `other => panic!("make_sample_point: unsupported quantity type in Point: {:?}", other)`.

3. **Line 567 — outer wildcard**: The top-level `_ => (Value::Real(1.0), Type::Real)` arm silently handles unexpected domain types. Replace with `other => panic!("make_sample_point: unsupported domain type: {:?}", other)`.

Memory hints:
- search "field_calculus_tests make_sample_point eval_field_op" for helper context
- search "Task 1291 review suggestions defensive coding" for origin

tasks.title: test_portable_timeout.sh: simplify kill regex + add substitution count check
tasks.description: Two fixes in tests/infra/test_portable_timeout.sh: (1) Line 649: remove dead `(^|[^-9])` prefix from kill regex — the `kill[[:space:]]+--` pattern already discriminates correctly. (2) Line 707: add count verification after the case-sanity-check to ensure exactly 1 occurrence of `local _pt_kill_grace=2` before parameter substitution, preventing silent partial replacement if a second occurrence is introduced.

tasks.title: Restore lost numerical value assertions in dimensional divergence/laplacian tests
tasks.description:
The Task 1291 refactoring to use `eval_field_op` lost numerical value assertions in two dimensioned sampling tests. The tests now only check dimensional tagging, not the computed value.

Two changes from Task 1291 review (S2, S3):

1. **`divergence_sample_dimensional_correctness_returns_scalar` (line ~1868)**: The identity-field body from `eval_field_op` produces divergence ≈ 3.0. Re-add `assert!((si_value - 3.0).abs() < 1e-4, ...)` in the `Value::Scalar { si_value, dimension }` match arm. This is straightforward since the eval_field_op identity field still produces the correct value.

2. **`laplacian_sample_dimensional_correctness_returns_scalar` (line ~1961)**: The linear body from `eval_field_op` produces laplacian ≈ 0.0, whereas the original test used a quadratic body (x²+y²+z²) with laplacian ≈ 6.0. Either:
   a. Add a quadratic-body variant to `eval_field_op` (e.g. `eval_field_op_quadratic`), or
   b. Add a separate focused test that manually builds a quadratic dimensioned field and asserts both `dimension == Temperature/Length²` and `si_value ≈ 6.0`.
   The existing `run_laplacian_quadratic_test` covers the dimensionless ≈6.0 case but not the dimensioned case.

Memory hints:
- search "field_calculus_tests eval_field_op test helper" for helper context
- search "Task 1291 review suggestions numerical assertion" for origin
- search "run_laplacian_quadratic_test" for the existing quadratic test

tasks.title: Add Orientation x and y boundary iteration-order tests
tasks.description:
The Orientation Ord impl chains w → x → y → z via total_cmp. After task 1452, only w and z have dedicated boundary iteration-order tests. Add value_set_orientation_boundary_x_iteration_order and value_set_orientation_boundary_y_iteration_order tests to fully cover all four component fallthroughs.

Follow the same pattern as the existing w/z tests in crates/reify-types/src/value.rs:
- x test: orient(0.0, v, 0.0, 0.0) — fixes w=0.0, y=0.0, z=0.0, varies x
- y test: orient(0.0, 0.0, v, 0.0) — fixes w=0.0, x=0.0, z=0.0, varies y

Use BOUNDARY_REALS for the values and assert_ieee754_total_order_real(&sorted) for the assertion.

tasks.title: Add Orientation x and y boundary iteration-order tests
tasks.description:
The Orientation Ord impl chains w → x → y → z via total_cmp. After task 1452, only w and z have dedicated boundary iteration-order tests. Add value_set_orientation_boundary_x_iteration_order and value_set_orientation_boundary_y_iteration_order tests to fully cover all four component fallthroughs.

Follow the same pattern as the existing w/z tests in crates/reify-types/src/value.rs:
- x test: orient(0.0, v, 0.0, 0.0) — fixes w=0.0, y=0.0, z=0.0, varies x
- y test: orient(0.0, 0.0, v, 0.0) — fixes w=0.0, x=0.0, z=0.0, varies y

Use BOUNDARY_REALS for the values and assert_ieee754_total_order_real(&sorted) for the assertion.

tasks.title: Tighten M9 integration test assertions and fix consistency issues
tasks.description:
Fix 6 review suggestions from Task 220's M9 integration tests:

1. **Two-engine pattern** (m9_integration.rs:615-622): Replace two separate Engine instances (eval_engine, check_engine) with a single engine — `engine.eval()` then `engine.check()` on the same instance, matching codebase conventions.

2. **assert_ne → assert_eq Violated** (m9_integration.rs:158-167): In `constraint_def_with_determinacy_violated`, replace `assert_ne!(satisfaction, Satisfied)` with `assert_eq!(satisfaction, Violated)` to catch erroneous Indeterminate returns.

3. **assert_ne → assert_eq Violated** (m9_integration.rs:364-368): Same fix in `recursive_structure_determinacy_terminates_on_undetermined`.

4. **Weak template count assertion** (m9_integration.rs:100-103): In `ri_file_parses_and_compiles`, assert expected template count (>= 4) and/or check that specific template names (Widget, Bracket, RecursiveChain, Plate) are present.

5. **Bracket DeterminedInRange count** (m9_integration.rs:557-563): Change `bracket_dir0.len() >= 1` to `>= 2` to verify both DeterminedInRange invocations (size and length) produce results.

6. **Doc comment inaccuracy** (examples/m9_integration.ri:17-18): Update Feature G description from 'determined(span) && depth > 0' to match actual implementation ('depth > 0' guard + per-level `constraint determined(span)`).

memory_hints: search("M9 integration test assertions", project_id="reify"); get_entity("m9_integration.rs")

tasks.title: Fix stale doc counts in bridge.rs and in_process_bridge.rs
tasks.description:
Two minor documentation accuracy fixes in the LSP bridge module:

1. **bridge.rs ~line 227**: Doc comment says "All nine deserializing arms" but UNSUPPORTED_METHOD covers the catch-all arm which does *not* deserialize params — only 8 arms actually deserialize. Change to "All eight deserializing arms" or "All nine match arms" to reflect the distinction accurately.

2. **in_process_bridge.rs line 13**: Doc on `assert_malformed_params_returns_error` says "All five 'malformed params' tests share this identical assertion triple." The count is stale — more callers now exist (error_prefix_constants_match_actual_errors, downstream_ops_after_malformed_initialize_without_initialized). Drop the numeric count: "All malformed-params tests share this identical assertion triple."

Both are single-line edits. No functional changes.

tasks.title: Poison-recovery follow-up: clarify coverage-scope doc, tighten write-path test signals, cosmetic cleanup
tasks.description:
Four small post-review cleanups from Task 1402's review triage (esc-1402-234). Bundle them because they all live in the same two files.

(1) crates/reify-runtime/tests/concurrent_eval.rs:1625-1640 — the `assert_poison_recovers` doc says "This helper covers **9 of the 11**" and then lists bullets that sum to 11 (5 helper-method sites + 6 into_result sites), not 9. A reader scanning the bullets can easily conclude the list IS the 9 covered sites, with the exclusions mentioned only as an afterthought. Rewrite as two explicitly labeled groups: "Covered (9 sites): 3 helper-method read/exclusive sites (values/read, snapshot_values/read, results/exclusive) + 6 into_result/build_result_shared sites" and "Not covered (2 sites): write_values (access=write), write_snapshot_values (access=write) — see poison_evaluate tests". Or use a small markdown table.

(2) crates/reify-runtime/tests/concurrent_eval.rs:2666-2706 — the two new async tests `evaluate_emits_access_write_for_poisoned_values` and `evaluate_emits_access_write_for_poisoned_snapshot_values` call `let _ = evaluate_with_recovery(&adapter, node);` and discard the Result. If a future regression causes `evaluate()` to panic before the write-site warn fires, `catch_unwind` inside `evaluate_with_recovery` returns Err, that Err is silently discarded, and the only remaining failure signal is "missing field" — not "panic". Neighboring tests (`tracing_warn_emitted_on_poison_evaluate`, `evaluate_recovers_poisoned_results`) use `.expect(...)`. Replace both with `evaluate_with_recovery(&adapter, node).expect("evaluate() should recover from poisoned values lock");` (and the snapshot_values analogue).

(3) crates/reify-test-support/src/tracing_support.rs:1528-1529 — trailing blank line before the closing `}` of `warn_capturing_structured_fields_are_captured` (left over from removing an old `(iv)` catch_unwind block). Delete the blank line.

(4) crates/reify-runtime/tests/concurrent_eval.rs:2147 — trailing blank line before the closing `}` of the `poison_recovery_extended` module (left over from removing the three `tracing_warn_emitted_on_poison_into_result*` tests). Delete the blank line.

Verify with: `cargo test -p reify-runtime --features test-utils concurrent_eval::poison` and `cargo test -p reify-test-support --features test-utils tracing_support::tests::warn_capturing`.

tasks.title: Restore missing completion/hover/definition malformed-params test coverage in in_process_bridge.rs
tasks.description:
Restore dropped test coverage so the `error_prefix_constants_match_actual_errors` sweep test actually anchors the "all eight deserializing arms" claim that Task 1498 added to bridge.rs line 227.

**Current state** (as of commit 8a9c4cc00, after Task 1498 merged):
- `crates/reify-lsp/src/bridge.rs` `error_prefix` module defines 8 deserializing-arm constants: INITIALIZE_PARAMS, INITIALIZED_PARAMS, DID_OPEN_PARAMS, DID_CHANGE_PARAMS, DID_CLOSE_PARAMS, COMPLETION_PARAMS, HOVER_PARAMS, DEFINITION_PARAMS (plus UNSUPPORTED_METHOD for the catch-all).
- `crates/reify-lsp/tests/in_process_bridge.rs` `error_prefix_constants_match_actual_errors` (lines ~991-1028) only asserts on 5 of those 8: INITIALIZE_PARAMS, INITIALIZED_PARAMS, DID_OPEN_PARAMS, DID_CHANGE_PARAMS, DID_CLOSE_PARAMS (plus UNSUPPORTED_METHOD). COMPLETION_PARAMS / HOVER_PARAMS / DEFINITION_PARAMS are **not** verified, so the sweep test does not actually anchor the updated doc claim.
- There are also no standalone `completion_with_malformed_params_returns_error` / `hover_…` / `definition_…` tests, unlike the did_open/did_change/did_close arms.

**History**: Commits `826f5fc72` ("add malformed-params tests for completion, hover, and definition arms", +54 lines) and `2d7bfe25d` ("extend error_prefix_constants_match_actual_errors to cover all 9 constants", +18 lines) added exactly this coverage. They were dropped by merge conflict resolution in `ecb267280` which took main's version of the file wholesale.

**Scope**:
1. Restore the three assertions from `2d7bfe25d` into `error_prefix_constants_match_actual_errors` — one each for `textDocument/completion`, `textDocument/hover`, `textDocument/definition` paired with their COMPLETION_PARAMS / HOVER_PARAMS / DEFINITION_PARAMS constants. Use the existing `assert_malformed_params_returns_error` helper for consistency.
2. (Optional, desirable for symmetry with did_open/did_change/did_close) Restore the three standalone `*_with_malformed_params_returns_error` tests from `826f5fc72`. They used `initialized_lsp()` + `assert_malformed_params_returns_error` — match whatever the current helper pattern is.
3. Run `cargo test -p reify-lsp --test in_process_bridge` to verify both the sweep test and any new standalone tests pass.

**Out of scope**: do not refactor other tests, do not modify the error_prefix constants themselves (they are fine), do not touch bridge.rs.

**Review source**: reviewer_comprehensive on Task 1498, captured in escalation esc-1498-273.

memory_hints: search "assert_malformed_params_returns_error helper" and "error_prefix constants source of truth bridge" for the established patterns.

tasks.title: Add diagnostics.len() assertion to scale-factor tests
tasks.description:
Two tests in `crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs` use `diagnostics.iter().any(...)` without first asserting the expected diagnostic count:

1. `compile_geometry_op_scale_negative_factor_returns_none` (~line 4754)
2. `compile_geometry_op_scale_nan_factor_returns_none` (~line 4798)

Both should add `assert_eq!(diagnostics.len(), 1, "expected exactly one diagnostic");` before the `any()` check, matching the pattern used by other tests in the same file (e.g., line 5034-5040). This makes tests more precise by catching unexpected extra diagnostics.

tasks.title: Add _has_expr_body_empty_guard_short_circuit to self-check in test_test_helpers.sh
tasks.description:
The self-check at lines 611-619 of tests/infra/test_test_helpers.sh validates that all file-local helpers use symmetric positive `_has_` naming, but it only checks for `_has_assert_sync_ref_exists` and `_has_if_n_guard`. The newly added `_has_expr_body_empty_guard_short_circuit` helper (line 248) is not included. If someone renames or removes that helper, the self-check won't catch the inconsistency.

Fix: Add `&& grep -qE '^_has_expr_body_empty_guard_short_circuit\(\)' "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"` to the self-check condition at line 613.

tasks.title: test_npm_ci_hardening: minor consistency and comment fixes
tasks.description:
Two small cleanups in tests/infra/test_npm_ci_hardening.sh from task 1440 review:

1. **Line 351 — positional-parameter consistency:** Test 23a's exit-status assertion uses direct interpolation (`"[ '$status23a' = '0' ]"`) while the DIAGNOSTIC assertion on line 354 uses the safer positional-parameter pattern. Use `bash -c '[ "$1" = "0" ]' _ "$status23a"` for consistency and copy-paste resilience.

2. **Line 444 — comment/code mismatch:** Comment says "The explicit echo | grep" but the code on line 449 uses `printf "%s\n" "$1" | grep`. Update comment to say "The explicit printf | grep".

tasks.title: Extract parameterized helper for orientation boundary iteration-order tests

The four orientation boundary iteration-order tests (w, x, y, z) in crates/reify-types/src/value.rs are nearly identical (~25 lines each), differing only in which orient() argument receives the boundary value and which field is extracted from the match arm. tasks.title: Add non-collection sub trait-qualified access test & fix sub_member_types doc comment
tasks.description:
Two related improvements for sub_member_types in reify-compiler:

1. **Test coverage gap (from esc-1345-302 S1):** The existing `sub_member_type_resolves_without_ice` test in silent_defaults_tests.rs uses a collection sub (`sub parts : List<Inner>`), so `collection_sub_member_types` is populated and the `or_else` fallback to `sub_member_types` in expr.rs:1510-1516 is never reached. Add a companion test using a non-collection sub (e.g., `sub part : Inner` instead of `sub parts : List<Inner>`) with trait-qualified access `part.(MechTrait::diameter)`. This exercises the fallback path that was the fix's primary target.

2. **Doc comment mismatch (from esc-1345-302 S2):** scope.rs:36 says `Member types for non-collection sub-components` but entity.rs:417 populates `sub_member_types` for ALL subs (confirmed by the comment at expr.rs:1506: "sub_member_types covers ALL subs"). Update scope.rs:36 to: `/// Member types for all sub-components: sub_name → { member_name → Type }.`

memory_hints:
- search: "sub_member_types scope entity population"
- get_entity: "sub_member_types"
- search: "non-collection sub trait-qualified access fallback expr.rs"

Source: esc-1322-29 → esc-1345-302 (S1, S2)

tasks.title: Add Orientation Ord chain-precedence tests (w→x→y→z)
tasks.description:
The existing value_set_orientation_boundary_{w,x,y,z}_iteration_order tests in crates/reify-types/src/value.rs verify that each component can drive ordering when higher-priority components compare Equal, but do NOT verify the chain-precedence itself. For example, the current suite would still pass under a bogus Ord impl like `x.total_cmp().then(w).then(y).then(z)`.

Add precedence tests to pin each chain link:
- w dominates x/y/z: e.g. `assert!(orient(1.0, NAN, NAN, NAN) < orient(2.0, NEG_INFINITY, NEG_INFINITY, NEG_INFINITY))`
- x dominates y/z: similar pattern holding w equal, varying x vs. later components
- y dominates z: holding w and x equal, varying y vs. z

Use IEEE 754 totalOrder semantics (total_cmp), and prefer contrasting "large" NaN/INFINITY values in the lower-priority positions to demonstrate that the higher-priority field alone determines order.

Related: see value_set_orientation_boundary_{w,x,y,z}_iteration_order in crates/reify-types/src/value.rs (~lines 3360–3445). The Ord impl being tested chains w → x → y → z via total_cmp.

Source: review triage of task 1487 (suggestion S2 from reviewer_comprehensive).

tasks.title: Move reify-eval out of reify-test-support [dependencies] to avoid transitive build cost
tasks.description:
reify-test-support currently lists `reify-eval.workspace = true` in `[dependencies]` (crates/reify-test-support/Cargo.toml:12) purely to support the 2-line `make_engine()` helper in src/helpers.rs. Because 10 workspace crates (reify-syntax, reify-compiler, reify-expr, reify-constraints, reify-geometry, reify-kernel-occt, reify-lsp, reify-runtime, reify-stdlib, reify-eval itself) use reify-test-support as a dev-dependency, every one of them now transitively compiles reify-eval before running tests — even the ones that never use an Engine. The other three pipeline helpers (parse_and_compile, parse_and_compile_with_stdlib, parse_compile_expect_err) only need reify-syntax + reify-compiler, which are already available.

Fix options (pick one):
(a) Make `reify-eval` an optional dependency gated by an `eval-helpers` feature and put `make_engine` behind `#[cfg(feature = "eval-helpers")]`. Opt-in for consumers that need it (reify-eval tests, reify-constraints, reify-kernel-occt).
(b) Move `make_engine` into reify-eval itself under a `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-helpers"))]` module, and delete it from reify-test-support entirely.
(c) Create a tiny `reify-eval-test-support` crate that depends on reify-eval + reify-test-support; consumers that need an Engine depend on it instead.

Option (a) is the smallest diff: add `[features] default = []; eval-helpers = ["dep:reify-eval"]`, flip the dep to `optional = true`, and add the cfg gate to make_engine + its self-test. Consumers that want the helper add `reify-test-support = { workspace = true, features = ["eval-helpers"] }` in their dev-deps — that's currently reify-eval, reify-constraints (if it uses it), reify-kernel-occt (if it uses it). A quick grep for `make_engine` will find the call sites.

**Priority:** low. Build-time optimization, not correctness — but affects the entire workspace's incremental test build graph.

**Source:** review of task 1332 (esc-1332-348, reviewer_comprehensive suggestion 1, architecture_and_design).

queries: resolution for: Move reify-eval out of reify-test-support [dependencies] to avoid transitive build cost

tasks.title: Migrate reify-compiler tests to shared parse_and_compile helper
tasks.description:
Two local copies of `fn parse_and_compile` remain after task 1332's consolidation:
- crates/reify-compiler/tests/unit_registry_tests.rs:14 — uses `ModulePath::single("unit_test")`
- crates/reify-compiler/tests/type_alias_compile_tests.rs:12 — uses `ModulePath::single("alias_test")`

reify-compiler already has `reify-test-support` as a dev-dependency (Cargo.toml:13), so the migration is just a use-site swap.

**Important behavior difference:** The shared `reify_test_support::parse_and_compile` asserts zero error-severity diagnostics, but the local copies in these two files return the raw `CompiledModule` without filtering. The existing callers use a separate `errors_only(module)` helper to inspect diagnostics, and some tests intentionally compile source that produces errors (e.g., cycle detection, unknown aliases). Those tests must NOT use the asserting shared helper.

Recommended approach:
1. Find all call sites in each file.
2. For tests that expect success, migrate to `reify_test_support::parse_and_compile`.
3. For tests that expect errors, use `reify_test_support::parse_compile_expect_err(source, needle)` or keep a local `parse_no_assert` helper that just calls `reify_syntax::parse` + `compile` without the assert.
4. Remove the local `parse_and_compile` fn once all call sites are migrated.
5. Verify no test depends on the `"unit_test"` or `"alias_test"` module-path strings (grep those literals).

**Priority:** low. Code-reuse / drift-prevention.

**Source:** review of task 1332 (esc-1332-348, reviewer_comprehensive suggestion 4, code_reuse).

SUBSUMED by task 401 (bundle consolidation 2026-04-11).

tasks.title: Tighten self-tests and remove duplication in reify-test-support helpers.rs
tasks.description:
Four minor quality issues in `crates/reify-test-support/src/helpers.rs` (all introduced or touched by task 1332):

1. **Extract private `parse_or_panic` (lines 19-36, 46-63, 72-96).** The three public helpers `parse_and_compile`, `parse_and_compile_with_stdlib`, and `parse_compile_expect_err` each inline the same 6-line parse-and-assert prologue. Extract `fn parse_or_panic(source: &str) -> reify_syntax::ParsedModule` and reuse it.

2. **`test_parse_compile_expect_err_needle_match` (lines 125-131) uses `"unknown"` as the needle.** That's ambiguous — the source contains `let x = unknown_variable`, so either the error message or the echoed identifier can satisfy the match. Use the full identifier `"unknown_variable"` (guaranteed to appear because we control the source) or assert against a diagnostic code/kind.

3. **`test_parse_and_compile_with_stdlib` (lines 134-148) couples to stdlib Material shape.** The test passes stdlib-specific source (`structure Steel : Material { param density: Real = 7850 … }`) and breaks if Material is renamed or its fields change, even when the helper itself is correct. Replace with the minimum source that unambiguously proves stdlib was loaded — e.g., reference a well-known stdlib primitive and assert its presence in `compiled.types`/`templates`, and optionally add a negative-control assertion that the same source produces an error under plain `parse_and_compile` (no stdlib).

4. **`test_make_engine` (lines 103-112) uses geometry-bearing source.** `bracket_source` contains `let body = box(width, height, thickness)` — a geometry call — and `make_engine()` has no kernel, so the eval produces diagnostics and only the scalar params survive. The `!result.values.is_empty()` assertion technically passes but doesn't prove the engine is wired correctly. Replace with a geometry-free source (e.g., `structure Foo { param x: Real = 42 }`) and assert the specific `ValueCellId` resolves to the expected `Value`.

All four items are in the same ~170-line file, so they can be addressed in a single small PR.

**Priority:** low. Test-quality / brittleness cleanup, not correctness.

**Source:** review of task 1332 (esc-1332-348, reviewer_comprehensive suggestions 2, 3, 5, 7).

queries: resolution for: Tighten self-tests and remove duplication in reify-test-support helpers.rs

tasks.title: Audit and fix expand_task/parse_prd deduplication logic
tasks.description: The expand_task and parse_prd tools generate new tasks without checking for existing tasks with identical or near-identical titles. This has caused confirmed duplicate task generation across multiple reconciliation cycles (e.g., tasks 1535, 1537, and 1574 were all identical). Fix the deduplication logic so that before generating a new task, the system checks for existing tasks (including completed/cancelled ones) with the same title or semantically equivalent content.
tasks.details:
Root cause: expand_task/parse_prd do not perform deduplication checks against existing tasks before creating new ones. This is documented across ≥2 reconciliation cycles (mem0 IDs: 8e68940b-b8f7-428b-8034-5409b459c10c, 246423bd).

Steps:
1. Locate the expand_task and parse_prd tool implementations in the fused-memory MCP server.
2. Add a pre-creation check that queries existing tasks (all statuses) for title/description similarity.
3. If a duplicate is found, skip creation and optionally log the dedup event.
4. Consider semantic similarity (not just exact match) to catch near-duplicate phrasings.
5. Write a regression test that verifies re-running expand_task on an already-expanded task does not create duplicates.

tasks.title: Support let-bound geometry variables as boolean op arguments
tasks.description:
The compiler's `compile_geometry_call` in `crates/reify-compiler/src/geometry.rs` only accepts inline geometry function calls as arguments to `difference()`, `union()`, and `intersection()`. It does not resolve `let`-bound geometry variable references, forcing users to inline all geometry expressions.

Currently fails:
```
let body = cylinder(outer_radius, height)
let hole = cylinder(hole_radius, height)
let result = difference(body, holes)  // ERROR: argument 1 must be a geometry expression
```

Currently works (but unreadable for complex models):
```
let result = difference(cylinder(outer_radius, height), cylinder(hole_radius, height))
```

This also blocks `circular_pattern` + `difference` compositions needed for the flange example (`examples/m5_geometry_flange.ri`).
tasks.details:
## Root cause

In `geometry.rs:54-74`, when `compile_geometry_call` encounters a boolean op argument, it calls itself recursively on the argument AST node. If the argument is an `ExprKind::Identifier` (a variable reference), the recursive call fails because identifiers aren't geometry function calls. The fix is to resolve identifiers by looking up the `let` binding in scope and recursively compiling its initializer expression.

## Implementation approach

1. In `compile_geometry_call`, when an argument is an `ExprKind::Identifier`, look it up in `scope` to find the `let` binding's initializer expression
2. Recursively call `compile_geometry_call` on the initializer
3. Handle the case where the binding's initializer is itself a variable reference (transitive resolution)
4. Same fix needed for `circular_pattern`'s target argument (line 273+)

## Files to modify
- `crates/reify-compiler/src/geometry.rs` — main fix
- `crates/reify-compiler/tests/let_scope_tests.rs` — add tests for variable-ref boolean ops

## memory_hints
- search: "compile_geometry_call boolean op arguments"
- search: "geometry variable reference let binding"
- entity: "compile_geometry_call"

In crates/reify-eval/tests/collection_sub_eval.rs, `from_templates_creates_collection_instances` (lines 54-98) still inlines the same Bolt+Parent template construction that `make_bolt_parent_engine` builds. tasks.title: collection_sub_eval: tighten Int→Undef→Undef→Int transition test with intermediate assertions and behavior-focused comments
tasks.description:
Test-quality follow-ups from task 958 review (reviewer_comprehensive) for `edit_param_count_int_undef_undef_int_transition` in crates/reify-eval/tests/collection_sub_eval.rs (~lines 440-485):

1. **Intermediate assertions.** The test only checks the final state after the full Int(4)→Undef→Undef→Int(2) sequence. A regression of step 1 or step 2 could be silently masked by step 3 overwriting bolts[0..2). Capture each EvalResult:
   - `let r1 = engine.edit_param(n_id.clone(), Value::Undef)?;` — assert bolts[0..4) are absent in `r1.values`.
   - `let r2 = engine.edit_param(n_id.clone(), Value::Undef)?;` — assert bolts[0..4) are absent in `r2.values`.
   - Then proceed to Int(2) and assert the existing final state.

2. **Behavior-focused comments.** The current comments tie the test to internal implementation details (`new_count=0 via `_ => 0``, `short-circuits via equality check`). Rephrase to describe observable contract instead:
   - `// Int(4) → Undef must clear all bolt instances`
   - `// Undef → Undef must be a no-op`
   - `// Undef → Int(2) must elaborate exactly bolts[0..2)`

These changes improve regression diagnosability and decouple the test from internal renames.

tasks.title: Clean up stale `TODO(task-2)` references in completion.rs
tasks.description:
Eight TODO comments in `crates/reify-lsp/src/completion.rs` (lines 759, 787, 821, 837, 887, 926, 1502, 1530) reference `task-2`, but task #2 is the OCCT FFI binding task — not a completion task. The actual position-sensitive completion tracking tasks are #481 (test-first) and #482 (implementation), both already DONE: `CursorContext`, `determine_context`, and the narrowing logic are all implemented in completion.rs and used by `compute_completions`.

Each TODO says something like "When position-sensitive filtering lands, verify these assertions still hold in StructureBody/Expression context." Since position-sensitive filtering HAS landed and tests are still passing at the listed positions (Position(1,0) is inside structure body, Position(7,17) is Expression context), the requested verification is implicitly complete.

Action: remove the 8 stale `TODO(task-2)` comments from `crates/reify-lsp/src/completion.rs`. Spot-check that each test's position is consistent with the narrowing predicted by `determine_context` so removal is safe.

tasks.title: Cleanup: remove redundant reify-types dev-dep and split pub use in reify-types lib.rs
tasks.description:
Two minor cleanups from review of task 883 (DiagnosticInfo/SourceLocationInfo move to reify-types):

1. `crates/reify-mcp/Cargo.toml:18` — `reify-types = { workspace = true, features = ["serde"] }` appears in BOTH `[dependencies]` and `[dev-dependencies]` with identical feature flags. Cargo makes `[dependencies]` available to integration tests, so the dev-dep entry is redundant. Delete the line from `[dev-dependencies]`. `tests/registry_tests.rs` will continue to reference `reify_types::DiagnosticInfo` via the regular dependency. Reduces drift risk if feature flags ever diverge.

2. `crates/reify-types/src/lib.rs:22` — the `pub use diagnostics::{Diagnostic, DiagnosticInfo, DiagnosticLabel, DiagnosticRef, Severity, SourceSpan};` line now exceeds the historical wrapping width and mixes the structural `Diagnostic*` types with the presentation `DiagnosticInfo` type. Optionally split into two `pub use` lines so the presentation type's separate role is visible at the crate root:
   - `pub use diagnostics::{Diagnostic, DiagnosticLabel, DiagnosticRef, Severity, SourceSpan};`
   - `pub use diagnostics::DiagnosticInfo;`

Verify: `cargo check -p reify-mcp --all-targets` and `cargo test -p reify-mcp`.

Those pointers risk going stale once the plan document is merged or removed, leaving future readers (or a CI that runs `--ignored` and flips the test) without a usable breadcrumb.

Fix: replace the plan-step reference with either (a) a one-line summary of the failure mode inlined into the reason string (e.g. "expected Scalar{1/Length} from dim_quotient_type cd==DIMENSIONLESS branch"), or (b) a tracked task/issue ID that will outlive the plan file. Option (a) is simpler and self-contained; the test docstrings above already contain the full analysis, so the reason string only needs a brief pointer.

tasks.title: Stamp field-op lambda bodies with codomain-consistent component types in eval_field_op
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1263 review triage (esc-1263-61, S2).

In crates/reify-expr/tests/field_calculus_tests.rs, the test helper `eval_field_op` (around lines 693–715) unconditionally builds the lambda body with `Type::Real`-typed `value_ref`s. For `divergence_sample_mixed_real_to_velocity_returns_scalar` (lines ~1886–1896, 2159–2169 for the lap counterpart), the declared codomain is `Vec3(Scalar<Velocity>)` but the lambda body is still `vec3(x,y,z)` with Real components. The regression guard passes only because the numerical differentiation path is loose about component types — it does not type-check that the lambda body's return type matches the field's declared codomain component type.

This couples the test to an evaluator quirk: a future tightening of type-checking in `vec3`/`sample` could break the regression guard for reasons unrelated to the dimensional-quotient logic it is meant to guard.

Fix options:
1. Preferred: extend `eval_field_op` (and the helpers it calls) to stamp the lambda body with codomain-consistent component types — parameterize the body construction on the codomain type and generate `value_ref`s with the matching component type. Then the Case B tests exercise the real invariant.
2. Fallback: add a short comment on the Case B tests noting the known type looseness so a future refactor is less surprising, and cross-reference this task.

Modules: crates/reify-expr/tests

tasks.title: Harden task 1597 meta-assertions against silent-pass failures
tasks.description: Two meta-assertions added to tests/infra/test_portable_timeout.sh on task 1597 silently pass under partial regression or on macOS BSD grep. (1) Assertion at ~line 764 uses `grep -c 'exit 2' -ge 2`, but the grep command line self-matches giving a baseline of 3. Reverting one of the two sanity-check `exit 2` lines to `exit 1` leaves count=2 and the assertion still passes. Fix: replace with two independent `grep -qF` assertions, one per sanity-check line (structural, not count-based). (2) Negative assertion at ~line 755 uses `grep -qE '^\s+# Uses the grep -cF <<< idiom'`. `\s` is a GNU grep / PCRE extension; BSD ERE (macOS default) does not interpret it as whitespace class, so on macOS the pattern never matches and the negative assertion trivially passes even if the stale cross-reference is reintroduced. Fix: replace `^\s+` with `^[[:space:]]+` to match the convention used elsewhere in the file.

Task 1619 left this untouched as out-of-scope. Simple cleanup: delete the block entirely or replace with a brief accurate comment.

tasks.title: Refactor compile_geometry_op rejection tests: helper + NaN assert + revolve dedup
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 1213 reviewer_comprehensive. Three related improvements to crates/reify-eval/tests/geometry_error_handling.rs, all touching the four `build_*_emits_diagnostic` tests added in task 1213.

S1 (code_duplication, ~520 lines in the four new tests): Extract a private helper `fn assert_rejected_at_compile(result: &BuildResult, ops: &[GeometryOpRecord], expected_primitive: fn(&GeometryOp) -> bool, warning_needles: &[&str])` that performs the shared 5-assertion block (kernel-ops count + primitive-kind match, geometry_output is None, specific Warning, compile Error, absence of 'geometry error' diagnostic). Rewrite each of the four tests to call the helper — ~30 lines of setup + one helper call. See review at crates/reify-eval/tests/geometry_error_handling.rs:1215-1759.

S2 (test_specificity): `build_extrude_nonfinite_distance_emits_diagnostic` currently asserts only 'extrude dropped' + 'degenerate' in the warning message, but both the non-finite arm and the near-zero arm of the Extrude check (lib.rs:3667-3678) share the same Some(v) branch and emit the same warning template. A regression swapping `v.is_finite()` for `true` would not be caught, since the sibling zero-distance test in stress_sweep_degenerate.rs would still pass. Add `.contains("NaN")` (Rust's Display format for f64::NAN) to the Warning assertion in this one test to pin the non-finite arm specifically. See review at crates/reify-eval/tests/geometry_error_handling.rs:1321-1396.

S3 (duplicate_coverage): `build_revolve_zero_angle_emits_diagnostic` in geometry_error_handling.rs intentionally duplicates `revolve_zero_angle` in stress_sweep_degenerate.rs. Both exercise the same code path (Sweep::Revolve zero-angle guard at lib.rs:3710). The new test has stricter assertions (ops_len==1, no 'geometry error') but the real guard coverage is identical. Decision: delete `revolve_zero_angle` from stress_sweep_degenerate.rs so `geometry_error_handling::build_revolve_zero_angle_emits_diagnostic` becomes the canonical angle-guard test. Confirm no other sibling stress tests depend on it.

All three changes land in the same file (plus one deletion in stress_sweep_degenerate.rs) and form one cohesive TDD cycle. Memory hints: entity "compile_geometry_op", search "geometry_error_handling helper pattern".

tasks.title: Change Value::Field::lambda from Box<Value> to Arc<Value> to avoid deep-cloning source field in differential operators
tasks.description:
In crates/reify-types/src/value.rs (~line 164), Value::Field::lambda is currently Box<Value>. In crates/reify-expr/src/calculus.rs, compute_gradient (~line 225), compute_divergence (~line 294), compute_curl (~line 365), and compute_laplacian (~line 423) each do `lambda: Box::new(field_val.clone())`, which deep-clones the entire source field (including its own inner lambda, captures, etc.) on every gradient/divergence/curl/laplacian call. Replacing Box<Value> with Arc<Value> would make these clones O(1) refcount bumps.

Scope: cross-crate type change. All constructors and destructurings of Value::Field need updating in: crates/reify-types/src, crates/reify-expr/src, crates/reify-eval/src, crates/reify-stdlib/src, crates/reify-test-support/src, gui/src-tauri/src, plus test code in crates/reify-expr/tests. Watch for derive impls (PartialEq, Clone, Debug, Ord, Hash) which should work transparently with Arc.

Verification: cargo test -p reify-expr after the type change, plus full workspace build. Benchmark gradient sampling if a bench exists.

Context: this is the one remaining unresolved item from task 412. Items (2) coords cloning and (3) captures.clone() have already been fixed — coords via scratch buffers + std::mem::take in eval_perturbed_point, captures via ValueMap's im::HashMap backing.

queries: resolution for: Change Value::Field::lambda from Box<Value> to Arc<Value> to avoid deep-cloning source field in differential operators

tasks.title: field_calculus_tests: tighten MAX_POINT_ARITY invariant and clean panic formatting
tasks.description: Follow-up from review of task 1466. Three related changes in crates/reify-expr/tests/field_calculus_tests.rs.
tasks.details:
In crates/reify-expr/tests/field_calculus_tests.rs:

1. Replace the MAX_POINT_ARITY const (line 628-631) with array-derived constants so raising the cap is a one-edit change that automatically grows the hard-coded arrays:
   const COORDS: [f64; 3] = [1.0, 2.0, 3.0];
   const NAMES: [&str; 3] = ["x", "y", "z"];
   const MAX_POINT_ARITY: usize = COORDS.len();
   Then use COORDS/NAMES in make_sample_point (line 648) and eval_field_op (line 702) instead of the inline literals. Remove or update the stale doc comment that claims changing the cap auto-syncs assert messages — the new formulation makes the dependency structural.

2. Delete the two guard-on-guard #[should_panic] tests: make_sample_point_panics_when_point_arity_exceeds_three (lines 674-679) and eval_field_op_panics_when_point_arity_exceeds_three (lines 748-753). They add no production-behavior coverage and impose lockstep maintenance cost. If MAX_POINT_ARITY becomes array-derived per item 1, the asserts themselves become structurally redundant too — consider removing the asserts along with the tests, letting the array slice bound check be the single source of truth (optional).

3. Change the two panic! calls at lines 657 and 670 to use captured-identifier format strings consistent with the rest of the file:
   panic!("make_sample_point: unsupported quantity type in Point: {other:?}")
   panic!("make_sample_point: unsupported domain type: {other:?}")

Source: review follow-up from task 1466 (escalation esc-1466-6).
