tasks.title: si_units: SolidAngle/sr + prefix-combo cleanups
tasks.description: Review follow-up from task 334 (escalation esc-334-7). Three related table-edits in crates/reify-compiler/src/si_units.rs.
tasks.details:
Three related changes, all table-edits in crates/reify-compiler/src/si_units.rs with matching test updates in crates/reify-compiler/tests/si_units_tests.rs:

**S1 — Add sr (steradian) for SolidAngle:**
Currently the error message in type_resolution.rs lists SolidAngle as a valid dimension, but there is no `sr` unit declared anywhere in the stdlib, so `param x : SolidAngle = 1sr` errors with 'unknown unit'. Fix:
- Add `SiDerivedUnit { name: "sr", dimension: "SolidAngle", factor: 1.0, prefix_combos: &[] }` to SI_DERIVED_UNITS.
- Bump the expected count in `si_units_tests.rs::si_derived_units_table_defines_newtons_pascals_joules` from 24 to 25.
- Remove or update the NOTE comment (~line 237-242) that rationalizes this as out-of-scope.

**S4 — Per-base prefix subsets for SI_PREFIX_BASES:**
SI_PREFIX_BASES currently has no prefix_combos, so the generator applies all 20 prefixes to every base (K, cd, rad, ...), producing 159+ nonsensical entries like QK (quettakelvin), qcd (quectocandela), Qrad, qrad. The SI_DERIVED_UNITS comment explicitly rejects this 'every prefix × every unit' pattern. Fix:
- Give SI_PREFIX_BASES entries their own `prefix_combos: &[&str]` field mirroring SI_DERIVED_UNITS.
- Suggested subsets: K → [n, u, m]; cd → [m, u]; rad → [m, u, n]. (Final choice is reviewer's call.)

**S5 — Extend F/S prefix_combos for RF/IC engineering range:**
F (farad) at si_units.rs:116 has `["p", "n", "u", "m"]` — missing femtofarads (fF), which are standard in RF/IC design. S (siemens) at ~:96 omits both `p` and `f` variants.
- Add `"f"` to F's prefix_combos (and optionally `"a"`).
- Add `"p"` and `"f"` to S's prefix_combos.
- Update any affected test count assertions in si_units_tests.rs.

queries: SI units table SI_DERIVED_UNITS SI_PREFIX_BASES steradian SolidAngle prefix_combos crates/reify-compiler/src/si_units.rs
task 334 SI derived units prefix table implementation done

tasks.title: si_units: format_f64_as_decimal hardening
tasks.description: Review follow-up from task 334 (escalation esc-334-7). Two related fixes to format_f64_as_decimal.
tasks.details:
Both changes target the same function `format_f64_as_decimal` in crates/reify-compiler/src/si_units.rs and its call-sites in stdlib_loader.rs. Unified error-handling refactor is cleaner than two overlapping patches.

**S3 — Guard large-magnitude inputs:**
format_f64_as_decimal (lines ~368-395) clamps precision to 0 via `(17-log-1).max(0)`, so for inputs with `log >= 17` (e.g. 1e18, 1e30) it emits the full decimal of the actual stored f64 — e.g. `1000000000000000019884624838656` for 1e30. Currently masked because SI_DERIVED_UNITS factors stay under 1e17, but SI_PREFIX_BASES-generated power-of-ten factors at Q/R/Y/Z scale should route through format_power_of_ten.
- Option A: Add `debug_assert!(log < 16, "format_f64_as_decimal large-value path untested for log={}", log);`.
- Option B: Route power-of-ten inputs through format_power_of_ten even when coming from the derived-unit path.
(B is more robust.)

**S6 — Surface bad factors as compile errors, not process panics:**
Line ~369 uses `assert!` (not `debug_assert!`) at stdlib-init time inside OnceLock::get_or_init. If a non-finite or non-positive factor ever sneaks into SI_DERIVED_UNITS, every consumer of the compiler panics with no recovery path. A unit-registration failure should be an internal compile error on the synthetic `std.si_units` module. Aligns with the project's 'no silent defaults' norm.
- Change format_f64_as_decimal signature to `Result<String, &'static str>` (or a richer error type).
- Bubble errors up through stdlib_loader.rs to a `Diagnostic::error` attached to the synthetic std.si_units module.
- Alternatively, strengthen the invariant by making `SiDerivedUnit.factor` a newtype that rejects non-positive values at construction.

queries: format_f64_as_decimal si_units.rs large magnitude guard power of ten
stdlib_loader.rs SI unit registration error handling panic assert

tasks.title: Broaden Check 4 regex in test_pm_standardization.sh to accept leading-slash gitignore forms
tasks.description:
Check 4 in scripts/test_pm_standardization.sh (lines 101-103) enforces `(^\*\*/|^gui/)pnpm-lock\.yaml$`, which rejects the canonical leading-slash gitignore form `/gui/pnpm-lock.yaml`. A future editor who writes that form in .gitignore would hit an unexpected lint failure even though it's valid syntax.

Fix: either widen the regex to `(^/?\*\*/|^/?gui/)pnpm-lock\.yaml/?$` (also accepts trailing slash), or explicitly document the two strict accepted forms in the Check 4 comment so the constraint is predictable for future editors. Preferred: widen the regex for robustness, and update the comment to enumerate accepted forms.

tasks.title: Harden meta-test `ignore_reason_strings_have_no_stale_plan_pointers` in field_calculus_tests.rs
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1622 review triage (esc-1622-16, S1+S2+S5).

Three related gaps in the meta-test `ignore_reason_strings_have_no_stale_plan_pointers` at crates/reify-expr/tests/field_calculus_tests.rs:~3906-3962:

1. **Docstring overstates positive-invariant coverage (S1, line ~3915-3917).** The docstring claims `starts_with("known bug:")` "catches stale pointers of any form (`see plan.md`, `step-3 of plan`, `analysis in step-7`, …)". That is only true when the stale pointer REPLACES the `known bug:` prefix. A reason like `#[ignore = "known bug: see plan.md step-3"]` passes the positive check and would only be caught by the sentinel if it happened to contain the literal `plan step-` substring. Variants like `plan-step`, `plan.md`, `step-3 of plan`, or `analysis in step-7` slip past both guards.
   Fix: either (a) tighten the docstring to say honestly "rejects reasons that don't start with `known bug:`, plus a sentinel for the original `plan step-` substring; other stale-pointer wordings inside compliant prefixes are not caught", or (b) strengthen the negative check to a small regex set like `(?i)(plan[\s\-_.]?step|plan[\s\-_.]?doc|see\s+(plan|step)|analysis in step)`.

2. **Bare `#[ignore]` silently exempt (S2, line ~3935-3948).** The walk loop only inspects what follows `#[ignore = "`. A bare `#[ignore]` (no reason string) bypasses the check entirely, even though the docstring says "every #[ignore] reason string". If someone drops the reason string, the guard will not flag it and the convention silently regresses.
   Fix: either explicitly forbid bare `#[ignore]` in this file (assert zero matches of `#[ignore]` not followed by ` = "`), or narrow the docstring to "every `#[ignore = "..."]` reason string".

3. **Runtime-assembled marker is obscure (S5, line ~3935).** The clever `["#[ignore", " = \""].concat()` and `["plan", " step-"].concat()` are easy to accidentally inline back, which would cause the meta-test to self-trigger and fail confusingly. The function docstring mentions this but there is no inline warning at the assembly site.
   Fix: add a brief inline comment at both concat sites like `// DO NOT inline — must avoid literal #[ignore = " appearing in this source file or this test will match itself`.

All three fixes touch the same function and are small. Keep the scope narrow: only the meta-test, no behavioral changes to the placeholder tests.

memory_hints:
- Task 1622 introduced this meta-test; see its commits (5f0db11ae, 8219f5c91) for rationale.
- The convention is "#[ignore] reasons in this file must begin with `known bug:`".

tasks.title: Promote stale-plan-pointer #[ignore] reason check to workspace-wide scope
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1622 review triage (esc-1622-16, S3).

The meta-test `ignore_reason_strings_have_no_stale_plan_pointers` in crates/reify-expr/tests/field_calculus_tests.rs only protects that one file. The convention "reason strings should not point at transient plan docs" is presumably repo-wide, but a sibling test file with `#[ignore = "see plan step-7"]` in any other crate would not be caught.

Fix: promote the check to a workspace-wide guard. Options:
- Small `xtask` that walks `**/tests/**/*.rs` and asserts the same convention.
- A dedicated `tests/` integration test in a workspace-scoped crate that does the walk.
- Alternatively, if workspace promotion is too heavy, add a comment on the existing narrow test acknowledging the one-file scope so future authors know to extend it when adding ignored tests elsewhere.

The reviewer (esc-1622-16, reviewer_comprehensive) explicitly noted: "No action required for Task 1622 itself" — this is pure follow-up scope expansion.

tasks.title: Tighten purpose_activation tests: rename S1, fix S2 comment, S3/S5 exact asserts, add S4 remap_entity coverage
tasks.description: Five test-quality improvements to crates/reify-eval/tests/purpose_activation.rs from Task 260 review (reviewer_comprehensive). All targeting the same file.
tasks.details:
Follow-up bundle from Task 260 review suggestions (esc-260-17). All changes live in a single file.

**S1 — `compiler_always_emits_params_query_per_structure_purpose_param` overstates behavior (line 226/280)**
In `crates/reify-compiler/src/traits.rs:345`, the resolved query is only pushed when `!param_ids.is_empty()`. A struct with zero params would NOT emit a query, so the test name lies. Fix: either rename (e.g. `compiler_emits_params_query_when_structure_has_params`) OR add a complementary `compiler_omits_params_query_for_zero_param_structure` case that constructs a param-less structure and asserts `resolved_queries.len() == 0`. Prefer adding the complementary case so the empty branch is covered.

**S2 — Incorrect comment at purpose_activation.rs:498-500**
Comment claims `make_engine()` uses `MockConstraintChecker` that returns `Unchecked`. But `MockConstraintChecker::new()` defaults to `Satisfaction::Satisfied` (crates/reify-test-support/src/mocks.rs:48). So the stated rationale for switching to `SimpleConstraintChecker` is wrong. Fix: verify whether the test actually passes with `make_engine()`. If yes, drop the manual `Engine::new(...)` and use `make_engine()`. If `SimpleConstraintChecker` is required for real dimensional checks, rewrite the comment with the correct reason.

**S3 — `injected_constraint_ids_have_purpose_prefix` weak assertion (lines 215-224/253-270)**
`crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs:433` constructs each injected entity as exactly `format!("purpose:{}@{}", purpose_name, entity_ref)`. Every injected id's entity should equal `"purpose:mfg_ready@Bracket"` — but the test uses `any` + `starts_with`, which is looser than the contract. Fix:
```rust
let entities: Vec<&str> = snapshot.graph.constraints.keys()
    .filter(|id| id.entity.starts_with("purpose:"))
    .map(|id| id.entity.as_str())
    .collect();
assert!(!entities.is_empty(), "expected at least one injected constraint");
assert!(entities.iter().all(|e| *e == "purpose:mfg_ready@Bracket"),
    "all purpose-injected constraints should have entity 'purpose:mfg_ready@Bracket'; got: {:?}", entities);
```

**S4 — Coverage regression: `expr.remap_entity` path not exercised (lines 78-94)**
The deleted `purpose_eval.rs::activate_purpose_with_minimize_objective` used `minimize subject + subject`, which forced `expr.remap_entity` at `crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs:440,446` to run on a non-literal expression. Every new fixture in purpose_activation.rs uses only literal expressions (`80mm + 60mm`, `5mm * 2`, `1 > 0`), so the rewrite path is uncovered. S5 note (line 620-625) explains why `subject.<param>` member access can't be tested yet — but bare `subject` (the pattern the deleted test used) stays within current compiler limits. Fix: add a test with fixture `purpose p(subject: Structure) { minimize subject + subject }`, activate against Bracket, and assert the resulting objective's expression has been remapped from the purpose name to the bound entity (inspect via `engine.active_objectives()` and check the inner expression's entity references equal `Bracket`).

**S5 — Weak `>=` counts on example-file tests (lines 485-494)**
`m10_purpose_activation_example_parses_and_compiles` asserts `>= 5` purposes and `m10_purpose_activation_example_constraints_satisfied` asserts `>= 8` constraint results. The example declares exactly 5 purposes and exactly 8 structural constraints on Bracket. Fix: change both to `==` so drift in the example file (accidental stray purpose/constraint) gets caught. Also consider asserting that `names` (after the named-purpose loop) has no duplicates.

**Verification**: `cargo test -p reify-eval --test purpose_activation` should pass all tests after changes.

**Scope**: Only modify `crates/reify-eval/tests/purpose_activation.rs`. No changes to production code.

queries: resolution for: Tighten purpose_activation tests: rename S1, fix S2 comment, S3/S5 exact asserts, add S4 remap_entity coverage

tasks.title: Imperial units tests: add `in` guard, cross-relationship tests, helper, tighter tolerances
tasks.description: Follow-up quality improvements to crates/reify-compiler/tests/imperial_units_tests.rs (from task 335 review). Four related test-quality changes in one file.
tasks.details:
Review of task 335 surfaced four improvements to imperial_units_tests.rs, all small and co-located:

1. **Regression guard for `in`** (test:374): `existing_imperial_units_ft_thou_lb_oz_unchanged_post_task_335` omits `in` (inch, 0.0254 m), even though the task touched that line. Add `("1in", 0.0254)` to `length_cases` and `"in"` to the offset-check loop. The literal `1in` parses fine in source position.

2. **Cross-relationship tests** (test:115-272): New units are verified only in isolation against hand-typed constants that mirror units.ri verbatim (LBF_SI, PSI_SI, KSI_SI, FL_OZ_SI, GAL_SI). A typo copied to both files would pass. Add a test that uses both forms and asserts equality: `1yd == 3ft`, `1ksi == 1000 psi`, `1gal == 128 fl_oz`, `1psi == 1 lbf / (1 in * 1 in)`. This locks dimensional relationships, not just magic numbers.

3. **Helper extraction** (test:79-110,135-165,200-230,285-315): The `units_module().units.iter().find(|u| u.name == X).expect(...)` pattern is repeated 7+ times with identical dimension/factor/offset assertions. Add a private helper `fn assert_simple_unit(name: &str, expected_dim: DimensionVector, expected_factor: f64, rel_tol: f64)` that centralises the offset=None invariant. Leave the parse→compile `_resolves_to_*` tests separate since they cover a different code path.

4. **Tighten psi/ksi tolerances** (test:163): `stdlib_psi_resolves_to_pressure_*` uses `PSI_SI * 1e-9` (≈ 6.9e-6 absolute). The constant in units.ri is the f64 round-trip of the exact value, so `1e-12` absolute (matching yd/ft/thou) would still pass and tighten the net by ~6 orders of magnitude. Same for ksi.

## Test plan
- Add `in` case to length_cases; verify offset-check loop still green.
- Add a new `#[test] cross_relationships_between_imperial_units()` that asserts the four equalities above within 1e-12 relative tolerance of the larger value.
- Extract `assert_simple_unit` helper and rewrite `stdlib_units_module_contains_*` tests to use it.
- Change psi/ksi tests to `1e-12` absolute tolerance.
- `cargo test -p reify-compiler --test imperial_units_tests` green.

Spawned from: task 335 review (esc-335-19).

tasks.title: Stdlib Volume: reconcile units.ri comment with si_units.rs (add L/m³ or rewrite comment)
tasks.description: Task 335 added fl_oz/gal to stdlib/units.ri with a comment claiming "SI litre and prefixed cubic-metre variants are generated by si_units.rs", but that is not true.
tasks.details:
In crates/reify-compiler/stdlib/units.ri:55-56, the Volume section header says:
    // SI litre and prefixed cubic-metre variants are generated by si_units.rs.

However, crates/reify-compiler/src/si_units.rs's `SI_DERIVED_UNITS` table contains no entry for L/litre, m³, or any Volume-dimensioned unit, and `SI_PREFIX_BASES` is just `(m, g, s, A, K, mol, cd, rad)`. After task 335, the only Volume-dimensioned units in the stdlib are `fl_oz` and `gal`. A future reader following the comment will look in si_units.rs for mL/L/m3 and be misled.

## Options (pick one)

1. **Rewrite the comment to reflect reality** — minimal change. Something like:
   "No SI litre or cubic-metre variants are currently generated; only US-customary fl_oz and gal are defined here. Add L/mL/m³ to si_units.rs when needed."

2. **Add L and m³ to si_units.rs** — makes the comment true. L would go in `SI_DERIVED_UNITS` (1 L = 1e-3 m³), and m³ prefix generation would need extending so kilo/milli/etc. prefixes on `m` produce `km³`/`mm³`/etc. (or `L` gets the standard g-style prefix treatment: mL, cL, dL, L, kL). Larger scope; only do this if a user requests SI volume literals.

**Recommendation:** Option 1 (comment rewrite) unless there is concrete demand for `1L` / `1mL` literals, in which case Option 2. Opening the task with Option 1 as a 2-line fix is the safe default.

## Test plan
- If Option 1: no new tests; just edit the comment.
- If Option 2: add tests asserting `1L == 0.001 m³`, `1mL == 1e-6 m³`, and dimension = Volume.

Spawned from: task 335 review (esc-335-19).

tasks.title: Tighten phase pre-guard comment: NaN vs Inf precision in complex.rs
tasks.description: In crates/reify-expr/src/complex.rs around line 41, the comment on the is_finite pre-guard says "sanitize_value cannot detect NaN/Inf inputs from these outputs alone". That is accurate for Inf inputs (atan2 collapses ±Inf to finite quadrant boundaries like 0, ±π, ±π/2), but NOT for NaN inputs — NaN propagates through atan2 and would be caught downstream by sanitize_value. The is_finite pre-guard is strictly necessary only for Inf; for NaN it is defense-in-depth for stylistic parity. Reword to make this precise. Suggested: "sanitize_value cannot detect Inf inputs from these outputs alone (NaN inputs would propagate through atan2 and be caught downstream, but for stylistic parity and defense-in-depth we reject both up-front)." Comment-only change, no behaviour change, no new tests needed.

tasks.title: Split phase_zero_complex_returns_undef into positive-zero and signed-zero tests
tasks.description: In crates/reify-expr/src/complex.rs around line 502, phase_zero_complex_returns_undef bundles two assertions in a single #[test]: (0.0, 0.0) and the IEEE-754 signed-zero variant (-0.0, -0.0). If the first assertion fires, the second is never run, so a regression in only the signed-zero path would be masked. Split into two independent #[test] functions — e.g. phase_zero_complex_returns_undef for (0.0, 0.0) and phase_signed_zero_complex_returns_undef for (-0.0, -0.0) — so each gives an independent failure signal. This matches the one-behavior-per-test convention already used in this file (cf. phase_inf_re_returns_undef vs phase_inf_im_returns_undef). Preserve the current explanatory comments on each test.

tasks.description: In crates/reify-expr/src/complex.rs: (1) Reword the comment at line ~41 to accurately distinguish NaN propagation (caught by sanitize_value downstream) from Inf collapse (requires the is_finite pre-guard), explaining the guard is defense-in-depth for NaN but strictly necessary for Inf. tasks.title: Investigate and implement compiler-side support for non-params reflective query filter members
tasks.description: Task 1638 completed evaluator-layer support for all five non-params filter members (geometric_params, material_params, sub_entities, ports, constraints), but reify-compiler only covers the 'params' reflective query kind. There are no references to material_params or geometric_params in crates/reify-compiler, and compiler tests cover only 'params'. This means queries using non-params filter members receive no compile-time validation — errors surface only at eval time. Investigate whether this gap is intentional (the 'geometry check deferred to eval time' pattern covers forall-variable geometry, but the scope of that deferral is unclear) or an omission. If the deferral is intentional for all non-params kinds, document the design decision. If not, implement compile-time validation for each missing kind, mirroring how 'params' is handled in crates/reify-compiler/src/traits.rs.
tasks.details:
## Investigation steps
1. Read crates/reify-compiler/src/traits.rs (and compile_reflective_query / related entry points) to understand how 'params' is validated statically.
2. Check the 'geometry check deferred to eval time' design decision (memory: "geometry check for forall variable members is deferred to eval time because the compiler can't statically determine the sub-component's geometry") — determine if this rationale applies to all non-params query kinds or just forall+geometry.
3. For each non-params kind (geometric_params, material_params, sub_entities, ports, constraints):
   - Can the compiler statically validate it? (type/membership available at compile time?)
   - If yes, implement compile-time validation and add compiler tests in crates/reify-compiler/tests/.
   - If no, add a doc comment or internal note confirming intentional deferral to eval time.
4. Update compiler tests to cover at least one case per kind that can be validated statically.

## Verification
- `cargo test -p reify-compiler` green.
- At least one compiler test per newly supported query kind.

## Memory hints
- Search: "reify-compiler reflective query params compile-time validation traits.rs"
- Search: "geometry check deferred eval time forall sub-component"
- Search: "Task 1638 geometric_params material_params evaluator layer completion"
- Entity: crates/reify-compiler/src/traits.rs
- Compiler tests only cover 'params' reflective query kind (Graphiti edge 79092d4a)
- No references to material_params in reify-compiler (Graphiti edge 7a7b8dae)

tasks.title: Extend sanitize_wildcard_variants_passthrough with List and Transform cases
tasks.description: Further widen the non-recursion tripwire in sanitize_wildcard_variants_passthrough by adding List and Transform cases to the mirrored tests in crates/reify-expr/src/sanitize.rs (~line 472) and crates/reify-stdlib/src/helpers.rs (~line 893). Task 1644 already broadened the sample from Bool/Int/String to include Vector and Frame; this follow-up extends coverage to two more container/struct wildcard variants that share the same risk profile.
tasks.details:
Append the following two cases to the `cases` array in both crates/reify-expr/src/sanitize.rs (sanitize_wildcard_variants_passthrough, ~line 472) and crates/reify-stdlib/src/helpers.rs (mirrored test, ~line 893):

- Value::List(vec![Value::Real(f64::NAN)])
- Value::Transform {
      rotation: Box::new(Value::Orientation { w: 1.0, x: 0.0, y: 0.0, z: 0.0 }),
      translation: Box::new(Value::Vector(vec![
          Value::Real(f64::NAN),
          Value::Real(0.0),
          Value::Real(0.0),
      ])),
  }

Both cases deliberately carry NaN payloads so the existing non-recursion tripwire semantics continue to hold: if sanitize_value were ever refactored to recurse into children, the inner NaN would become Undef and the assert_eq! would fail (Value::PartialEq uses to_bits(), so NaN == NaN here).

SYNC contract: the two copies of this test MUST remain in step — add identical cases in both files in the same commit. Update the doc comment ("five of ~25") in both crates to reflect the new count.

Verify with:
    cargo test -p reify-expr sanitize_wildcard_variants_passthrough
    cargo test -p reify-stdlib sanitize_wildcard_variants_passthrough

Memory hints: search "sanitize_wildcard_variants_passthrough task 1644 Vector Frame"; search "task 892 sanitize_value consolidation"; related: Task 1643 (asymmetric SYNC comment in same area).

Confirm that Value::List and Value::Transform variants actually exist in crates/reify-expr/src/value.rs with the expected shape before coding — if the Transform variant shape differs (e.g., uses different field names or a non-Box inner value), adjust accordingly while preserving the NaN tripwire intent.

tasks.title: Tighten check_ignore_reasons helper and its unit tests in field_calculus_tests.rs
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1641 review triage (esc-1641-27). Four small tightening fixes in `crates/reify-expr/tests/field_calculus_tests.rs`, all scoped to the `check_ignore_reasons` helper and its unit tests. No behavioral changes.

**S1 — Section banner count is stale (~line 4332).** The comment reads "These five tests exercise the check_ignore_reasons helper" but eight unit tests now exist (rejects_bare_ignore_in_code, allows_bare_ignore_mentioned_in_doc_comments, accepts_compliant_source, rejects_non_known_bug_reason, rejects_stale_plan_step_needle, allows_bare_ignore_in_inner_doc_comment, regular_comment_is_not_skipped, accepts_source_with_no_ignore_attributes). Update to "These eight tests" or "These tests".

**S2 — `rejects_non_known_bug_reason` assertion does not discriminate guards (~line 4378).** The test asserts `err.contains("known bug:")` to verify the positive-invariant guard fired — but the stale-pointer error message (`check_ignore_reasons`, ~line 4275) also literally contains `"known bug: dim_quotient_type..."` inside its example phrase. So the assertion does not actually discriminate guard 2 (positive invariant) from guard 3 (stale-pointer sentinel). Today it still passes because the synthetic source does not contain the stale needle and guard 2 returns early. A future refactor swapping guard order or error wording could regress silently.
Fix: assert on a substring unique to the positive-invariant error format, e.g. `err.contains("does not begin with")` or `err.contains("Task 1622 convention")` (the stale-pointer error uses "Found stale-pointer substring" instead).

**S3 — `regular_comment_is_not_skipped` does not pin which guard fired (~line 4426).** The test only asserts `.is_err()`. If a future refactor accidentally rejected all `//` lines via the stale-pointer path or any other unrelated guard, this test would still pass while no longer pinning the documented "`//` is not skipped" semantics.
Fix: add `assert!(err.contains("bare"))` (or `"line 1"`) so the bare-ignore guard is confirmed as the one that fired, mirroring `rejects_bare_ignore_in_code`.

**S4 — Scanner silently ignores non-canonical `#[ignore="..."]` form (~line 4248).** The scanner recognises only the rustfmt-canonical `#[ignore = "..."]` (space-equals-space-quote). A non-canonical `#[ignore="..."]` would fall through both guards silently: `after.starts_with(']')` is false, `after.starts_with(" = \"")` is false, the loop advances. Rustfmt currently enforces the canonical form so this is latent, not an active bug, but it is undocumented and has no unit test pinning the assumption.
Fix (preferred, cheapest): document the rustfmt assumption in the `check_ignore_reasons` docstring — e.g. "assumes canonical `#[ignore = \"...\"]` formatting; non-canonical forms are silently ignored". Optionally strip ASCII whitespace after "ignore" before matching `=`/`"` — but docstring-only is sufficient and matches the meta-test's intent.

All four fixes touch the same function / its sibling unit tests. Keep scope narrow: no changes outside `check_ignore_reasons` and its eight unit tests. Run `cargo test -p reify-expr --test field_calculus_tests check_ignore_reasons` plus `ignore_reason_strings_have_no_stale_plan_pointers` to verify.

memory_hints:
- Task 1622 introduced the meta-test; Task 1641 extracted `check_ignore_reasons` and added the bare-ignore guard.
- Convention: `#[ignore]` reasons in this file must begin with "known bug:".
- Be careful of self-triggering: synthetic sources and scanner constants are assembled via `.concat()` so this file does not contain the literal guarded sequences.

tasks.title: dispatch_constraints: fast path when optimization_registry is empty
tasks.description: Add an early-return fast path at the top of Engine::dispatch_constraints in crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs (~line 400) that short-circuits the bucketing/merging machinery when self.optimization_registry.is_empty() — the overwhelmingly common case. Build a ConstraintInput directly from entries (dropping the Option<&str> target field) and return self.constraint_checker.check(&input) with no BTreeMap, no Vec<Option<ConstraintResult>> scratch, no unzip, no rebuild. Behavior must remain identical; goal is to eliminate the 4-5 extra allocations per batch that Task 273 introduced on every check / edit_check / eval_cached hot-path call site, restoring pre-273 performance when no optimized impls are registered.

tasks.title: @optimized annotation: fix extractor/validator asymmetry and narrow missing-target warning
tasks.description:
Two related cleanups in crates/reify-compiler/src/annotations.rs:

1. optimized_target (lines 196-208) short-circuits on the first @optimized annotation and returns None if that first one is malformed — silently dropping any later valid @optimized("kernel::foo"). This contradicts validate_annotations' "first wins, rest are duplicates" rule: a user with `@optimized` then `@optimized("valid")` gets two warnings AND loses the valid target. Fix: continue scanning for a string-literal @optimized when the first is malformed, bringing the extractor into agreement with the validator's precedence.

2. validate_annotations' "missing string-literal target" warning (lines 80-106) fires for @optimized on structure and occurrence contexts even though the target is only consumed in constraint_def. Users on structures/occurrences see a warning telling them to add a string that nothing reads. Fix: narrow the missing-target warning to `context == "constraint_def"` only, and update the inline comment to match (it currently already says "constraint_def is the only context where this target is actually consumed, but we warn regardless for consistency" — remove the "consistency" justification since it actually misleads users).
tasks.details:
Source: esc-273-83 suggestions #2 (robustness) and #6 (design_coherence) from reviewer_comprehensive.

Both touch the same file and both concern the @optimized annotation's compiler-side contract, so bundling is appropriate.

For (1), update the for-loop in optimized_target to continue past malformed entries:
```rust
for ann in annotations {
    if ann.name == "optimized"
        && let Some(first) = ann.args.first()
        && let reify_syntax::ExprKind::StringLiteral(s) = &first.kind
    {
        return Some(s.clone());
    }
}
None
```
Then add/update tests in crates/reify-compiler/tests/optimized_annotation_tests.rs asserting that `@optimized` followed by `@optimized("valid")` still resolves to Some("valid").

For (2), in validate_annotations' "optimized" arm, move the missing-target warning inside a `context == "constraint_def"` guard.

Coordinate with Task 274 test-coverage followups (compiler-side assertions on CompiledConstraint::optimized_target).

Modules: crates/reify-compiler/src, crates/reify-compiler/tests

tasks.title: OptimizedImpl contract violation: diagnostic + fallback instead of engine panic
tasks.description: In crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs dispatch_constraints (lines 456-463), convert the `assert_eq!` that checks `output.results.len() == indices.len()` for a registered OptimizedImpl into a soft failure: emit a Diagnostic::error naming the target and per-constraint ids, and reroute the affected constraints to the language-level self.constraint_checker fallback instead of panicking. The registry is a third-party extension point intended to support hot-reload and external impls, so a single buggy plugin should not crash the engine. Keep the internal ConstraintChecker result-count assertion (lines 481-487) as a debug_assert — that path is in-tree and far less likely to fire in production, but we still want the safety net during dev.
tasks.details:
Source: esc-273-83 suggestion #5 (robustness) from reviewer_comprehensive.

Design trade-off: panicking is simpler and catches plugin bugs loudly, but for a user-extension point it converts a plugin defect into a process crash. Graceful degradation (diagnostic + fallback) is the right default.

Flow:
- On contract violation, push a Diagnostic::error with context like "OptimizedImpl for target {:?} returned {} results for {} constraints — falling back to language-level checker for this batch".
- Rebuild a ConstraintInput from the bucket's (ConstraintNodeId, &CompiledExpr) pairs and dispatch through self.constraint_checker.check(...).
- Weave those results into the `results` vec via the original indices as today.
- The engine needs a handle to emit diagnostics from dispatch_constraints. Check whether the current call sites thread a diagnostic sink — if not, this task may need to collect violations and return them alongside ConstraintResult, or store them on the Engine for the caller to drain.

Tests to add:
- A #[should_panic] test removed in favor of a test that registers a MockOptimizedImpl returning the wrong result count and asserts (a) a diagnostic is emitted, (b) all N constraints still get a ConstraintResult via the fallback.

Convert the internal-path assertion to `debug_assert_eq!` in the same patch.

Modules: crates/reify-eval/src, crates/reify-eval/tests

tasks.title: Tighten sample() arity-mismatch tests: discriminating body, redundancy cross-ref, type-coherence docs
tasks.description:
Post-merge review of Task 774 identified three test-quality improvements in `crates/reify-expr/tests/field_eval_tests.rs` for the two arity-mismatch tests added there. All three target the same file and complementary concerns; address them together.

1. **Make one-param test discriminating (lines 184-270).** `sample_one_param_lambda_with_three_element_point_returns_undef` currently asserts end-state Undef with an inline `negate_value(Point) → Undef` body_check. But a hypothetical failure mode where sample() decomposed the Point into 3 args (triggering apply_lambda's arity check: 3 args vs 1 param → Undef) would still make the assertion pass, defeating the stated "binds the entire Point as a single value" contract. Fix: use a body that observably depends on what `x` is bound to — e.g., `count(x)` returning 3 iff x is bound to the whole Point, or a String codomain body that lets you assert on a Point-shaped result. Goal: make the binding observable rather than inferred from a shared Undef sink.

2. **Address redundancy with gradient_tests.rs:262-299 (lines 259-346).** `sample_multi_param_lambda_returns_undef_due_to_no_unpacking` hits the same apply_lambda arity check (lib.rs:586) that `wrong_size_point` in gradient_tests.rs already pins (passes a 2-elt Tensor as 1 arg to a 3-param lambda → Undef). Prefer: add a cross-reference comment pointing to gradient_tests.rs so maintainers see both pin the same contract, AND extend the assertion to also verify the constant body `42.0` is NOT observed (e.g., `assert_ne!(result, Value::Real(42.0))`) to make the "unreachable body" claim explicit. Alternative: drop the test in favor of gradient_tests.rs coverage.

3. **Document intentional type-incoherence (lines 309-310 and 244).** The multi-param test's Field has `domain_type: Type::Real` but its lambda has 3 params — a type-incoherent Field the compiler would never produce. Add a sentence to the doc comment acknowledging this is intentionally synthetic to exercise the runtime defensive path and noting that sample()'s analytical dispatch does not consult the type metadata. Likewise, in the one-param test, `field_type` uses `Type::point3(Type::length())` but the runtime Point uses plain `Value::Real(...)` — consider using `Value::Scalar { dimension: LENGTH }` components to match the type (see `sample_gradient_of_constant_field_near_zero` at ~line 408 for the idiomatic pattern).

memory_hints: search "apply_lambda arity check", read crates/reify-expr/tests/field_eval_tests.rs:184-346 and crates/reify-expr/tests/gradient_tests.rs:260-299 before starting.

tasks.title: deps.rs: refine doc wording and add conditional duplicate-preservation test
tasks.description:
Three refinements in crates/reify-eval/src/deps.rs from task 1374 review:

1. **Lines 441-443**: Doc comment says `extract_value_deps` is "the deduplicating variant" but it also sorts (see lines 588-594). Change to: "the deduplicating-and-sorting variant".

2. **Lines 4-9**: Module doc says "conditional branches and match arms may short-circuit at runtime" — misleading for pure Reify expressions where a Conditional evaluates its condition and exactly one branch. Rephrase to: "conditional branches and match arms include every reachable ValueRef across all arms, even though only one arm is taken at runtime — so the static set is a superset of the runtime-read set."

3. **Line 446 area**: Add a companion test using `conditional_expr(flag, x, x)` (same ValueCellId in both arms) that asserts `trace.reads.len() == 3` (flag + x + x) and that two copies of `x` are present. This directly ties the duplicate-preservation contract to the "all branches" property documented at lines 4-9.

tasks.title: Harden zero-arg warning test: tighten predicate & add negative case
tasks.description:
Two improvements to `stdlib_fn_no_args_emits_type_inference_warning` in silent_defaults_tests.rs:

1. **Tighten assertion predicate (line ~529-531):** The `any` predicate matches any warning containing "zero-arg function" and "defaulting to Real" but doesn't verify the warning references `__test_zero_arg_fn`. Add `&& d.message.contains("__test_zero_arg_fn")` so the warning must be about the specific call site under test.

2. **Add negative counterpart test:** Add a sibling test that compiles a single-arg stdlib call (e.g. `sqrt(1.0)`) and asserts `warnings.iter().all(|d| !d.message.contains("zero-arg function"))` — verifying no false positive. Without this, a regression that unconditionally emits the warning would not be caught.

tasks.title: Resolve sample() Point-only unpacking asymmetry with calculus paths and add Vector pinning test
tasks.description:
sample() multi-param lambda unpacking (lib.rs:143-146) only matches Value::Point, while the calculus paths (extract_point_coords in calculus.rs:497) treat Value::Point and Value::Vector identically ("Accept both Point and Vector — they share structural representation"). This creates an asymmetry: if a Value::Vector is passed as the second argument to sample() with a multi-param lambda, it silently falls through to the single-arg path and returns Undef, whereas calculus operators would have accepted it.

Two changes needed:

1. **Decide and implement**: Either extend the guard to `Value::Point(items) | Value::Vector(items)` to match the calculus convention, or add an explicit comment at lib.rs:143 explaining why Vector is intentionally excluded from sample()'s unpacking. Check if there's a semantic reason to distinguish (e.g., Points are positions, Vectors are displacements and shouldn't be "sampled").

2. **Add pinning test**: Add `sample_multi_param_lambda_with_vector_input` in field_eval_tests.rs that passes a `Value::Vector(vec![Real(1.0), Real(2.0), Real(3.0)])` to a 3-param lambda. Assert the expected behavior (Undef if Vector stays excluded, Real(6.0) if Vector support is added). This prevents accidental regressions either way.

memory_hints: search "extract_point_coords Point Vector" and "sample apply_lambda multi param"; get_entity "sample" and "apply_lambda"

tasks.title: si_units: disambiguate prefix_combos field semantics between SiPrefixBase and SiDerivedUnit
tasks.description:
The `prefix_combos` field on `SiPrefixBase` and `SiDerivedUnit` shares the same name and type (`&'static [&'static str]`) but has opposite empty-slice semantics: `&[]` on `SiDerivedUnit` means "no prefixed variants" (unprefixed only), while `&[]` on `SiPrefixBase` means "emit all 20 prefixes." This is a maintenance hazard.

Options:
(a) Rename the field on `SiPrefixBase` to something distinct like `restrict_to_prefixes` to signal the different semantics.
(b) Introduce a small enum wrapper (e.g. `PrefixSet::All` vs `PrefixSet::Only(&[&str])`) so both structs can share the same empty-means-none convention.

Modules: crates/reify-compiler/src/si_units.rs

memory_hints: search "SiPrefixBase prefix_combos semantics" for prior context on this inconsistency.

tasks.title: Restore 'Fillet' needle in modify test and generalize assert_rejected_at_compile helper
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 1628 review (esc-1628-28, reviewer_comprehensive). Two improvements to crates/reify-eval/tests/geometry_error_handling.rs:

S1 (test_coverage): `build_modify_missing_arg_no_kernel_error` (line ~1143) dropped the 'Fillet' needle during refactoring. The original test verified the warning message contained 'missing required geometry argument', 'radius', AND 'Fillet'. The current call only checks the first two: `&["missing required geometry argument", "radius"]`. Add 'Fillet' back: `&["missing required geometry argument", "radius", "Fillet"]`. Also restore 'Fillet' mention in the doc comment at line ~1080 (assertion 3 description).

S2 (code_reuse): `build_primitive_missing_arg_no_kernel_error` (lines ~1006-1069) has the same 5-assertion pattern as the helper but checks `ops.is_empty()` instead of `ops.len() == 1`. Generalize `assert_rejected_at_compile` to accept an `Option<fn(&GeometryOp)->bool>` for the primitive check — `None` means expect zero ops (no preceding primitive), `Some(f)` means expect exactly one op matching `f`. This lets `build_primitive_missing_arg_no_kernel_error` use the helper too.

tasks.title: Harden transitive pub-unit pinning tests: positive one-hop assertion and name-based module lookup
tasks.description:
Two robustness improvements to the transitive pub-unit tests in crates/reify-compiler/tests/user_defined_unit_tests.rs (added by task 1396):

1. **Positive one-hop assertion (around line 482-487):** The `transitive_pub_unit_not_visible_prelude` test checks that module B compiles without errors, but never verifies that B can actually *resolve* `mil`. Add a compile_with_prelude_helper call that uses `mil` in B (e.g. `structure T { param x : Length = 1mil }`) and assert it succeeds. This confirms the one-hop positive case holds, not just that B doesn't error on a no-op source.

2. **Name-based module lookup (line 553):** `transitive_pub_unit_not_visible_via_compile_project` uses `modules.last()` to find the entry module (C), which implicitly depends on topological ordering. Replace with `modules.iter().find(|m| ...)` keyed on module name/path to make the test ordering-independent.

tasks.title: Complete guard_compilation.rs characterization suite: Chain/AssociatedType/MetaBlock tests + strengthen silent-drop assertion
tasks.description:
Two improvements to the guard_compilation.rs characterization test suite (follow-up from task 1669 review):

1. **Missing variant coverage**: `compile_guarded_members` silently drops six variant types. Task 1669 added characterization tests for Port and Connect (alongside the existing ConstraintInst test), but Chain, AssociatedType, and MetaBlock remain untested. Add analogous characterization tests for all three, following the same pattern as the Port/Connect tests.

2. **Assertion gap**: The `assert_no_error_diagnostics` helper (lines 30-40) only checks for `Severity::Error`. The characterization tests claim to pin "silently ignored" behavior, but a future change that emits `Severity::Warning` instead of an error would still pass. Add a stricter assertion (e.g. `assert_no_diagnostics` or `assert_silent_drop`) that verifies no Warning-level diagnostics are emitted either, for tests whose intent is truly "no diagnostics at all."

tasks.title: collection_sub_eval: add count assertion after final Int(2) transition in edit_param_count_int_undef_undef_int_transition
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-eval/tests/collection_sub_eval.rs`, the `edit_param_count_int_undef_undef_int_transition` test checks bolts[0..2) exist and bolts[2..4) are absent after the final Undef→Int(2) edit, but does not verify the total count is exactly 2 via `count_bolt_diameter_instances`. Stray indices beyond [0..4) would go undetected.

Add after line ~537 (after the bolts[2..4) absence checks):
```rust
assert_eq!(
    count_bolt_diameter_instances(&result.values),
    2,
    "exactly 2 bolt diameter instances after Int(2)"
);
```

This mirrors the pattern already used in other transition tests (e.g. line 284).

tasks.title: Handle poisoned mutex in cli_lsp_protocol.rs test lock
tasks.description: Follow-up from esc-1672-43 (reviewer_comprehensive, task 1672). The acquire_lsp_test_lock() at cli_lsp_protocol.rs:19 calls .lock().unwrap(), which panics with opaque 'PoisonError' if a prior test panicked while holding the lock. This was observed in practice during post-merge verify (esc-1672-40) where lsp_initialize_returns_capabilities failed with PoisonError after lsp_full_interactive_loop_through_binary timed out. Fix: use .lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) to recover from poisoned mutex, or at minimum .expect("LSP test lock poisoned — a prior LSP test panicked") for a clearer diagnostic.

tasks.title: Harden integration_corner_cases.rs test assertions and eliminate double eval
tasks.description:
Four test quality improvements in `crates/reify-eval/tests/integration_corner_cases.rs` (from Task 292 review):

1. **Check eval diagnostics in `eval_and_check_ri()`** (correctness, lines 92-98): Currently discards the `EvalResult` with `let _ = engine.eval(&compiled)`, silently swallowing eval errors. Three tests (`auto_free_multiple_solutions`, `all_non_auto_constraints_satisfied`, `assertion_count_at_least_25`) rely solely on this function and will not detect eval errors. Fix: assert no eval-severity diagnostics, matching `eval_ri_file()`.

2. **Assert range inclusivity in `range_equal_bounds_value`** (test-coverage, lines 448-466): `lower_inclusive` and `upper_inclusive` are destructured then discarded with `let _ = ...`. For a degenerate range `5mm..5mm`, assert the expected inclusivity values.

3. **Tighten `option_some_undef_is_determined` assertion** (test-coverage, lines 315-325): Currently only checks `matches!(s_val, Value::Option(Some(_)))` but never verifies the inner value is `Value::Undef`. Tighten to verify `inner == Value::Undef`.

4. **Eliminate double eval in `trait_all_member_kinds`** (performance, lines 147-203): Calls both `eval_ri_file()` and `eval_and_check_ri()`, each creating a fresh Engine and evaluating the module. Inline the engine setup so eval and check share one engine instance.

memory_hints: search for "integration_corner_cases" and "eval_and_check_ri"; get_entity for "Task 292"

tasks.title: si_units: add PrefixSet ordering doc-comment and debug_assert guard
tasks.description:
Two minor improvements to PrefixSet in crates/reify-compiler/src/si_units.rs, from task 1676 review:

1. **Ordering doc-comment (lines 362-376):** The derived-unit loop iterates SI_PREFIXES in declaration order and filters via PrefixSet::includes(). Add a one-line comment above the loop: "// Note: emits prefixed variants in SI_PREFIXES order (ascending magnitude), regardless of the order symbols appear in PrefixSet::Only." This makes the ordering invariant explicit for future contributors.

2. **debug_assert guard (lines 63-66):** PrefixSet::includes() returns true for *any* string when the variant is All, including invalid prefix symbols. Add a debug_assert in the All arm: `debug_assert!(super::SI_PREFIXES.iter().any(|(s,_)| *s == sym), "unknown prefix symbol");` This guards against misuse while being zero-cost in release builds.

Modules: crates/reify-compiler/src/si_units.rs

tasks.title: Harden structural_physical_tests: reuse common helper, improve diagnostics, add boundary test
tasks.description:
Three improvements to crates/reify-compiler/tests/structural_physical_tests.rs from task 558 review:

1. **Reuse common helper (S1):** The `compile_structure` helper (lines 24-44) duplicates `compile_with_stdlib_helper` + `errors_only` from `tests/common/mod.rs`. Add `mod common;` to the file and rewrite `compile_structure` to delegate: `let compiled = common::compile_with_stdlib_helper(source); assert!(common::errors_only(&compiled).is_empty(), ...); compiled`. Note: module path changes from "test" to "unit_test" — verify this is acceptable. Removes ~15 lines of duplication.

2. **Improve operator-match diagnostics (S3):** In `plastic_constraint_expressions_use_correct_operators` (lines 598-616), the operator-matching loop falls through silently on unexpected IR shapes (`_ => {}` and `_ => continue`). If the compiler's IR ever changes the expression shape, the test passes vacuously. Collect the actual `(member, op)` pairs found and include them in assertion messages, or add an explicit else branch that logs unrecognised shapes.

tasks.title: Generic geometry-arg resolution in compile_geometry_call (fixes 14 broken functions)
tasks.description:
Every geometry function that takes a "target" or "profile" geometry argument is broken for let-bound geometry refs, inline nesting, and cross-let composition. Only boolean ops (difference/union/intersection/union_all/intersection_all) work correctly after task 1609. The remaining 14 functions all hardcode `target: GeomRef::Step(0)` and compile the geometry arg as a scalar expression, which silently produces wrong results (geometry disappears or references the wrong handle).

Affected functions (all in `crates/reify-compiler/src/geometry.rs`):
- **Transforms (4):** translate, rotate, scale, rotate_around
- **Patterns (3):** circular_pattern, linear_pattern, mirror
- **Sweeps (5):** extrude, revolve, revolve_full, sweep, loft
- **Modifiers (3):** shell, thicken, draft

The fix should be generic, not per-function copy-paste of the boolean-op pattern.
tasks.details:
## Root cause

In `geometry.rs`, all non-boolean geometry functions fall through to line 238:
```rust
let compiled_args: Vec<CompiledExpr> = args
    .iter()
    .map(|arg| compile_expr(arg, scope, enum_defs, functions, diagnostics))
    .collect();
```

This compiles every argument — including geometry targets — as scalar expressions. Geometry identifiers like `hole` or `body` resolve to `Value::Undef` at eval time, and inline geometry calls like `cylinder(...)` are compiled as function-call expressions rather than geometry sub-ops.

Meanwhile, the op is emitted with `target: GeomRef::Step(0)`, which assumes the target shape is the first handle in the current realization — but no sub-op was emitted to produce that handle.

## Proposed approach: argument-type metadata table

Define a small metadata table mapping each function to which arg positions are geometry refs:

```rust
fn geometry_arg_indices(name: &str) -> &[usize] {
    match name {
        "translate" | "rotate" | "scale" | "shell" | "thicken" | "draft" => &[0],
        "rotate_around" => &[0],
        "circular_pattern" | "linear_pattern" | "mirror" => &[0],
        "extrude" | "revolve" | "revolve_full" => &[0],
        "sweep" => &[0, 1],  // profile + path
        "loft" => &[],  // all args are geometry (variadic, handle separately)
        _ => &[],
    }
}
```

Then a single generic code path:
1. For each geometry arg index, recursively call `compile_geometry_call` → collect sub-ops → track the step offset
2. For each scalar arg index, call `compile_expr` as now
3. Emit sub-ops first, then the main op with correct `GeomRef::Step(N)` indices

This replaces all 14 broken per-function blocks AND the 5 working boolean blocks with one shared implementation. Boolean ops become just another entry in the metadata table.

## Files to modify
- `crates/reify-compiler/src/geometry.rs` — main refactor
- `crates/reify-compiler/tests/let_scope_tests.rs` — add tests for geometry-arg resolution in transforms/patterns/sweeps

## Test strategy
- `translate(hole, ...)` where hole is a let-bound `cylinder()` → should produce correct sub-ops
- `circular_pattern(hole, ...)` where hole is a let-bound geometry → should resolve
- `extrude(profile, ...)` where profile is let-bound → should resolve
- `difference(body, translate(hole, ...))` — nested composition across categories
- Cycle detection still works for transitive refs

## memory_hints
- search: "compile_geometry_call geometry argument resolution"
- search: "GeomRef::Step(0) hardcoding"
- entity: "compile_geometry_call"
- entity: "task 1609"

tasks.title: value.rs guard pattern: extend or add exclusion comment for Value::Point/Vector

The implementor should inspect the guard, determine the correct intent, and apply option (a) or (b) accordingly.

tasks.title: Add edge-case eval dispatch tests for field analysis (zero/hydrostatic tensors)
tasks.description:
The field analysis dispatch tests in crates/reify-eval/tests/field_eval_tests.rs all use the same uniaxial/pure-shear stress patterns. Add edge-case tests through the eval dispatch path:

1. **Zero tensor**: von_mises=0, safety_factor=yield/0 → infinity or error. Verifies divide-by-zero handling in the safety_factor dispatch path.
2. **Hydrostatic stress** (σI): von_mises=0, a known special case. The sister file (field_analysis_tests.rs) covers hydrostatic for von_mises but not at the eval dispatch level.
3. Optionally: a fully populated symmetric tensor with off-diagonal entries for principal_stresses, to exercise eigenvalue computation on a non-trivial input.

These strengthen confidence in dispatch correctness beyond the happy-path tensors.

tasks.title: Design Tree panel MVP + basic visibility controls
tasks.description: Add a new Design Tree panel to the GUI (tabbed alongside PropertyEditor) showing a hierarchical tree of entities with geometric presence. Each node shows kind icon, name, type label, and an eye icon cycling show/ghost/hidden. Default rule: show `param geometry` from Physical trait, hide other Solid let-bindings. Collapse/expand for sub-structure hierarchy. Click to select (synced with viewport + PropertyEditor). State stored in a new SolidJS `designTreeStore` + `viewStateStore`.
tasks.details:
## Scope

PRD: docs/prd-design-tree-views.md, Sections 4 (Design Tree Panel) and 5.1 (Visibility States), 5.4 (Default Rules).

### Frontend (gui/src/)

1. **New component: `panels/DesignTree.tsx`**
   - Recursive tree component rendering entity hierarchy
   - Each node: chevron (expand/collapse), kind icon, name, type label (dimmed), eye icon
   - Eye icon click cycles: show -> ghost -> hidden -> show
   - Click selects entity (update selectionStore)
   - Double-click flies camera to entity bounding box
   - Tabbed with PropertyEditor (new tab bar in the right panel area of App.tsx)

2. **New store: `stores/designTreeStore.ts`**
   - Holds hierarchical entity tree from engine
   - TreeNode: { entityPath, kind, typeName, hasMesh, traitGeometry, children, expanded }
   - Rebuilt from `get_entity_tree()` Tauri command on evaluation complete
   - Preserves expand/collapse state across rebuilds (by entity path)

3. **New store: `stores/viewStateStore.ts`**
   - Holds per-entity visibility state: show | ghost | hidden | inherit
   - Default rule: if traitGeometry -> show, else if Solid let-binding -> hidden, else show
   - `getEffectiveVisibility(entityPath)` resolves inherit chains

4. **Modify `viewport/meshManager.ts`**
   - Consult viewStateStore before adding meshes to scene
   - Show: normal rendering (existing behavior)
   - Hidden: don't add to scene / remove from scene
   - Ghost: placeholder behavior for now (will be replaced by Task 2) — render with low opacity

5. **Modify `App.tsx`**
   - Add tab bar for Tree / Properties in right panel area
   - Wire up DesignTree component

### Backend (gui/src-tauri/)

6. **New Tauri command: `get_entity_tree`**
   - Walk the evaluation graph to produce hierarchical entity tree
   - Each node: entity_path, kind (structure/sub/let/param/port), type_name, has_mesh, trait_geometry flag
   - Emit on evaluation complete alongside existing mesh/value/constraint updates

### Requirements covered
T-1, T-2, T-3, T-4, T-5, T-6, T-7, T-8, T-13, T-14, VIS-1 (partial), VIS-3

tasks.title: Ghost rendering mode in Three.js viewport
tasks.description: Implement the ghost rendering mode for entities with visibility state "ghost". Ghost meshes render as transparent, non-selectable, neutral-colored overlays that provide spatial context without competing for attention. Uses MeshBasicMaterial with low opacity, no depth-write, excluded from raycasting.
tasks.details:
## Scope

PRD: docs/prd-design-tree-views.md, Sections 5.1 (States table) and 11 (Ghost Rendering).

### Implementation

1. **Ghost material setup** (`viewport/ghostMaterial.ts` or inline in meshManager)
   - `MeshBasicMaterial` — no lighting response
   - Color: Catppuccin `surface0` or similar neutral from theme
   - transparent: true, opacity: 0.12-0.18
   - depthWrite: false
   - side: THREE.FrontSide (no backface rendering)

2. **Ghost mesh group** (`viewport/meshManager.ts`)
   - Add a `ghostGroup` to the Three.js scene, rendered after opaque meshes
   - When entity visibility = ghost: clone the mesh geometry reference (not the buffer — share vertex/index data), apply ghost material, add to ghostGroup
   - When entity visibility = show: remove from ghostGroup, add to main scene group with normal material
   - When entity visibility = hidden: remove from both groups

3. **Raycasting exclusion** (`viewport/selection.ts`)
   - Exclude ghostGroup from raycaster intersection tests
   - Clicks on ghost meshes pass through to opaque geometry behind, or to empty space
   - No hover tooltips for ghost meshes

4. **Performance**
   - Ghost meshes share vertex/index buffers with opaque counterparts (no geometry duplication)
   - Switching show<->ghost is a material swap + group reassignment, not a geometry rebuild
   - Target: < 16ms for visibility toggle (within one frame)

5. **Z-fighting mitigation**
   - Apply `polygonOffset` on ghost material: `polygonOffset: true, polygonOffsetFactor: 1, polygonOffsetUnits: 1`
   - This pushes ghost surfaces slightly behind coplanar opaque surfaces

### Requirements covered
VIS-1, VIS-2, VIS-3, Ghost Rendering section (11.1, 11.2, 11.3)

tasks.title: View state model with cascading visibility and override
tasks.description: Implement the full view state model: recursive visibility cascading (parent changes propagate to all descendants), per-node override (child explicit state survives parent cascade), and the "inherit" mechanism. Includes right-click context menu for bulk visibility operations (show/ghost/hide this, children, only this).
tasks.details:
## Scope

PRD: docs/prd-design-tree-views.md, Sections 5.2 (Cascading), 5.3 (Effective Visibility Resolution), 4.3 (Interaction — right-click menu).

### Implementation

1. **Extend `viewStateStore.ts`**
   - Per-node state: `explicit: "show" | "ghost" | "hidden" | null` (null = inherit)
   - `setVisibility(entityPath, state, cascade: boolean)`:
     - Sets explicit state on the target node
     - If cascade=true: sets all descendant nodes to `inherit` (clearing their overrides) so they follow the parent
   - `setVisibilityWithoutCascade(entityPath, state)`:
     - Sets explicit state on target only; descendants keep their current state
   - `resetToInherit(entityPath)`:
     - Clears explicit state on target, making it follow parent
   - `getEffectiveVisibility(entityPath) -> "show" | "ghost" | "hidden"`:
     - Walk up the tree: first node with explicit state wins; if root reached with no explicit state, use view default

2. **Eye icon behavior in DesignTree**
   - Single click on eye icon: cycles show -> ghost -> hidden -> show
   - This is a cascading change (sets target + all descendants follow)
   - Visual indicator on eye icon reflects effective state, with a small "override" dot if the node has an explicit state different from what it would inherit

3. **Right-click context menu** (`panels/DesignTreeContextMenu.tsx`)
   - "Show this and children" — set explicit show, cascade
   - "Ghost this and children" — set explicit ghost, cascade
   - "Hide this and children" — set explicit hidden, cascade
   - "Show only this" — hide all siblings and their children, show this + cascade
   - "Reset to default" — clear all explicit overrides on this node and descendants
   - Separator
   - "Show only this (no cascade)" — show this node only, don't affect children
   
4. **Keyboard shortcuts** (when tree panel is focused)
   - `H`: toggle selected node(s) to hidden
   - `G`: toggle selected node(s) to ghost  
   - `S` or `Enter`: toggle selected node(s) to show

### Requirements covered
VIS-4, VIS-5, VIS-6, VIS-7, T-9

tasks.description: Extend the single-selection model to multi-selection. Ctrl+click toggles entities in/out of selection set, Shift+click range-selects in the tree. Multi-selection syncs between tree and viewport. Bulk visibility operations apply to all selected entities. Escape clears selection.

tasks.title: Auto view generation (Default + All Geometry + purpose-linked)
tasks.description: Implement automatic view generation. The "Default" auto view applies Physical-trait-aware visibility rules (show geometry param, hide other solids). The "All geometry" auto view shows everything. Purpose-linked auto views are generated when purposes are active, showing entities relevant to that purpose. Auto views update dynamically as the design changes.
tasks.details:
## Scope

PRD: docs/prd-design-tree-views.md, Sections 5.4 (Default Rules) and 6.1 (Auto Views).

### Implementation

1. **Auto view generator** (`stores/autoViewGenerator.ts`)
   - `generateDefaultView(tree: EntityTree) -> ViewDefinition`:
     - Walk tree; for each node:
       - If node.traitGeometry: visibility = show
       - Else if node.kind == "let" && node.typeName in {Solid, Surface, Curve}: visibility = hidden
       - Else if node.kind == "structure" || node.kind == "sub": visibility = show (container)
     - Name: "Default", auto: true
   - `generateAllGeometryView(tree: EntityTree) -> ViewDefinition`:
     - All geometric nodes: visibility = show
     - Name: "All geometry", auto: true
   - `generatePurposeViews(tree: EntityTree, activePurposes: string[]) -> ViewDefinition[]`:
     - Heuristic: for each active purpose, generate a view emphasizing relevant entities
     - e.g., `manufacturing_ready` → show final geometry + material-related params, ghost intermediates
     - This is v0.1 heuristic — can be refined later
     - Name: purpose name, auto: true

2. **Extend `viewStateStore.ts`**
   - `views: Map<string, ViewDefinition>` — all views (auto + user)
   - `activeViewId: string` — currently applied view
   - `regenerateAutoViews(tree)`: called on every tree rebuild; regenerates auto views, preserves user views
   - Auto view IDs prefixed with `auto:` (e.g., `auto:default`, `auto:all-geometry`)
   - User view IDs prefixed with `user:` (e.g., `user:my-assembly-view`)

3. **Wire into evaluation cycle**
   - When engine emits new state → rebuild designTreeStore → regenerateAutoViews → reconcile active view
   - If active view is auto: apply regenerated version
   - If active view is user: keep user state, apply default rules only for NEW entities

4. **Backend support**
   - `get_entity_tree` response must include `traitGeometry` flag per node
   - Engine must expose which purposes are currently active (may already be available via evaluation state)

### Requirements covered
VM-1, VM-2, VM-3 (heuristic)

tasks.title: Named views, view management UI, and copy-on-write
tasks.description: Implement user-created named views, the view selector dropdown UI, and the copy-on-write mechanism for auto views. Users can create, rename, delete, and duplicate views. Modifying an auto view creates a COW copy that becomes a user view, preserving the original auto view.
tasks.details:
## Scope

PRD: docs/prd-design-tree-views.md, Sections 6.2 (Named Views), 6.3 (Copy-on-Write), 6.4 (View Selector Interaction).

### Implementation

1. **View selector dropdown** (`panels/ViewSelector.tsx`)
   - Dropdown at top of Design Tree panel, above the tree
   - Shows active view name with a chevron
   - Dropdown content:
     - Auto views section (gear icon prefix, sorted by name)
     - Separator
     - User views section (diamond icon prefix, sorted by name)
     - Separator
     - "+ New view" action
     - "Manage views..." action (opens modal)
   - Active view indicated with checkmark
   - Click to switch active view

2. **View CRUD operations** (in `viewStateStore.ts`)
   - `createView(name: string)`: create empty user view with default rules
   - `renameView(viewId: string, newName: string)`: rename (user views only)
   - `deleteView(viewId: string)`: delete (user views only, cannot delete auto)
   - `duplicateView(viewId: string, newName: string)`: deep copy with new ID
   - `switchView(viewId: string)`: set as active, recompute effective visibility

3. **Copy-on-write mechanism**
   - When user modifies visibility while an auto view is active:
     1. Clone the auto view's current state
     2. Create a new user view named "{auto_name} (modified)"
     3. Apply the user's change to the clone
     4. Switch active view to the clone
     5. Original auto view continues to auto-update
   - Detection: any `setVisibility` call while `activeView.auto == true` triggers COW
   - The COW view gets `auto: false, modified: true` so the UI can show "(modified)" badge

4. **View management modal** (`panels/ViewManageModal.tsx`)
   - List all views with actions: rename, delete, duplicate
   - Auto views: duplicate only (no rename/delete)
   - User views: all actions
   - Drag to reorder user views (ordering is cosmetic only)

5. **Keyboard shortcut**
   - Number keys 1-9: quick-switch to first 9 views in the selector

### Requirements covered
VM-4, VM-5, VM-6, VM-7, VM-8, VM-9, VM-10

tasks.title: Viewport-view decoupling and viewport store
tasks.description: Decouple viewports from views so that multiple viewports can independently reference different views (or the same view with different cameras). Introduce a viewportStore that manages viewport instances, their view assignments, and camera states. This is the abstraction boundary that enables dual viewports and future split viewports.
tasks.details:
## Scope

PRD: docs/prd-design-tree-views.md, Sections 3.2 (New Stores — viewportStore) and 9 (Multi-Viewport Architecture).

### Implementation

1. **New store: `stores/viewportStore.ts`**
   - `viewports: Map<string, ViewportState>`
   - `ViewportState`:
     - `id: string` — stable ID (e.g., "design-main", "def-preview")
     - `type: "design" | "def-preview"`
     - `viewId: string | null` — which view this viewport shows (design viewports only)
     - `defPath: string | null` — which definition to preview (def-preview only)
     - `camera: { position: [x,y,z], target: [x,y,z], up: [x,y,z], zoom: number }`
     - `active: boolean` — is this the focused viewport?
   - `setActiveViewport(id)`: focus a viewport
   - `assignView(viewportId, viewId)`: assign a view to a viewport
   - `updateCamera(viewportId, cameraState)`: persist camera state
   - Initialize with two viewports: "design-main" and "def-preview"

2. **Refactor `viewport/Viewport.tsx`**
   - Accept a `viewportId: string` prop instead of being a singleton
   - Read view assignment from `viewportStore` -> `viewStateStore`
   - Read camera state from `viewportStore`
   - Each viewport instance manages its own Three.js scene/camera/renderer
   - Save camera state back to viewportStore on orbit/pan/zoom

3. **Refactor `viewport/meshManager.ts`**
   - Accept a viewport context (which view to consult for visibility)
   - Multiple meshManager instances can coexist (one per viewport)
   - Shared geometry buffers where possible (multiple viewports showing the same mesh data)

4. **Refactor `viewport/selection.ts`**
   - Selection is global (shared across viewports) — selecting in one viewport highlights in all
   - Raycasting operates on the active viewport's scene

5. **Design tree panel**
   - View selector controls the active design viewport's view assignment
   - Switching views in the tree panel updates `viewportStore.assignView("design-main", newViewId)`

### Requirements covered
VP-4, VP-8, Section 9 (9.1, 9.2)

tasks.title: Dual viewport: def preview + design composition
tasks.description: Implement the dual viewport layout: a def preview viewport (showing a prototype of the selected definition with defaults applied) and a design viewport (showing the instance tree with view controls). Adaptive layout: both/either/neither active. Inactive viewports greyed out and minimized to a strip. Def preview activates when editor cursor is inside a definition body.
tasks.details:
## Scope

PRD: docs/prd-design-tree-views.md, Sections 7 (Dual Viewport) and 3.3 (Backend — get_def_preview).

### Frontend

1. **Dual viewport layout** (`viewport/DualViewport.tsx` or refactor in `App.tsx`)
   - Two viewport regions in the center panel area
   - Layout states:
     - Both active: horizontal split (adjustable divider)
     - Only design active: design fills center, def-preview is a 40px strip at top with label
     - Only def-preview active: def-preview fills center, design is a 40px strip at bottom
     - Neither: placeholder message
   - Each viewport renders as an independent `<Viewport>` component with its own viewportId

2. **Def preview viewport behavior**
   - Active when editor cursor is inside a `structure def` or `occurrence def` body
   - Label in corner: "Preview: {DefName}"
   - Shows prototype instance with all defaults
   - Always uses auto "Default" view rules (show geometry, hide intermediates)
   - Independent camera state (doesn't follow design viewport)
   - No view selector — always auto default
   - Updates live as definition is edited

3. **Activation logic** (`stores/viewportStore.ts`)
   - `defPreviewActive`: derived signal
     - True when editor cursor is inside a definition body
     - Requires: editor cursor position → resolve to containing definition (via LSP or AST query)
   - `designViewportActive`: derived signal
     - True when evaluation graph has instantiated entities (at least one mesh exists)
   - Connect to editorStore cursor position changes

4. **Cursor-to-definition resolution**
   - New Tauri command or LSP request: `get_containing_definition(file: string, line: number, col: number) -> Option<DefInfo>`
   - `DefInfo`: `{ name: string, kind: "structure" | "occurrence", span: SourceSpan }`
   - Debounce: 200ms after cursor stops moving

5. **Minimized viewport strip**
   - 40px tall, full width, with:
     - Viewport type label ("Preview" / "Design")
     - "Click to expand" hint or auto-expand on relevant action
   - Greyed out background (theme surface color)
   - Click to force-expand (manual override of auto-activate)

### Backend

6. **New Tauri command: `get_def_preview`**
   - Input: definition name/path
   - Creates temporary evaluation scope with all defaults
   - Evaluates geometry, tessellates
   - Returns GuiState (meshes + values + constraints)
   - Discards temporary scope
   - Cache by definition content hash (avoid re-evaluating when cursor moves within same def)

7. **New Tauri command: `get_containing_definition`**
   - Input: file path, line, column
   - Returns the definition containing that position, or null
   - Can piggyback on existing AST/LSP infrastructure

### Requirements covered
VP-1, VP-2, VP-3, VP-4, VP-5, VP-6, VP-7

tasks.title: View persistence: localStorage + sidecar file + fuzzy path matching
tasks.description: Implement two-layer view persistence. Layer 1: localStorage for ephemeral per-file view state. Layer 2: sidecar .ri.views.json for durable/shareable persistence. Entity path exact matching on re-evaluation, stale path preservation for undo/branch-switch restoration, and fuzzy path recovery with user confirmation for renames.
tasks.details:
## Scope

PRD: docs/prd-design-tree-views.md, Sections 8 (View Persistence).

### Implementation

1. **localStorage layer** (`stores/viewPersistence.ts`)
   - Key: `reify:views:{absoluteFilePath}`
   - Value: JSON-serialized `PersistentViewState`:
     - `activeViewId: string`
     - `userViews: ViewDefinition[]` (auto views are NOT persisted — they're regenerated)
     - `viewportCameras: Map<string, CameraState>`
     - `timestamp: string` (ISO 8601)
   - Save: debounced (500ms) after any view state change
   - Load: on file open, before auto view generation
   - Priority: sidecar file > localStorage > fresh defaults

2. **Sidecar file layer** (`stores/sidecarPersistence.ts`)
   - File: `{filename}.ri.views.json` alongside the `.ri` file
   - Same schema as localStorage value, plus:
     - `version: string` (schema version for forward compat)
   - Write: on explicit "Save views" action (button in view management UI) + on clean app exit
   - Read: on file open (takes priority over localStorage)
   - New Tauri commands:
     - `read_view_sidecar(ri_path: string) -> Option<PersistentViewState>`
     - `write_view_sidecar(ri_path: string, state: PersistentViewState) -> Result<()>`
   - "Save views" button in view selector dropdown (next to "Manage views...")

3. **Re-evaluation reconciliation** (extend `viewStateStore.ts`)
   - On entity tree rebuild:
     1. Exact match: stored paths that exist in new tree → keep visibility state
     2. New paths: not in any stored view → apply active view's default rules
     3. Missing paths: in stored view but not in new tree → mark as "stale"
   - Stale entries: kept in view state, greyed out in tree panel (if shown at all)
   - Auto-restoration: if a stale path reappears (undo, branch switch), its stored state is restored

4. **Fuzzy path recovery** (`stores/fuzzyPathMatcher.ts`)
   - On re-evaluation, for each stale path, attempt recovery:
     - **Suffix match**: stale `"Assembly.flange.geometry"`, new `"Assembly.bolt_flange.geometry"` — match by shared suffix under renamed parent
     - **Structural match**: same type name + same parent type + similar child structure
   - Never auto-apply: if fuzzy match found, show a non-blocking notification:
     - "Entity 'flange' was renamed. Rebind view settings to 'bolt_flange'? [Yes] [No] [Ignore]"
   - "Yes" rebinds the stored visibility to the new path
   - "No" keeps the stale entry
   - "Ignore" dismisses without action

5. **Camera state persistence**
   - Per-viewport camera state saved in the same persistence layers
   - Restored on file open

### Requirements covered
P-1, P-2, P-3, P-4, P-5, P-6

tasks.title: Engine: entity tree emission, def preview, and identity hints
tasks.description: Backend work to support the Design Tree & View System. Three new Tauri commands: get_entity_tree (hierarchical entity tree from evaluation graph with kind/type/trait metadata), get_def_preview (prototype instance of a definition for the def preview viewport), and get_entity_identity_map (content hashes and structural fingerprints for fuzzy persistence matching).
tasks.details:
## Scope

PRD: docs/prd-design-tree-views.md, Sections 3.3 (Backend Support), 12 (Engine Integration).

### Implementation

1. **`get_entity_tree` Tauri command** (`src-tauri/src/engine.rs` or new `tree.rs`)
   - Walk the evaluation graph to produce a hierarchical tree
   - Each node:
     - `entity_path: String` — dot-separated (e.g., "Assembly.housing.geometry")
     - `kind: String` — "structure", "sub", "let", "param", "port"
     - `type_name: Option<String>` — e.g., "Solid", "BoltFlange", "List<Vent>"
     - `has_mesh: bool` — whether this entity produced tessellated geometry
     - `trait_geometry: bool` — true if this is the `geometry` param from Physical trait
     - `children: Vec<EntityTreeNode>`
   - The tree reflects the INSTANCE structure (elaborated graph), not the definition structure
   - Emitted on every evaluation complete, alongside mesh/value/constraint updates
   - Serialization: JSON via serde, sent as Tauri event or command response

2. **Determining `trait_geometry` flag**
   - During elaboration, when a structure implements `Physical` (or any refinement), the compiler knows which param is `geometry`
   - Tag this in the evaluation graph so it's queryable at GUI time
   - If the trait metadata isn't readily available at tessellation time, an alternative: check if the entity path ends in `.geometry` AND the parent structure's type implements Physical. This heuristic covers the common case.

3. **`get_def_preview` Tauri command** (`src-tauri/src/engine.rs`)
   - Input: definition name (e.g., "BoltFlange") or qualified path
   - Process:
     1. Look up the definition in the current compilation unit
     2. Create a temporary Engine scope (or lightweight instance)
     3. Instantiate the definition with all param defaults applied
     4. Evaluate to produce geometry
     5. Tessellate all Solid results
     6. Build and return a GuiState (meshes + values + constraints)
     7. Discard the temporary scope
   - Caching: cache by (definition name, definition body content hash). Return cached result if definition body hasn't changed.
   - Performance target: < 500ms for typical single-structure definitions
   - Error handling: if definition has required params with no defaults (undef), return partial state with diagnostics

4. **`get_entity_identity_map` Tauri command** (`src-tauri/src/engine.rs`)
   - Returns a map: entity_path -> EntityIdentity
   - `EntityIdentity`:
     - `content_hash: String` — hash of the entity's definition body (stable across renames)
     - `structural_fingerprint: String` — "{type_name}:{parent_type}:{child_count}:{child_types_hash}"
     - `source_span: Option<SourceSpan>` — file + line range
   - Used by frontend fuzzy persistence matching
   - Can be computed lazily (only when persistence layer requests it)

5. **`get_containing_definition` Tauri command**
   - Input: file path, line number, column number
   - Output: Option<DefInfo> where DefInfo = { name, kind, span }
   - Implementation: walk the AST for the file, find the innermost `structure def` or `occurrence def` containing the position
   - Can reuse existing LSP infrastructure (document symbols, or direct AST query)

6. **New IPC types** (`src-tauri/src/types.rs`)
   - `EntityTreeNode { entity_path, kind, type_name, has_mesh, trait_geometry, children }`
   - `EntityIdentity { content_hash, structural_fingerprint, source_span }`
   - `DefInfo { name, kind, span }`

### Requirements covered
Sections 3.3, 12.1, 12.2, 12.4, plus backend support for T-6 (trait_geometry flag), VP-5 (def preview), P-5 (identity hints)
