tasks.title: Fix Lambda-body test fixture and deduplicate compound-variant coverage in type_error_propagation_tests
tasks.description:
Two review follow-ups from task 1920's new find_node tests (crates/reify-compiler/tests/type_error_propagation_tests.rs).

(S1) Lambda body fixture does not test what it claims. At line ~451, `CompiledExpr::lambda(vec![], vec![], error_leaf(), vec![], Type::Error)` gives the outer Lambda node itself `result_type: Type::Error`. The `find_node` function applies `pred(expr)` to the outer node *before* entering its `match`, so this test passes whether the Lambda arm recurses into `body` or not — defeating the table's purpose of asserting each arm visits the correct children. Fix: change the last argument to `Type::Bool` so only the body carries `Type::Error`. Every other row in the table correctly gives the outer node a non-Error type (SetLiteral uses Type::Set, MapLiteral uses Type::Map, Quantifier uses Bool, etc.) — Lambda is the sole hole.

(S2) Duplication between `find_node_traverses_previously_unhandled_kinds` (lines 332-384) and `find_node_compound_variant_coverage` (lines 386-509). The smoke test covers ListLiteral, OptionSome, and Quantifier(collection); the table-driven test already covers every compound variant (and can trivially add those three rows). Two tests asserting substantially the same invariant doubles maintenance when variants change. Fix: either fold ListLiteral / OptionSome / Quantifier-collection rows into the table and delete the smoke test, or drop those rows from the table and keep the smoke test. One source of truth.

Both fixes are localized to the one test file and require no production code changes.

Review followup from task 1920; suggestions S1 and S2 from reviewer_comprehensive.

tasks.title: Harden cross-module compile tests: create stdlib subdir so resolver tolerance is not load-bearing
tasks.description: Several cross-module tests in crates/reify-compiler/tests/constraint_def_compile_tests.rs (cross_module_constraint_def_import ~499 and cross_module_constraint_def_name_collision_emits_shadow_warning ~615) call `ModuleResolver::new(&dir, dir.join("stdlib"))` without creating the stdlib subdirectory — the tests rely on the resolver silently tolerating a missing stdlib path. If the resolver ever becomes strict about stdlib existence, all such tests break together. Add `fs::create_dir_all(dir.join("stdlib")).unwrap();` in each affected test (or factor into a shared fixtures helper — see task 877's tempfile helper extraction idea).

tasks.title: Add public constructors for UserFunctionCall and Match CompiledExprKind variants
tasks.description:
Review follow-up from task 1920; suggestion S3 from reviewer_comprehensive (non-blocking).

Context: task 1920's new `find_node_compound_variant_coverage` test in crates/reify-compiler/tests/type_error_propagation_tests.rs (lines 400-419) has in-test raw struct-literal helpers `make_user_fn_call` and `make_match` because no public constructor exists for `CompiledExprKind::UserFunctionCall` or `CompiledExprKind::Match`. The other 10+ variants in the table use `CompiledExpr::list_literal`, `set_literal`, `map_literal`, `lambda`, `quantifier`, `range_constructor`, `ad_hoc_selector`, `option_some`, `literal`.

The ad-hoc helpers fabricate content-hash bytes (`ContentHash::of(&[0xf0])`, `0xf1`) which risks drifting from the real per-variant tag bytes used by the production constructors (7/8/9/10/12/13/14/15/16/18/19). Today `0xf0`/`0xf1` don't collide, but the risk grows as variants are added.

Fix: add `CompiledExpr::user_function_call(...)` and `CompiledExpr::match_expr(...)` constructors in `crates/reify-types/src/expr.rs` following the pattern of the existing per-variant constructors (with next-available tag bytes). Then update the test to use them and delete the ad-hoc helpers.

Non-blocking — current in-test helpers work — but removes a small correctness hazard and matches the convention already established for other variants.

queries: resolution for: Add public constructors for UserFunctionCall and Match CompiledExprKind variants

done_provenance.note: Deliverables already landed on main prior to task/1972 branching. Verified on main: CompiledExpr::user_function_call (expr.rs:873) and CompiledExpr::match_expr (expr.rs:901) constructors exist with unit tests; type_error_propagation_tests.rs (lines 428,438,447) uses the constructors and the ad-hoc make_user_fn_call/make_match helpers with fabricated 0xf0/0xf1 hash bytes are gone. A drift guard (constructor_hash_tests.rs) also exists. Implementing commits per architect: 760b14cc7, 6964e6dad, f92593dc6, 768739a82, 1293ac16a, 262004bd7, ad40a1ebb. Task HEAD 1a5398435 is an ancestor of main.

tasks.title: conformance.rs: tighten pass2_skipped Let-advertisement guard + add annotated-Let coverage
tasks.description:
Three coupled improvements to the Option B fix landed in task 1951 (`crates/reify-compiler/src/conformance.rs` ~344-351 and the in-crate unit test ~932-1115).

1. **Over-broad guard (robustness)** — conformance.rs:344-351. The current guard `if pass2_skipped.contains(name) { return None; }` fires on the WHOLE `DefaultKind::Let` arm regardless of `cell_type`. If two traits both provide a Let default for the same name — one annotated (Pass 1 registers it as a real cell) and one unannotated (Pass 2 records the name in `pass2_skipped`) — the guard drops BOTH from `available_defaults`. Meanwhile the injection loop at ~520-527 still emits a cell for the annotated Let, so the advertisement map is asymmetric with injection in the OPPOSITE direction the comment claims. A future `RequirementKind::Let` lookup against that name would emit a bogus "missing required member" instead of matching the legitimately injected annotated Let. Latent (RequirementKind::Let is not parser-reachable today, same reason esc-1951-6 and the unit test had to hand-build one), but a regression waiting for the parser extension.

   Fix: tighten to `if cell_type.is_none() && pass2_skipped.contains(name) { return None; }`. That preserves Option B for the unannotated skipped Let while keeping any sibling annotated-Let advertisement intact, matching what the injection loop actually does. Update the rationale comment at ~324-334 to reflect the narrower scope.

2. **Missing annotated-Let coverage (test_coverage)** — conformance.rs:932-1115 unit test. The current test covers the pass2_skipped-triggered-by-Param case but not pass2_skipped-triggered-by-annotated-Let. That latter case (Trait A: `let x : Length = 1mm`, Trait B: `let x = 5.0`) is exactly where the over-broad guard above would silently drop a legitimate annotated-Let advertisement. Add a second sub-case (or sibling test) where Pass 1 registers an annotated Let, Pass 2 records pass2_skipped from an unannotated Let with the same name, and a hand-built `RequirementKind::Let` requirement of the matching type is satisfied (no "missing required member" diagnostic emitted).

3. **Single-letter substring match in test (test_quality)** — conformance.rs:1095-1105. The filter `d.message.contains('x')` matches incidental `x` letters in diagnostic prose ("expr", "exists", "expect", "syntax", "mismatch", etc.). Tighten to a delimited match — diagnostics quote member names as `'{}'`, so use `d.message.contains("'x'")` or `d.message.contains("member 'x'")`. Apply on both the phantom-diagnostic filter and the missing-member filter.

Verify: `cargo test -p reify-compiler` green; `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check`.

Origin: reviewer_comprehensive on task 1951 (esc-1951-20, suggestions 1 + 3 + 4). Distinct from task 1952 (Pass 1 ↔ Pass 2 cell-injection symmetry — that's about the injection loop; this is about the advertisement builder) and from task 1914 #3 (shared advertised-type helper — the helper extraction would touch the same region; coordinate sequencing if both land).

Memory hints: search "task 1951 pass2_skipped phantom Let advertisement Option B"; entities: DefaultKind, AvailableDefaultKind, RequirementKind.

tasks.title: Delete tautological phantom_let_advertisement_contract_for_future_parser_extension test
tasks.description:
Delete the `phantom_let_advertisement_contract_for_future_parser_extension` integration test in `crates/reify-compiler/tests/trait_merge_tests.rs` (~1404-1522, ~120 lines).

The test is explicitly self-documented as non-distinguishing: "all three assertions pass unconditionally today" and "cannot distinguish the patched binary from the unpatched one". The behavioral assertions are tautologies against the current code — it provides zero regression coverage for the Option B fix landed in task 1951 (the in-crate unit test `option_b_fix_blocks_phantom_let_entry_for_pass2_skipped_name` in conformance.rs is what actually gates the behavior).

Assertion 3 in particular is a pure wording pin on a diagnostic that is never emitted in the reachable universe, so it will continue to pass even if the Option B guard is deleted. Meanwhile the test locks in source-level details (trait/member/type names, compile-cleanly expectations) that can break for unrelated reasons.

Action:
- Delete the test function (and any test-only imports it solely uses).
- Leave a short breadcrumb comment in `conformance.rs` near the pass2_skipped guard pointing to the in-crate unit test, so a future contributor extending the parser to make `RequirementKind::Let` reachable knows where the behavioral coverage lives.
- When `RequirementKind::Let` becomes parser-reachable, write the regression test then in the reachable form (likely follow-up to esc-1951-6 / task 1951's design_concern escalation).

Verify: `cargo test -p reify-compiler` green; `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean.

Origin: reviewer_comprehensive on task 1951 (esc-1951-20, suggestion 2).

Memory hints: search "task 1951 forward-compat placeholder phantom Let advertisement test"; the test was added in commit 7351ae1c0 (worktree 1951 branch).

tasks.title: conformance.rs: tighten pass2_skipped Let-advertisement guard + annotated-Let coverage + delimited substring
tasks.description:
Three coupled improvements to task 1951's Option B fix in `crates/reify-compiler/src/conformance.rs`.

1. Over-broad guard (robustness, line ~344-351): The guard `if pass2_skipped.contains(name) { return None; }` fires on the WHOLE `DefaultKind::Let` arm regardless of `cell_type`. If two traits both provide a Let default for the same name (one annotated, one unannotated), it drops BOTH from `available_defaults` while the injection loop still emits the annotated cell — asymmetric in the OPPOSITE direction the comment claims. Fix: `if cell_type.is_none() && pass2_skipped.contains(name) { return None; }`. Update rationale comment ~324-334. Latent today (RequirementKind::Let not parser-reachable) but a regression waiting for the parser extension.

2. Missing annotated-Let test coverage (line ~932-1115 unit test): Add a sub-case where Pass 1 registers an annotated Let, Pass 2 records pass2_skipped from an unannotated Let with the same name, and a hand-built `RequirementKind::Let` requirement is satisfied (no missing-member diagnostic). Surfaces the bug from #1.

3. Single-letter substring (test_quality, line ~1095-1105): `d.message.contains('x')` matches incidental letters in diagnostic prose. Tighten to `contains("'x'")` or `contains("member 'x'")` on both filters.

Verify: `cargo test -p reify-compiler`; `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`; `cargo fmt --check`.

Origin: reviewer_comprehensive on task 1951 (esc-1951-20, suggestions 1+3+4). Distinct from task 1952 (cell-injection symmetry) and task 1914 #3 (shared advertised-type helper).

tasks.title: Serialize OCCT-touching tests with a file lock to prevent cross-worktree deadlocks
tasks.description: OCCT's kernel is not thread-safe beyond STEP export (documented in memory: `feedback_occt_thread_safety.md`). When the orchestrator runs multiple tasks concurrently, each worktree runs its own `cargo test --workspace` — this produces multiple OCCT test processes executing in parallel, which has been observed to deadlock: three cargo test runs sat at 0% CPU for ~10 hours on 2026-04-19.
tasks.details:
## Observed incident — 2026-04-19

Three concurrent `cargo test --workspace --release` invocations (one per active task's worktree) entered 0% CPU state and never progressed. Each worktree had its own `target/` dir, so this wasn't cargo's own build-lock contention. The most likely culprit is OCCT kernel thread-safety: cross-process OCCT calls through shared system resources (filesystem temp files, OCCT's own global state in the C++ library) can serialize or deadlock when multiple test processes hit them simultaneously.

## What exists today

- `feedback_occt_thread_safety.md`: "OCCT kernel not thread-safe beyond STEP export, M4 needs dedicated kernel thread"
- The kernel is accessed via `crates/reify-kernel-occt/` with FFI bindings to the C++ library.

## Proposed fix

### Option A — file-lock gate around OCCT test binaries (cheap, recommended)

Wrap `cargo test` for OCCT-touching crates with a flock on a file in `/tmp`:

```
flock /tmp/reify-occt.lock cargo test -p reify-kernel-occt ...
```

This serializes OCCT test execution across all concurrent worktrees without changing any test code. Add a helper (e.g. `scripts/cargo-test-occt-gated.sh`) and have the orchestrator's test_command route OCCT-touching tests through it.

### Option B — `#[serial]` attribute via the `serial_test` crate (in-process only)

Inside a single cargo test process, marks OCCT tests as serial so Rust's test harness runs them one at a time. Does not address cross-process concurrency — multiple `cargo test` runs in parallel would still collide. Useful as defence-in-depth alongside Option A.

### Option C — dedicated kernel thread (M4 long-term plan)

Funnel all OCCT calls through a single dedicated thread per process. Does not address cross-process concurrency; multiple processes would each have their own kernel thread and still collide. Slower to implement but right for in-GUI concurrency.

## Recommendation

Ship Option A first — it directly addresses the observed cross-worktree deadlock with a 5-line shell wrapper. Option C remains the right long-term answer for in-process concurrency. Option B is defensive.

## Failing tests to write

- Repro test: spawn two background `cargo test -p reify-kernel-occt` invocations (in two copies of the repo or two worktrees), verify both complete within a bounded time when the flock gate is active, deadlock-or-timeout when it isn't.
- Assertion that the flock gate path covers all OCCT-touching crates (`reify-kernel-occt`, `reify-eval` when geometry tests run, `reify-compiler` geometry integration tests).

## Module tags

crates/reify-kernel-occt, crates/reify-eval, crates/reify-compiler, scripts

## Memory hints

- search: "OCCT thread safety" "kernel thread"
- entity: feedback_occt_thread_safety.md
- Related: orchestrator verify timeouts (orchestrator.yaml, 2026-04-19) catch hangs but waste budget; this task prevents the hangs themselves.

queries: resolution for: Serialize OCCT-touching tests with a file lock to prevent cross-worktree deadlocks

All ancestors of HEAD 950ce6b82. Verified: tests/infra/test_occt_flock_gate.sh reports 14 passed, 0 failed.

2) (Optional, non-blocking) Narrower export surface (gui/src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:26). ID_TO_CALLBACK is exported solely to satisfy the invariant test, which widens the module's public surface for an internal mapping. Consider either (a) adding an "exported for test only — do not import elsewhere" comment, or (b) replacing the export with a narrow `hasCallbackWiring(id: ShortcutId): boolean` predicate (and using `__test__` / internals-for-test style if a richer view is needed). Risk is low since it's a GUI-internal module.

tasks.title: Correct reverse-direction gap NOTE and tighten wildcard diagnostic assertion in geometry dispatch tests
tasks.description:
Two review suggestions from Task 1927 on `crates/reify-compiler/src/geometry.rs`:

1. **Fix incorrect NOTE about reverse-direction coverage (lines 808-810).** The current NOTE states that `all_registry_names_reach_non_wildcard_arm` catches "an arm added to `compile_geometry_call` without a corresponding entry in one of the four lists." This is wrong: that companion test iterates over the four registry lists and checks each list entry reaches a non-wildcard arm — it's the forward direction (list → dispatch). A dispatch arm whose name is NOT in any list would go undetected. Restore an explicit acknowledgement of the gap, e.g. "NOTE: this test does NOT detect the reverse — an arm added to compile_geometry_call whose name is not listed in any of the four lists. The companion `all_registry_names_reach_non_wildcard_arm` only covers the forward direction (list → dispatch). True bidirectional coverage would require a source-text scan of the match arms."

2. **Tighten substring assertion on wildcard diagnostic (line 864).** `d.message.contains(UNSUPPORTED_GEOMETRY_FN_MSG)` could in principle be tripped by an arm-specific diagnostic that embeds the literal words "unsupported geometry function". Replace with either a prefix match (`starts_with(&format!("{}:", UNSUPPORTED_GEOMETRY_FN_MSG))`) or an exact match against `format!("{}: {}", UNSUPPORTED_GEOMETRY_FN_MSG, name)` so only the wildcard arm's output can trip the assertion.

Both suggestions are from reviewer_comprehensive on Task 1927.

tasks.title: perf(reify-eval): replace Vec<CompiledFunction> clone with Arc in Engine::eval and edit_param
tasks.description: Every call to `Engine::eval()` and `Engine::edit_param()` clones the entire merged function table (`self.functions: Vec<CompiledFunction>`). `CompiledFunction` contains boxed expression trees, so cost is O(|user_fns + prelude_fns| * avg_tree_size) per call — a significant allocation for modules with a large prelude.
tasks.details:
Change `Engine::functions` from `Vec<CompiledFunction>` to `Arc<Vec<CompiledFunction>>` so the borrow-checker workaround clone in `eval()` and `edit_param()` becomes O(1).

Key sites:
- `crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs` near `let functions: Vec<CompiledFunction> = self.functions.clone();` inside `eval()` (the PERFORMANCE NOTE comment describes the fix)
- `crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs` near line ~1548: `let functions = self.functions.clone();` inside `edit_param()` — same pattern
- `crates/reify-eval/src/concurrent.rs`: `ConcurrentEditSetup::functions` field is typed `Vec<CompiledFunction>` and assigned via `functions: self.functions.clone()` — needs the type change to `Arc<Vec<CompiledFunction>>`

The desired end state:
```rust
self.functions = Arc::new({ let mut v = module.functions.clone(); v.extend(prelude); v });
let functions = Arc::clone(&self.functions);   // O(1)
```

The `concurrent.rs` change is the blocker that prevented this fix in task 515 (it lies outside that task's scope lock). This task should scope both files together.

tasks.title: OCCT flock gate: timeout ordering — outer timer burns budget waiting for lock
tasks.description: Follow-up to task 1992 review. In orchestrator.yaml the `timeout --kill-after=60 45m` envelope wraps `./scripts/cargo-test-occt-gated.sh ...`, so the 45-minute timer starts *before* flock is acquired. Under multi-worktree contention (48 concurrent worktrees observed 2026-04-19), a waiting invocation can burn its entire budget idling on the flock and be killed before running any tests — the opposite of what the gate is meant to prevent.
tasks.details:
## Context

Task 1992 landed `scripts/cargo-test-occt-gated.sh` and routed `cargo test --workspace` invocations in `orchestrator.yaml` through it. Reviewer flagged that the pre-existing `timeout --kill-after=60 45m` wrappers now fire while the wrapper is still blocked on `flock -x`. With N≥3 active worktrees each needing ~20–30m of test runtime, a late-arriving waiter can exhaust its 45-minute envelope waiting.

## Options

- **(a) Move timeout inside the wrapper** — wrapper acquires lock, then runs `timeout --kill-after=60 <N>m cargo test ...`. Timer starts after lock. Requires exposing a `--timeout` / env-var knob on the wrapper.
- **(b) Raise outer budget to N × runtime** — coarse; wastes wall-clock budget and depends on knowing N.
- **(c) Add `flock -w <seconds>` lock-wait timeout + log wait duration** — fails fast on pathological contention with a clear error; keeps current ordering.
- **(d) Combine (a) + (c)** — bounded wait, post-lock timeout, diagnosable.

Recommend (d): wrapper accepts `REIFY_OCCT_LOCK_WAIT` (default e.g. 30m) and `REIFY_OCCT_TEST_TIMEOUT` (passed through to an internal `timeout` invocation). Emit a log line with wait duration on acquisition so contention is visible in verify output.

## Acceptance

- Wrapper supports post-lock timeout with sensible default.
- Wrapper supports bounded lock wait with a clear error on timeout.
- `orchestrator.yaml` `test_command` uses the wrapper's internal timeout instead of an outer `timeout` so the 45m/60m budgets measure actual test runtime.
- `tests/infra/test_occt_flock_gate.sh` grows assertions: (i) lock-wait timeout fires with non-zero exit and clear message; (ii) post-lock timeout fires after N seconds of execution, not N seconds after wrapper start.

## Modules

scripts/cargo-test-occt-gated.sh, orchestrator.yaml, tests/infra/test_occt_flock_gate.sh

## Memory hints

- search: "OCCT flock gate" "cargo-test-occt-gated"
- See task 1992 for the base wrapper.

tasks.title: Disambiguate hash tag byte collision between UserFunctionCall and Match CompiledExprKind
tasks.description:
Review follow-up from task 1972 (suggestion 1, reviewer_comprehensive, non-blocking).

Both `CompiledExpr::user_function_call` (crates/reify-types/src/expr.rs:887) and `CompiledExpr::match_expr` (:917) use hash tag byte `[6]`. The collision is pre-existing in production: the compiler's inline emission at crates/reify-compiler/src/expr.rs:554 (UserFunctionCall) and :1318 (Match) also both use `[6]`. This creates a latent hash-collision risk between structurally equivalent UserFunctionCall and Match expressions.

Fix: retag one variant (e.g. Match → `[7]`) with coordinated changes to both the constructor in reify-types and the compiler inline path (reify-compiler/src/expr.rs:1318). Confirm no on-disk hashes depend on the current tag byte before changing, and update any golden fixtures / hash-pinning tests.

tasks.title: OCCT flock gate: narrow scope from workspace-wide to OCCT-touching crates
tasks.description: Follow-up to task 1992 review. The wrapper currently serializes the entire `cargo test --workspace` invocation across all concurrent worktrees. Only a subset of workspace crates actually touches OCCT (reify-kernel-occt, reify-eval, reify-compiler geometry tests) — the rest (tree-sitter bindings, protocol, persistence, etc.) run one-at-a-time under the gate for no safety reason.
tasks.details:
## Context

Task 1992 applied the flock gate at the `cargo test --workspace` level — coarse but simple. Under N concurrent worktrees this forces verify to run ~O(N) in wall-clock time for *all* crates, not just OCCT-touching ones.

## Options

- **(a) Two-pass test_command** — run `cargo test -p <occt-crates...>` gated, then `cargo test --workspace --exclude <occt-crates...>` ungated. Preserves parallelism for non-OCCT crates.
- **(b) Per-test gate via `serial_test` crate** — marks OCCT tests serial in-process only; doesn't address cross-process contention. Defense-in-depth alongside (a).
- **(c) Document the trade-off and defer** — acknowledge in the script header block that workspace-wide serialization is intentional simplicity.

Recommend (a). Requires tracking which crates are OCCT-touching — maintain a single list in both `orchestrator.yaml` and the wrapper (or a shared include).

## Acceptance

- `test_command` splits into gated (OCCT-touching crates) and ungated (all other crates) invocations.
- A test in `tests/infra/` asserts the split covers every OCCT-touching crate (no crate silently drops out of the gate).
- Header comment in `cargo-test-occt-gated.sh` documents the design.
- Wall-clock verify time under N concurrent worktrees drops for non-OCCT crates.

## Modules

orchestrator.yaml, scripts/cargo-test-occt-gated.sh, tests/infra

## Memory hints

- search: "OCCT thread safety" "flock gate" "cargo-test-occt-gated"
- See task 1992 for the base gate. Depends on that landing first.

tasks.title: Extend constructor_hash_tests with multi-arg UserFunctionCall and multi-arm/multi-pattern Match cases
tasks.description:
Review follow-up from task 1972 (suggestion 2, reviewer_comprehensive, non-blocking).

crates/reify-compiler/tests/constructor_hash_tests.rs:72-85 covers single-pattern/single-arg happy paths but not multi-arg UserFunctionCall or multi-arm / multi-pattern Match against the compiler's inline emission. A refactor that e.g. folds arms in reverse, or stops combining the second pattern in a multi-pattern arm, would be caught only by the unit test in expr.rs — not by these integration tests.

Fix: add one integration case per variant with ≥2 args and ≥2 arms / multi-pattern arms (e.g. `match c { Red | Blue => 1, _ => 2 }` and a two-arg function call) to pin per-iteration combine order end-to-end against the compiler.

tasks.title: OCCT flock gate: narrow scope from workspace-wide to OCCT-touching crates
tasks.description: Follow-up to task 1992 review. The wrapper currently serializes the entire `cargo test --workspace` invocation across all concurrent worktrees. Only a subset of workspace crates actually touches OCCT (reify-kernel-occt, reify-eval, reify-compiler geometry tests) — the rest (tree-sitter bindings, protocol, persistence, etc.) run one-at-a-time under the gate for no safety reason.
tasks.details:
## Context

Task 1992 applied the flock gate at the `cargo test --workspace` level — coarse but simple. Under N concurrent worktrees this forces verify to run ~O(N) in wall-clock time for *all* crates, not just OCCT-touching ones.

## Options

- (a) Two-pass test_command — `cargo test -p <occt-crates...>` gated, then `cargo test --workspace --exclude <occt-crates...>` ungated. Preserves parallelism for non-OCCT crates.
- (b) Per-test `serial_test` marking — defense-in-depth but doesn't address cross-process contention.
- (c) Document the trade-off and defer.

Recommend (a) once the OCCT-touching crate list is stable.

## Acceptance

- test_command splits into gated (OCCT crates) and ungated (all other crates) invocations.
- A test in tests/infra asserts the split covers every OCCT-touching crate.
- Header in scripts/cargo-test-occt-gated.sh documents the scoping.

## Modules

orchestrator.yaml, scripts/cargo-test-occt-gated.sh, tests/infra

## Memory hints

- search: "OCCT thread safety" "flock gate"
- Depends on task 1992 landing first.

queries: resolution for: OCCT flock gate: narrow scope from workspace-wide to OCCT-touching crates

Phantom-done analogous to task 2001 closure.

tasks.title: Extend constructor_hash_tests with multi-arg UserFunctionCall and multi-arm/multi-pattern Match cases
tasks.description:
Review follow-up from task 1972 (suggestion 2, reviewer_comprehensive, non-blocking).

crates/reify-compiler/tests/constructor_hash_tests.rs:72-85 covers single-pattern/single-arg happy paths but not multi-arg UserFunctionCall or multi-arm / multi-pattern Match against the compiler's inline emission. A refactor that e.g. folds arms in reverse, or stops combining the second pattern in a multi-pattern arm, would be caught only by the unit test in expr.rs — not by these integration tests.

Fix: add one integration case per variant with >=2 args and >=2 arms / multi-pattern arms (e.g. `match c { Red | Blue => 1, _ => 2 }` and a two-arg function call) to pin per-iteration combine order end-to-end against the compiler.

done_provenance.note: Phantom-done: deliverables folded into task 1972's amend commit (multi-arg UserFunctionCall + multi-arm/multi-pattern Match integration tests in constructor_hash_tests.rs, plus match_expr_multi_pattern_arm_combine_order unit test).

tasks.title: Fix content-hash tag byte divergence in reify-test-support builders
tasks.description:
Test-support builders emit content-hash tag bytes that diverge from production, causing any test comparing test-support-built expressions against compiler-produced hashes to silently mismatch:

- `crates/reify-test-support/src/builders/expr.rs:159` — `conditional_expr` uses `ContentHash::of(&[4])`, but compiler emits Conditional with `[5]` at `crates/reify-compiler/src/expr.rs:1375`.
- `crates/reify-test-support/src/builders/expr.rs:181` — `fn_call` uses `[5]`, which collides with Conditional in production.

Fix: align the test-support builder tag bytes with production values used in `reify-compiler/src/expr.rs`. Add a cross-crate tag-agreement test to reify-test-support that compiles a sample expression with the real compiler and asserts equal content_hash.

This is a correctness hazard for any content-hash-based assertion in tests.

tasks.title: Wire focus-entity and navigate-to-source events from MCP tools into GUI frontend
tasks.description:
Backend emits two Tauri events that have no frontend listener — round-trips silently go nowhere:

- `gui/src-tauri/src/main.rs:266` — `focus_entity` command emits `focus-entity`
- `gui/src-tauri/src/mcp_context.rs:302` — MCP `focus_entity` tool emits `focus-entity`
- `gui/src-tauri/src/mcp_context.rs:310` — MCP `navigate_to_source` tool emits `navigate-to-source`

Grep of `gui/src/` returns zero listeners for either event. Frontend navigation today works only because `gui/src/navigation.ts` calls local helpers directly after the IPC round-trip — but when an external agent (Claude via MCP) issues focus/navigate, the UI does not update.

Additionally, `gui/src-tauri/src/claude_bridge.rs:278` builds the intercepted-reify_ tool context with `.with_selection()` but does NOT call `.with_event_emitter()`, so the MCP path always emits from a `None` emitter when invoked by Claude. Contrast with the direct `mcp_tool_call` command at `gui/src-tauri/src/main.rs:296` which does wire the emitter.

Fix:
1. Add `listen` calls in the frontend (likely `gui/src/navigation.ts` or the app-layout component) to receive `focus-entity` and `navigate-to-source` events and dispatch to flyToEntity/selectEntity/editor-scroll.
2. Wire the event emitter into the Claude-bridge-constructed ToolContext in `claude_bridge.rs:278`.
3. Add tests (frontend listener present; backend Claude-path emitter non-None).

tasks.title: Eliminate Engine reconstruction in CliToolContext (same antipattern as LSP didChange)
tasks.description:
The known LSP didChange "new Engine per call" antipattern (tracked in tasks 479/480) has crept into reify-cli's MCP tool context:

- `crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs:80` — `CliToolContext::load_file` constructs a fresh `reify_eval::Engine`
- `crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs:366` — `update_source` same
- `crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs:482` — `open_file` same

Each call throws away the previous Engine, losing all incremental state. Consecutive `update_source` calls re-compile and re-evaluate from scratch instead of using incremental re-eval.

Fix: hold a long-lived Engine on CliToolContext and route load/update/open through `edit_source`/`edit_param` APIs that preserve incremental state, consistent with the intended design.

Note: briefing explicitly tracks this pattern for LSP under 479/480 — this is a separate instance in a different module and deserves its own task. Coordinate fix approach with 479/480 so the caching strategy stays uniform.

tasks.title: Surface degraded-mode signal when GUI is built without OCCT
tasks.description:
`gui/src-tauri/src/main.rs:427` unconditionally calls `OcctKernelHandle::spawn()`. When Reify is built without OCCT, the stub handle (`crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/stubs.rs:75-108`) is silently registered, and every geometry op returns `"OCCT libraries not available"` at runtime — but the user sees no explicit signal that the GUI is in a degraded state. No call site checks `OCCT_AVAILABLE`.

Symptom: silent geometry-op failures that look like design errors.

Fix:
1. Branch on `OCCT_AVAILABLE` (const from `reify-kernel-occt`) before spawning the kernel handle. If false, surface a top-of-viewport banner or status-bar message ("Geometry kernel not available — OCCT not linked") and skip the planner registration so failures become obvious.
2. Add a regression test (feature-gated on `OCCT_AVAILABLE=false`) that asserts a user-visible signal is raised.

This matches the briefing's stability-first posture: failures should be noisy, not silent.

queries: resolution for: Surface degraded-mode signal when GUI is built without OCCT

tasks.title: Fix Draft geometry op: evaluate plane expression instead of using step_handles.last() placeholder
tasks.description:
`crates/reify-eval/src/geometry_ops.rs:298-310` implements `ModifyKind::Draft` by taking `step_handles.last()` as the plane reference instead of evaluating the `plane` argument expression. A comment acknowledges the placeholder.

Result: Draft produces incorrect output whenever the plane argument doesn't happen to be the most recent step. The `target_id` is correctly resolved via `resolve_geom_ref`, but `plane_id` is hard-coded.

Fix:
1. Plumb the plane argument through as a `GeomRef` (or full expression that evaluates to a face/plane reference), then resolve it via `resolve_geom_ref` like `target`.
2. Add an integration test that draft-operates on a non-most-recent step to catch regression.

Also audit related ops in this file for the same placeholder pattern.

tasks.title: Implement name-based GeomRef::Sub resolution (currently falls back to last step)
tasks.description:
`crates/reify-eval/src/geometry_ops.rs:175-186` — `GeomRef::Sub(name)` resolution currently ignores the name and falls back to `step_handles.last()`, emitting a warning diagnostic. Downstream boolean/modify ops target the wrong shape whenever a model refers to a named sub-geometry.

Fix:
1. Extend the geometry compile phase to record a name→step-index map for named sub-references (or expose it on TopologyTemplate).
2. Resolve `GeomRef::Sub(name)` by lookup into that map.
3. Return a proper error (not a warning + fallback) if the name is unknown — follow the "errors must be noisy" convention.
4. Test with a sketch that has multiple named sub-references.

Related to stdlib geometry work; confirm this isn't already covered by an existing task before duplicating.

tasks.title: reapply_user_overrides: distinguish CellNotFound from other engine errors
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs:169-185`, `reapply_user_overrides` silently swallows ALL errors from `engine.edit_param()`. The doc comment claims "CellNotFound means topology changed — skip silently", but `edit_param` can also return `TypeKindMismatch`, `DimensionMismatch`, and `NotInitialized`. Those errors are equally ignored, which can mask genuine bugs (e.g. an override whose dimension no longer matches the new topology's cell type after a user edit).

Fix: match specifically on `Err(EngineError::CellNotFound { .. })` for the silent-skip path; propagate or at minimum log other variants (warn-level tracing) so type/dimension regressions surface instead of disappearing into the override re-apply loop.

tasks.title: Add Engine::edit_source for incremental re-eval across structural source edits
tasks.description:
Currently Engine::eval (crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs:827) rebuilds the Snapshot from the CompiledModule from scratch and re-evaluates every Value node on every call. Engine::edit_param (crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs:1544) already has the incremental machinery (dirty-cone ∩ demand cone, topological eval of affected nodes only) but scoped to param changes.

Task 480 closed part of the LSP gap by adding eval_cached fast-path when the CompiledModule's content_hash is unchanged. The remaining gap, which this task targets, is structural source edits (different content_hash) where most of the module is still the same — the common LSP didChange case.

edit_source would:
1. Take a fresh CompiledModule (post-recompile of edited source).
2. Diff against the prior snapshot via stable node identity (source-range or name-path keying, not positional cell IDs, since today cell IDs are assigned positionally within a fresh compile).
3. Compute changed node set → dirty cone → demand cone intersection.
4. Re-evaluate only affected Value nodes; preserve unchanged snapshot state for everything else.

Primary driver: LSP didChange (tasks 479/480) — keystroke-rate structural edits where ~99% of module is unchanged but content_hash differs, so 480's eval_cached path falls through to cold-start Engine::eval. MCP update_source (task 2008, now using ensure_engine() for prelude/function-table reuse) also benefits but less acutely.

Known complications:
- Stable identity across recompiles: need a keying scheme that survives compiler internals (source-range + name-path is a candidate; needs a design pass).
- Additive/deletive edits have no pre-image — need a correctness-preserving fallback (evaluate newly-added nodes; drop deleted ones and anything that depended on them).
- Compiler changes that break the identity scheme would silently regress incrementality. Needs a test that cross-checks edit_source against a cold eval on the same final source.

Coordinate with: 479/480 (LSP didChange hot path), 2008 (CLI MCP update_source). Caching strategy should stay uniform across entry points.

memory_hints:
- search: "Engine edit_param dirty cone demand cone incremental"
- search: "CompiledModule content_hash stable node identity"
- search: "LSP didChange eval_cached"
- entity: "Engine"
- entity: "EvalState"
- entity: "CompiledModule"

tasks.title: Avoid Vec clone in reapply_user_overrides; consider batch edit_param API
tasks.description:
`reapply_user_overrides` in `crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs:169-185` clones the entire `state.user_overrides` Vec on every invocation, then performs N separate `edit_param` calls — each of which clones the snapshot and walks a dirty cone. For a user with many parameters set, every topology-preserving `update_source` now pays O(N × snapshot-clone) work to re-apply overrides sequentially.

Two options:
- (a) Iterate `user_overrides` in place by index (split borrows) to avoid the Vec clone.
- (b) Add a batch-edit API on `Engine` (e.g. `edit_params(impl IntoIterator<...>)`) that applies a set of param changes against a single snapshot revision, amortizing the clone + dirty-cone traversal.

Reviewer notes this is not a hot path yet — low priority — but the work compounds with parameter count.

tasks.title: Harden parse-error regression test in update_source_invalid_content_does_not_construct_new_engine
tasks.description:
`update_source_invalid_content_does_not_construct_new_engine` in `crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs:868-908` uses the literal string `"enum { }"` to trigger a parse failure. The guard under test specifically checks that `parsed.errors` is non-empty (line 436). If the Reify grammar ever accepts `enum { }` and defers the problem to compile time, this test would silently exercise the wrong branch — the pipeline would commit new state instead of taking the intended "no-op on parse failure" path.

Fix: replace `"enum { }"` with content known to be a hard parse error. Either:
- read `tests/fixtures/bracket_parse_error.ri` (already exists per reviewer), or
- use a syntactically nonsense input like `"{{{{"`, or
- assert at the top of the test that `reify_syntax::parse(input, …)` actually emits a parse error before exercising `update_source`.

tasks.title: Eliminate user_overrides double-bookkeeping by teaching Engine::eval to honor param_overrides
tasks.description:
`CliState::user_overrides: Vec<(ValueCellId, Value)>` exists solely because `Engine::eval()` rebuilds the snapshot from module defaults and does NOT consult the engine's internal `param_overrides` map. The Mcp context therefore tracks overrides twice (once in the engine, once in `CliState`) and re-applies them via `reapply_user_overrides` after every `eval()`. See `crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs:142-185`.

Cleaner long-term fix: teach `Engine::eval()` to consult `param_overrides` (or add `eval_with_overrides`), letting `CliState::user_overrides` and `reapply_user_overrides` be deleted entirely. This also removes the override-leak risk between file switches (see related task on resetting param_overrides on load_file/open_file).

Minimum acceptable: add a FIXME comment in `mcp_context.rs` next to `user_overrides` referencing this follow-up.

tasks.title: viewStateStore: unify "no override" to absence-of-key and export ViewStateStore type (fresh re-scope of #1881)
tasks.description:
Re-scoped replacement for cancelled task #1881. The original scope work (commits bfc97d7f2, 44a1b9af5, 4dea23d1d, a16326895 on branch task/1881) was correct against the viewStateStore as it existed 2026-04-17 but could not be merged because branch task/1881 diverged 140 files / ~16K lines from main. Main has since evolved significantly (added user-view mirror, auto-view generator, ViewState.views/activeViewId fields), so the original diff cannot be cherry-picked cleanly — the scope must be re-applied fresh.

Problem (still present on main as of commit 33c9ef0e6):
- gui/src/stores/viewStateStore.ts setTree() prunes via `delete s.explicit[path]` (→ undefined) while setVisibility/resetToInherit/showOnly clear descendants via `s.explicit[desc] = null`. Consumers see a mix of undefined and null meaning the same thing. Tests pin the inconsistency via both toBeNull() and toBeUndefined() assertions. Any future code using `path in explicit` or `Object.keys(explicit)` behaves differently depending on which path it checks.
- gui/src/panels/DesignTree.tsx imports `createViewStateStore` as a value solely to derive `ReturnType<typeof createViewStateStore>` for a prop type — couples the consumer to the factory when a named type alias would suffice.
tasks.details:
Scope (five changes, all in gui/):

1. gui/src/stores/viewStateStore.ts — unify "no override" to absence-of-key in ALL four mutation sites on main (current file is 444 lines; line numbers will need re-verification on the fresh worktree):
   - setVisibility: cascade loop `s.explicit[desc] = null` → `delete s.explicit[desc];`
   - resetToInherit: both the target-path assignment AND the cascade loop → `delete s.explicit[path];` / `delete s.explicit[desc];`
   - showOnly: ancestors loop `s.explicit[anc] = null` → `delete s.explicit[anc];`, AND the optional post-cascade descendants loop → `delete s.explicit[desc];`
   - (setTree's existing `delete` stays as-is; it's already correct.)
   - mirrorExplicitToActiveUserView uses `Object.entries(s.explicit)` and already skips null values via `if (val != null)` — switching to `delete` is semantically equivalent because Object.entries naturally skips missing keys. Verify this is true before/after the change.

2. gui/src/stores/viewStateStore.ts — update the `ViewState` interface JSDoc comment on `explicit` to document the invariant: "Absence of a key means 'no override' (inherits from nearest explicit ancestor, or falls back to the default rule). Entries are **deleted** on clear; `null` is never stored at runtime even though `ExplicitVisibility` admits it as a type."

3. gui/src/stores/viewStateStore.ts — at end of file, add: `export type ViewStateStore = ReturnType<typeof createViewStateStore>;`

4. gui/src/stores/index.ts — add `export type { ViewStateStore } from './viewStateStore';` immediately after the existing `export type { ViewState } from './viewStateStore';` line.

5. gui/src/panels/DesignTree.tsx — replace `import { createViewStateStore } from '../stores/viewStateStore';` with `import type { ViewStateStore } from '../stores/viewStateStore';` (type-only), and change `viewStateStore: ReturnType<typeof createViewStateStore>` in the Props interface to `viewStateStore: ViewStateStore`. Verify no runtime import of `createViewStateStore` remains in DesignTree.tsx.

6. gui/src/__tests__/viewStateStore.test.ts — flip all assertions that read a cleared explicit entry from `toBeNull()` to `toBeUndefined()` (count will differ from original plan — re-scan the file on the fresh worktree). Add a test that asserts `path in explicit` is false after `resetToInherit` — this pins the invariant beyond what `toBeUndefined()` covers.

7. gui/src/__tests__/DesignTree.test.tsx — flip any `toBeNull()` assertions on `store.state.explicit[...]` entries to `toBeUndefined()` (re-scan on fresh worktree — locations have almost certainly shifted since #1881 was drafted).

Verification:
- `cd gui && npm run typecheck` — passes.
- `cd gui && npx vitest run` — all tests pass.
- Grep to confirm zero `s.explicit[...] = null` assignments remain in viewStateStore.ts (only test fixtures and ExplicitVisibility type definition should retain `null`).

Do NOT base this on task/1881 branch. Start from current main.

tasks.title: Propagate end_line/end_column through navigate-to-source MCP event path
tasks.description:
The navigate-to-source Tauri event emitted from the MCP context (gui/src-tauri/src/mcp_context.rs:307-319) only carries {file, line, column}, dropping end_line/end_column that the reify-mcp tool handler computes. The App.tsx listener then reconstructs the range as {end_line: line, end_column: column}, collapsing to a single point — so a focus range emitted via the MCP navigate-to-source path can never highlight a range wider than one character. The tool's return JSON includes end_line/end_column, but the event emission throws them away.

Fix: Extend the ReifyToolContext::navigate_to_source signature (and the focus range fed to it in tools/navigation.rs) to carry end_line/end_column, include them in the emitted payload, and pass them through bridge.ts and App.tsx into scrollToLocation.

Source: reviewer_comprehensive on task 2007.

tasks.title: Remove double-dispatch from navigateToEntity so focus-entity listener is sole terminal dispatcher
tasks.description:
After task 2007 shipped the focus-entity event listener in App.tsx:365-391, user-initiated navigation still double-dispatches flyToEntity and selectEntity — once inside navigation.ts navigateToEntity, once via the listener. It's idempotent today but brittle: if selectEntity ever toggles or flyToEntity becomes non-idempotent, correctness silently breaks.

Fix: Strip the direct flyToEntity/selectEntity calls from navigateToEntity in gui/src/navigation.ts so the focus-entity listener is the sole terminal dispatcher in practice as well as in name.

Source: reviewer_comprehensive on task 2007.

queries: resolution for: Remove double-dispatch from navigateToEntity so focus-entity listener is sole terminal dispatcher

done_provenance.note: Refactor landed on main via eca0ea95c (remove double-dispatch) + 79d3d584c (clarifying JSDoc amend) before task/2020 branch was created. navigation.ts NavigateToEntityDeps now only exposes focusEntity; navigateToEntity body awaits deps.focusEntity only. App.tsx focus-entity listener is documented as the sole terminal dispatcher. navigation.test.ts already asserts focusEntity-called-once and graceful rejection handling.

tasks.title: Strengthen focus-entity App test to assert selected entity identity is 'Bracket'
tasks.description:
gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx:642-670 asserts mockFlyToEntity was called and that one selected group appears in PropertyEditor, but does not check that the entity path routed to selectionStore.selectEntity is 'Bracket'. A regression that selected the wrong entity would still satisfy `selectedGroups.length === 1`.

Fix: Also assert the selected group's identity (e.g. that the data-selected element corresponds to 'Bracket', or inspect selectionStore state) so the test verifies the value routed through, not merely that something got selected.

Source: reviewer_comprehensive on task 2007.

queries: resolution for: Strengthen focus-entity App test to assert selected entity identity is 'Bracket'

done_provenance.note: Identity assertion expect(capturedViewportProps.selectedEntity).toBe('Bracket') already merged to main at gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx:673.

Source: reviewer_comprehensive on task 2007.

tasks.title: viewStateStore: purge stale null-sentinel references from JSDoc after absence-of-key refactor
tasks.description: Task #2018 changed `s.explicit[path]` clearing from `= null` to `delete`, but two JSDoc blocks still describe the old null-sentinel contract and will mislead future readers.
tasks.details:
Two stale comments identified in the post-merge review of #2018:

1. gui/src/stores/viewStateStore.ts lines 153-166 (JSDoc on `mirrorExplicitToActiveUserView`):
   - Rewrite "mirror the current (non-null) entries in `s.explicit`" → "mirror the current entries in `s.explicit` (cleared keys are simply absent)".
   - Drop the paragraph starting "Null entries in `s.explicit`..." — no null entries ever exist at runtime under the new convention established in #2018.
   - Decide whether to keep the `val != null` guard in the body (around line 173): since `Object.entries` never yields undefined values for present keys, the guard is dead under the new invariant. Either remove it, or keep it as defensive redundancy with a one-line comment explaining why. Recommendation: remove, since keeping defensive code without a reason leaks the old model.

2. gui/src/stores/autoViewGenerator.ts around line 25 (module doc comment on live user-view mirror):
   - Replace "dropping null (inherit-cleared) entries" with "omitting cleared (deleted/absent) entries" (or equivalent wording) to match the #2018 convention.

Verification:
- `cd gui && npm run typecheck` passes.
- `cd gui && npx vitest run src/__tests__/viewStateStore.test.ts src/__tests__/autoViewGenerator.test.ts` passes.
- Grep to confirm no remaining references to "null entries" / "non-null entries" in the viewStateStore or autoViewGenerator doc comments.

Reviewer: reviewer_comprehensive (post-merge review of task #2018).

tasks.title: Tighten ExplicitVisibility type: drop `| null` now that absence-of-key is the sole "no override" signal
tasks.description: After task #2018, `s.explicit[path]` is never `null` at runtime — cleared overrides are deleted. But `gui/src/types.ts:162` still defines `export type ExplicitVisibility = VisibilityState | null`, which keeps a ghost sentinel in the type system that every reader of `state.explicit[path]` must reason about.
tasks.details:
Scope:

1. gui/src/types.ts line 162: consider changing `export type ExplicitVisibility = VisibilityState | null` → `export type ExplicitVisibility = VisibilityState`, OR delete the alias entirely if no non-trivial callers remain.

2. gui/src/stores/viewStateStore.ts: tighten the `ViewState.explicit` field (currently `Record<string, ExplicitVisibility>`) to `Record<string, VisibilityState>`. The existing ViewState JSDoc (lines 16-21 on post-#2018 main) already acknowledges this type-vs-runtime gap and can be simplified.

3. Simplify null-checks at call sites:
   - `getEffectiveVisibility` — `exp != null` becomes `exp !== undefined` (or a truthiness check under `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`).
   - `hasOverride` — same.
   - `mirrorExplicitToActiveUserView` — the `val != null` guard becomes unnecessary; remove.

4. Audit every importer of `ExplicitVisibility` across gui/src/ before narrowing. If any consumer relies on the `null` branch intentionally (e.g. a persisted/serialized shape that accepts null as "clear"), either:
   - Carve out a separate serialization type, OR
   - Leave `ExplicitVisibility` as-is and instead narrow only `ViewState.explicit` at the store level.

Verification:
- `cd gui && npm run typecheck` passes.
- `cd gui && npx vitest run` passes.
- Grep confirms no remaining `ExplicitVisibility | null` unions or `explicit[...] != null` checks where a simpler form would do.

Reviewer: reviewer_comprehensive (post-merge review of task #2018). Marked "out of scope if `ExplicitVisibility` is used elsewhere with intentional null semantics" — so audit first, and close as not-applicable if audit reveals legitimate null callers.

queries: resolution for: Tighten ExplicitVisibility type: drop `| null` now that absence-of-key is the sole "no override" signal

tasks.title: Add negative test: Real-literal arg to trait-typed param triggers conformance error
tasks.description: Lock in the current behaviour that passing a Real (or other non-struct, non-trait) literal to a trait-typed param fires the 'does not conform to trait' diagnostic. The `_` fallthrough in `check_trait_arg_conformance` handles this case (arg_call_name is None so the suppression block does not apply). Adding a test guards against accidental regressions to the suppression heuristic.

tasks.title: Extend trait-arg conformance check to unwrap Option/collection wrappers on declared param types
tasks.description: `check_trait_arg_conformance` currently bails out on any declared param type that is not a `Type::TraitObject`, so `param m : Option<Material>` (and collection-typed trait params like `List<Material>`) silently bypass the conformance check. This is a real correctness gap acknowledged by a TODO in the source. Unwrap wrapper types before deciding whether to enforce conformance.
tasks.details:
## Scope
Review suggestion on task 1874 (esc-1874-31, reviewer_comprehensive).

## Location
crates/reify-compiler/src/conformance.rs:920-924

## Problem
The early return on `!Type::TraitObject` skips trait conformance for any wrapped form:
- `param m : Option<Material>`
- `param ms : List<Material>` / vec-typed
- Any other wrapper over a trait object.

## Approach
- Recognise Option / collection wrappers on the declared param type.
- When the inner type is a `TraitObject`, enforce conformance against the (possibly wrapped) argument.
- For Option, accept `None` / unset values without emitting the error; otherwise check the Some-arm value type.
- For collection types, ideally check each element; at minimum, check the element type if the argument is a literal collection.

## Acceptance
- Tests in `trait_typed_param_tests.rs` covering:
  - `param m : Option<Material>` with non-conforming Some, conforming Some, and None.
  - Collection-typed trait params with conforming / non-conforming elements (at least one negative test).
- Existing trait-typed-param tests continue to pass.

### memory_hints
- search: "check_trait_arg_conformance TraitObject Option"
- search: "Option collection trait param conformance"

tasks.title: Eliminate `arg_call_name` capture by teaching `compile_expr` about structure templates
tasks.description: The `arg_call_name` capture in entity.rs plus the Real→StructureRef promotion in conformance.rs form a two-layer path-split that exists only because `compile_expr` does not know about the template registry when it lowers `FunctionCall` nodes. Have `compile_expr` emit `Type::StructureRef` for calls whose callee is a structure template (deferring to the post-pass if necessary), then drop `arg_call_name` and the Real→StructureRef promotion branch in conformance.rs.
tasks.details:
## Scope
Review suggestion on task 1874 (esc-1874-31, reviewer_comprehensive).

## Location
- crates/reify-compiler/src/entity.rs:859-864 (arg_call_name capture)
- crates/reify-compiler/src/conformance.rs (Real→StructureRef promotion branch)

## Problem
Current design smell acknowledged by TODO in the diff. The indirection:
1. `compile_expr` assigns `Type::Real` (or similar) to structure-instantiation FunctionCalls because template info is unavailable at that point.
2. entity.rs captures the callee name into `arg_call_name` on the side.
3. conformance.rs post-pass promotes the Type based on `arg_call_name` before checking trait conformance.

## Approach
- Route template lookups into `compile_expr` (or defer the final type assignment of FunctionCall to the post-pass with enough information to resolve it directly).
- When the callee resolves to a structure template, emit `Type::StructureRef(name)` instead of Real.
- Drop `arg_call_name` threading in entity.rs.
- Drop the Real→StructureRef promotion branch in conformance.rs.

## Acceptance
- All existing trait-typed-param tests pass unchanged.
- `arg_call_name` field removed.
- Promotion branch in conformance.rs removed.
- No new `Type::StructureRef` mishandling regressions across the rest of the compiler.

### memory_hints
- search: "arg_call_name capture entity compile_expr"
- search: "StructureRef promotion trait conformance"

queries: resolution for: Eliminate `arg_call_name` capture by teaching `compile_expr` about structure templates

tasks.title: Remove low-value SweepKind variant-existence tests in types.rs
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 322 review. The four tests added to crates/reify-compiler/src/types.rs (sweep_kind_extrude_symmetric_variant_exists, sweep_kind_sweep_guided_variant_exists, sweep_kind_loft_guided_variant_exists, sweep_kind_new_variants_distinct_from_existing, lines 643-689) exercise derived trait machinery (Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash) and enum-discriminant uniqueness that the Rust compiler already guarantees at build time. They add ~45 LoC of maintenance surface for zero behavioral coverage.</description>
<parameter name="details">Delete the four tests at crates/reify-compiler/src/types.rs:643-689. The enum declarations plus the real compile/eval tests elsewhere in the task-322 diff (extrude_symmetric_integration, sweep_guided_integration, loft_guided_integration) already cover behavior. Rationale: if the variants vanish, the rest of the crate stops compiling long before these tests run; if #[derive(Hash)] were removed, every real call site would fail. Low-value canaries, not meta-doc tests.

tasks.title: Factor loft/loft_guided arg resolution helper + hoist silent-fallback rationale
tasks.description: Follow-up from task 322 review. The loft_guided arm in crates/reify-compiler/src/geometry.rs:476-530 manually rebuilds the refs vec with the same `geom_refs.get(&i).cloned().unwrap_or(GeomRef::Step(step_offset + i))` pattern as the nearby loft arm, and constructs positional-key args inline. The diff already factored the sweep family via resolve_named_geom_arg but left loft/loft_guided un-factored. Additionally, the silent-fallback vs labelled-per-arg rationale is now repeated across three arms (sweep, sweep_guided, loft_guided).
tasks.details:
Two changes in crates/reify-compiler/src/geometry.rs:

1. **Extract shared helper** (mirrors resolve_named_geom_arg for the sweep family): a small helper such as `resolve_loft_like_args(name, args, compiled_args, geom_refs, step_offset, guide_suffix: bool) -> (Vec<GeomRef>, Vec<(String, CompiledExpr)>)` reused by both the `loft` arm (lines ~455-474) and the `loft_guided` arm (lines ~476-530). The `guide_suffix` flag selects whether the last keyed arg is labelled "guide" (loft_guided) or `profile_{last_idx}` (loft).

2. **Consolidate rationale comments**: the "silent fallback vs. labelled per-arg" explanation currently appears in full in the loft_guided arm (lines 488-498) and in shorter form in the sweep / sweep_guided arms. Hoist the rationale to a single module-level doc comment (or onto `resolve_named_geom_arg`'s docstring) and reduce each per-arm comment to a one-line pointer referencing the canonical location.

Source: reviewer_comprehensive suggestions (code_duplication + documentation_accuracy) from esc-322-22.

tasks.title: Move reify-compiler lib.rs `mod tests` to integration test file
tasks.description:
Move the `mod tests` block from `crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs` (L1067-2656, 58 `#[test]` fns, ~1600 lines) into appropriate test files based on the type of API they test.

The `mod tests` block mixes two categories:

1. **Public-API tests (~48)** — Move these to a new integration test file at `crates/reify-compiler/tests/compile_api_tests.rs`.
2. **Internal-API tests (~10)** — Relocate each to a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` block inside the file that owns the helper under test:
   - Tests of `compile_curve_op` → `geometry_curve.rs`
   - Tests of `compile_transform_op` → `geometry_transform.rs`
   - Tests of `compile_modify_op` → `geometry_modify.rs`
   - Tests of `compile_geometry_call` + `CompilationScope::new` → `geometry.rs` (or `scope.rs` if that's a cleaner fit — architect to decide)
   - Tests referencing only `is_geometry_function` → `units.rs`

Remove the now-empty or trimmed `mod tests` block in `lib.rs`.

Revised success criteria:
- `cargo test -p reify-compiler` runs the same total test count (all 58 + any pre-existing others) and they all pass.
- `crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs` drops from 2656 → ≤ 1500 lines.
- No visibility changes to `pub(crate)` items; no new `pub` surface; no `#[doc(hidden)]` re-exports.

Why: lib.rs is the #1 contended file (35 pending tasks serialized behind it). Shedding the test bulk unblocks wide parallelism, and this split keeps unit tests next to the units under test (standard Rust convention) while moving public-API tests out of the crate root.

tasks.title: Split reify-eval/src/lib.rs `impl Engine` block across sibling files
tasks.description:
`crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs` (3868 lines) contains one `impl Engine` block (L352-3368, 58 methods) plus `mod tests` (L3369-3868). Currently ~32 pending tasks are serialized behind this file.

Rust supports multiple `impl Engine { ... }` blocks across files. Split by concern into ~5 sibling modules:
- `engine_eval.rs` — core `eval()` and direct eval helpers
- `engine_edit.rs` — `edit_param`, `set_param_and_invalidate`, incremental re-eval methods
- `engine_purposes.rs` — `activate_purpose`, `deactivate_purpose`, `is_purpose_active`, `active_objectives`
- `engine_constraints.rs` — `dispatch_constraints` and constraint dispatch helpers
- `engine_admin.rs` — `new`, `register_optimized_impl`, `unregister_optimized_impl`, prelude/functions_count/is_initialized/snapshot/journal accessors, solver/cache/unfold-depth setters

Also move `mod tests` (L3369-3868) to a new integration test at `crates/reify-eval/tests/engine_tests.rs` if it compiles against the public API; otherwise leave as a `tests` submodule in lib.rs.

Keep `pub struct Engine`, `pub enum EngineError`, `pub struct EvaluationState`, `CacheStats`, `EvalResult`, etc. in `lib.rs` (shared type definitions). Move only the `impl Engine { ... }` method bodies.

Success criteria:
- `cargo test -p reify-eval` passes identically.
- `lib.rs` drops from 3868 to ~600-800 lines.
- Each sibling file is < 1500 lines.

Why: single biggest parallelism unlock — 32 pending tasks become concurrently dispatchable when they target different sibling files.

tasks.title: Extract `MergeContext` + `collect_all_requirements` from conformance.rs into sibling module
tasks.description:
After task #2033 lands (tests moved out), extract the `MergeContext` struct and `collect_all_requirements` fn (L697-926, ~230 lines) from `crates/reify-compiler/src/conformance.rs` into a new sibling file `crates/reify-compiler/src/trait_requirements.rs`.

These functions are conceptually distinct from `check_trait_conformance`: they walk the trait refinement hierarchy (handling diamond patterns, depth guards, MAX_TRAIT_DEPTH) to collect all requirements and defaults — an independent responsibility from comparing structure members against a flattened requirement set.

Implementation:
- Create `crates/reify-compiler/src/trait_requirements.rs` with `MergeContext` struct + `impl MergeContext` + `collect_all_requirements` fn. Add `pub(crate) mod trait_requirements;` and `pub(crate) use trait_requirements::*;` to lib.rs.
- Update `conformance.rs` — the remaining `check_trait_conformance` call site already uses `collect_all_requirements` as a free fn, so re-export makes the move transparent.
- Keep `MAX_TRAIT_DEPTH` const with `collect_all_requirements` (it's only used there).

Success criteria:
- `cargo test -p reify-compiler` passes identically.
- `conformance.rs` drops to ~830 lines with only `check_trait_conformance` and `check_trait_arg_conformance`.

Why: separates the hierarchy-walk responsibility from the conformance-check responsibility, reducing the set of tasks that need to touch conformance.rs.

tasks.title: Extract `compile_with_prelude_refs` phases into `compile_builder` submodules
tasks.description:
After task #2031 lands (tests moved out), decompose the 820-line `compile_with_prelude_refs` fn in `crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs` (L245-1066) into per-phase free functions.

The function is already sectioned by comment into 13 passes:
1. parse-errors forwarding
2. module-pragma validation
3. pre-pass: collect decl references + entity-name tracker
4. units compilation (prelude seeding + local units)
5. type alias DFS resolution
6. enum collection
7. functions pass
8. traits pass
9. fields pass
10. constraint-defs pass (with cross-prelude shadow warnings)
11. post-pass: deferred bound checks
12. post-pass: recursion detection + termination + hash remix
13. post-pass: dup-sig check, field-composition, purpose compilation, final hash

Design:
- Introduce `CompilationCtx` (probably `pub(crate) struct`) holding the shared mutable state: `diagnostics`, `scope`, registries (`alias_registry`, `trait_registry`, `constraint_def_registry`, `template_registry`, `field_registry`), `enum_defs`, `resolution_enums`, `functions`, `fields`, `templates`, `imports`, `seen_entity_names`, etc.
- Factor each sectioned pass into a free fn `fn phase_<name>(ctx: &mut CompilationCtx, parsed: &ParsedModule, prelude: &[&CompiledModule])` or similar signature.
- House them in a submodule tree: `crates/reify-compiler/src/compile_builder/{ctx.rs, pre_pass.rs, units.rs, aliases.rs, enums.rs, functions_pass.rs, traits_pass.rs, fields_pass.rs, defs_pass.rs, post_passes.rs, hash.rs}` (or a single `compile_builder.rs` if the phases feel small enough).
- `compile_with_prelude_refs` becomes a thin orchestrator: construct `CompilationCtx`, call each phase in order, assemble `CompiledModule` from ctx fields.

Success criteria:
- `cargo test -p reify-compiler` passes identically (no behavioral change).
- `compile_with_prelude_refs` body shrinks to < 80 lines (orchestrator only).
- `lib.rs` drops below 300 lines (module decls + public entry points + reexports).

Why: biggest structural win — ~35 pending tasks target lib.rs but most actually edit one specific pass. After the split, they lock only the relevant phase file and dispatch concurrently.

Risk: `CompilationCtx` field design takes thought (what moves, what stays as call args). Prefer keeping per-pass call args explicit where natural — don't force everything into ctx just for uniformity.

queries: resolution for: Extract `compile_with_prelude_refs` phases into `compile_builder` submodules

tasks.title: Decompose `check_trait_conformance` remainder into per-phase helpers (post-934, post-2034)
tasks.description:
After tasks #934 (resolve_enum_type helper + check_trait_conformance optimization) and #2034 (MergeContext + collect_all_requirements extraction) both land, `check_trait_conformance` in `crates/reify-compiler/src/conformance.rs` will still be a large monolithic function (~400+ lines estimated). Decompose it into phase helpers.

First read the current state of `check_trait_conformance` and identify natural phase boundaries. Expected candidates:
- **resolve-structure-members** — build the `structure_members: HashMap<String, Type>` from the EntityDefRef (currently the ~80-line filter_map closure).
- **walk-requirements** — invoke `collect_all_requirements` (already extracted by #2034) and collect the flattened requirement + default sets.
- **check-members-vs-requirements** — compare each required member against the structure's members, emit mismatch diagnostics.
- **emit-missing-member-diagnostics** — report requirements unsatisfied by the structure.
- **merge-defaults** — inject default value cells + constraints for trait-provided defaults the structure did not override.

Extract each phase into a `fn check_phase_<name>(...)` with a narrow signature. The top-level `check_trait_conformance` becomes a thin orchestrator (< 80 lines) calling each phase.

If the extracted helpers together grow conformance.rs past ~1000 lines again, move them to a sibling submodule `crates/reify-compiler/src/conformance/checker.rs` (converting conformance.rs into `conformance/mod.rs` or reorganizing as appropriate).

Success criteria:
- `cargo test -p reify-compiler` passes identically — no behavioral change intended.
- `check_trait_conformance` body shrinks to < 80 lines.
- ~12 pending tasks that currently contend on `conformance.rs` can split their edits across phase helpers and dispatch concurrently.

Why: task #934 was scoped as an optimization, not a decomposition. Even after it lands, the 700-line function remains the lock bottleneck. Proper decomposition is the durable fix.

Note to executor: if after reading the post-#934 state you conclude check_trait_conformance is already < 250 lines and naturally cohesive (i.e. #934 did more than expected), defer this task with a note explaining — don't manufacture phase boundaries that don't exist.

tasks.title: Test reapply_user_overrides warn-dedupe downgrade behavior
tasks.description:
Existing tests (`reapply_user_overrides_warns_on_type_kind_mismatch`, `reapply_user_overrides_warns_on_dimension_mismatch`) only verify that the FIRST occurrence of a mismatch emits exactly one WARN. The warn-dedupe feature (downgrade to debug on repeats, clear on success) is not exercised. A regression that breaks the HashSet dedupe (hashing bug, missing insert, spurious clear) would silently re-emit WARNs forever with no test catching it.

Add at least two tests in `crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs`:
1. `reapply_user_overrides_warn_deduped_on_repeat`: call `update_source` with `BRACKET_INT_WIDTH` twice and assert the WARN delta from target `reify_cli::mcp_context` is 1 (second call downgraded to debug).
2. `reapply_user_overrides_warn_cleared_on_success`: mismatch → compatible → mismatch sequence, assert total WARN count is 2 (dedupe entry cleared on the successful reapply, so the second mismatch warns again).

Both tests should use the existing `run_reapply_with_source` test harness (or a small extension) to keep the target-filtered counter idiom consistent.

tasks.title: reapply_user_overrides: clear warned_overrides when user_overrides empty; tighten NotInitialized doc
tasks.description:
Two small cleanups in `crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs` `reapply_user_overrides`:

1. (robustness, line ~192) The `warned_overrides` HashSet is only pruned on per-cell success and on `load_file`/`open_file`. Across a long MCP session with many transient mismatches on distinct cells, the set can accumulate entries bounded by |cells| × 3 variants — not a real leak, but harmless to prune. In the early-return branch when `state.user_overrides.is_empty()`, also clear `state.warned_overrides` (nothing left to dedupe against). Alternatively, add a one-line comment acknowledging the session-cells cap.

2. (design/docs, line ~183) The docstring lists `NotInitialized` among the error variants `edit_param` might return. In practice this helper is only invoked after `eval()` has populated the snapshot, so the engine is always initialized at that point. `NotInitialized` is matched only to satisfy exhaustiveness. Tighten the docstring to state that `NotInitialized` is unreachable in this call path and is matched for exhaustiveness only — or fold it into the silent-skip branch alongside `CellNotFound` if a broader engine failure should also be silent here.

Keep both changes in one commit — they are adjacent and editorial.

queries: resolution for: reapply_user_overrides: clear warned_overrides when user_overrides empty; tighten NotInitialized doc

done_provenance.note: Work landed in commits 7e7121b12 (TDD red test), f182ef80c (fix + doc), and b64c830e8 (refactor to CliState::clear_overrides helper). All are ancestors of main via task/2038 branch.

tasks.title: autoViewGenerator.test: consolidate duplicated ViewDefinition shape assertion
tasks.description: The key-shape assertion `expect(Object.keys(view).sort()).toEqual(['auto', 'id', 'name', 'visibility'])` is repeated verbatim at three locations in gui/src/__tests__/autoViewGenerator.test.ts (lines 112, 152, 184). If a legitimate new ViewDefinition field is ever added (e.g. a real `modified` flag, a `kind` discriminator, icon hint, etc.), the assertion must be updated in three places. Consolidate the shape contract into a single test or helper so the maintenance point is centralized.
tasks.details:
## Scope
- gui/src/__tests__/autoViewGenerator.test.ts (lines 112, 152, 184)
- (optionally) gui/src/types.ts or gui/src/stores/autoViewGenerator.ts if adopting a type-level assertion

## Options
1. Extract a local helper `expectViewDefinitionShape(view)` in autoViewGenerator.test.ts and call it from the three existing `(e)`/`(c)`/`(b)` tests.
2. Preferred: replace the three runtime assertions with a single compile-time contract test using `expectTypeOf<keyof ViewDefinition>().toEqualTypeOf<'id' | 'name' | 'auto' | 'visibility'>()`. That pins the shape once, needs no runtime work, and the per-generator tests can then just check the fields they care about.

Either approach preserves the original intent (prevent accidental resurrection of `modified` or stealth addition of fields) while centralizing maintenance.

## Acceptance
- The three `Object.keys(view).sort()` assertions are removed or reduced to a single shared check.
- A single failure point exists if ViewDefinition's keyset changes.
- `bun test gui/src/__tests__/autoViewGenerator.test.ts` passes.

## Origin
Escalation esc-1959-53 (review suggestion on Task 1959 cleanup branch).

- search: "ViewDefinition shape assertion autoViewGenerator"
- search: "ViewDefinition modified field"

tasks.title: Strengthen generic_constraint_def_with_type_param_type test with positive assertion
tasks.description: The regression test `generic_constraint_def_with_type_param_type_compiles_cleanly` in crates/reify-compiler/tests/constraint_def_compile_tests.rs (added by task 1964) currently only asserts the absence of 'unknown type' errors. Add a positive assertion that the `Aligned` constraint def was actually compiled with the expected shape (e.g. `module.constraint_defs` contains `Aligned` with 2 params and 1 type_param). This turns a negative-only guard into a stronger regression guard against silent drops where the def is never compiled but no unknown-type error is emitted either.
tasks.details:

```
    .expect("Aligned constraint def must compile");
assert_eq!(def.params.len(), 2, "expected params t and w");
assert_eq!(def.type_params.len(), 1, "expected type param T");
```

tasks.title: Promote CompiledExpr hash tag bytes to named constants
tasks.description:
Review follow-up from task 1999 (suggestion 2, reviewer_comprehensive, non-blocking).

The tag-byte allocation table at crates/reify-types/src/expr.rs:212-235 is a manually-maintained comment registry separate from the call sites. Each CompiledExpr constructor seeds ContentHash with a literal tag byte (e.g. `ContentHash::of(&[24])` for Match), and the reify-compiler's inline emission paths (crates/reify-compiler/src/expr.rs:554, :1318) also use literal bytes. This makes drift likely the next time a variant is added.

Fix: Introduce `pub(crate) const TAG_LITERAL: u8 = 0; ... TAG_MATCH: u8 = 24;` (or similar, re-export as needed) in one authoritative location and reference them from both the reify-types constructors and the reify-compiler inline paths. Keep the comment table, but make it reflect the constants. Verify existing golden fixtures / hash-pinning tests still pass — the constant values must equal the current literals exactly.

tasks.title: Polish resolve_loft_like_args: trim docstring, pin test ordering, add debug_assert precondition
tasks.description: Three minor polish items on the newly-introduced resolve_loft_like_args helper in crates/reify-compiler/src/geometry.rs (from task 2030 review).
tasks.details:
All three changes in crates/reify-compiler/src/geometry.rs:

1. **Trim docstring de-duplication (lines 124-170).** The `resolve_loft_like_args` docstring currently restates the silent-fallback policy that now lives in the module-level doc block (lines 1-25). Shrink the per-function doc to describe only the mechanical contract (inputs/outputs, fallback rule, key-naming rule) and keep just a one-line cross-reference to the module-level policy note. Rationale single-sourced in the module header.

2. **Pin CompiledExpr ordering in the unit test (lines ~1490-1560).** The `resolve_loft_like_args_builds_profiles_and_named_args` test currently uses identical `Value::Real(1.0)` placeholders for every slot, so it cannot catch an accidental re-order in the `into_iter().enumerate()` pipeline. Change `make_args(n)` to produce `Value::Real(i as f64)` per slot, and after calling the helper, assert that `named_args[i].1` carries the i-th marker (e.g. by matching on `CompiledExpr.value` for `Value::Real(f)` and checking `f == i as f64`). This pins ordering against future refactors (`.rev()`, shuffled zip, etc.).

3. **Add debug_assert precondition for guide_suffix (around line 161).** The `guide_suffix && n > 0 && i == n - 1` guard handles `n == 0` gracefully, but an arity-0 or arity-1 caller with `guide_suffix=true` silently returns `(vec![], vec![])` / `(vec![guide], [(guide, _)])` instead of signalling the invalid state. Callers currently guard upstream (`loft_guided` requires >= 3), but since the helper is now a shared abstraction, a `debug_assert!(!guide_suffix || n >= 2, "loft_guided requires at least 2 args: profiles + guide")` at the top of the function documents and enforces the precondition for future call sites.

memory_hints: search "resolve_loft_like_args loft_guided silent fallback" entity "SweepKind"

Source: reviewer_comprehensive suggestions from esc-2030-60 on task 2030.
