tasks.title: Harden OCCT-gate test assertions against flag-order/prefix-collision fragility
tasks.description: Follow-up to task 2000 review. Three test_quality suggestions on the OCCT-gate infra tests share a theme: brittle grep/sed patterns that pass today but have latent failure modes as orchestrator.yaml evolves.
tasks.details:
## Context

Task 2000 review flagged three latent fragilities in the OCCT-gate infra tests. All three pass against the current orchestrator.yaml but are sensitive to edits that are otherwise legitimate.

## Issues

### 1. `tests/infra/test_occt_flock_gate.sh:130-135` — order-sensitive sed strip

```sh
STRIPPED=$(echo "$LINE" | sed 's|cargo test --workspace --exclude[^&]*||g')
```

This pattern pins `--exclude` directly after `--workspace`. If someone adds e.g. `cargo test --workspace --release --exclude ...`, the strip won't match and Test 12 will falsely flag a bare ungated workspace invocation.

**Fix options:** (a) make the regex tolerate flag interleaving — match `cargo test --workspace` segments that contain `--exclude` anywhere before the next ` && `; or (b) add a comment that Test 12 intentionally pins flag order and reorderings must update it.

### 2. `tests/infra/test_occt_gated_scope.sh:148,161` — prefix-match without word boundary

```sh
grep -qF " -p $crate"
grep -qF " --exclude $crate"
```

`grep -qF` with a space prefix has no trailing boundary. If a future crate named `reify-cli-extra` is added while `reify-cli` is already declared, the `-p reify-cli` assertion matches `-p reify-cli-extra` spuriously.

**Fix:** switch to `grep -qE " -p ${crate}( |$)"` / `" --exclude ${crate}( |$)"`.

### 3. `tests/infra/test_occt_flock_gate.sh:241` — partial debug-timeout assertion

The current assertion only greps up to `-p reify-kernel-occt`, so dropping `-p reify-eval -p reify-cli` from the debug invocation (but leaving them on release) would still pass Test 17. test_occt_gated_scope.sh's Test 4 covers the full per-crate assertion.

**Fix options:** (a) tighten the grep string to `REIFY_OCCT_TEST_TIMEOUT=2700 ./scripts/cargo-test-occt-gated.sh cargo test -p reify-kernel-occt -p reify-eval -p reify-cli --`; or (b) add a comment that Test 17 intentionally only pins timeout+first-crate adjacency and delegates per-crate coverage to test_occt_gated_scope.sh.

## Acceptance

- Test 12's allowed-form strip tolerates `--release` (or other flags) appearing between `--workspace` and `--exclude`, or documents the pinned order explicitly.
- Tests 4/5/8 in test_occt_gated_scope.sh use word-boundary matching so crate-name prefix collisions cannot produce false positives.
- Test 17 either pins the full per-crate sequence or explicitly documents its narrowed scope.
- All existing tests still pass.

## Memory hints

- See task 2000 for the two-pass gate design. Files touched: tests/infra/test_occt_flock_gate.sh, tests/infra/test_occt_gated_scope.sh.

queries: resolution for: Harden OCCT-gate test assertions against flag-order/prefix-collision fragility

tasks.title: Measure and tune ungated workspace-pass timeout budgets introduced by OCCT gate split
tasks.description: Follow-up to task 2000 review. The two-pass split introduced a separate outer `timeout --kill-after=60 30m/45m` on the ungated `cargo test --workspace --exclude ...` pass. Previously these 11 non-OCCT crates were folded into a 45m budget covering the whole workspace. Verify the new 30m/45m budgets have 2× headroom against real pipeline runtimes.
tasks.details:
## Context

Before task 2000, debug+release `cargo test --workspace` each ran under a single outer `timeout` (historically 2700s = 45m). After the split, the ungated workspace-exclude pass has:

- debug: `timeout --kill-after=60 30m`
- release: `timeout --kill-after=60 45m`

covering 11 non-OCCT crates (tree-sitter bindings, protocol, persistence, etc.). If those crates collectively take >30m on a loaded CI host, verify will now time out where the old command would not.

## Acceptance

- Measure peak observed ungated non-OCCT-pass runtime on the pipeline (debug and release).
- Set the outer `timeout --kill-after=60 <N>m` to ~2× that peak.
- If no measurement data is available yet, bump the debug ungated pass to 45m to match the pre-split historical budget, and document the decision in orchestrator.yaml's comment block.
- Write the rationale and the measured/assumed runtimes into the comment block at orchestrator.yaml:12-26 so the next tightening doesn't revert blind.

## Modules

orchestrator.yaml

## Memory hints

- See task 2000 for the two-pass split. See commit 5cb5fb951 for the initial budgets.

queries: resolution for: Measure and tune ungated workspace-pass timeout budgets introduced by OCCT gate split

tasks.title: Finish PRELUDE_SENTINEL_OFFSET migration in engine_tests; tighten convergence assertions
tasks.description:
Two small follow-ups from task 1948 review.

(1) Code reuse: In `gui/src-tauri/src/tests/engine_tests.rs`, two test sites still use the raw `u32::MAX as usize` literal for the prelude sentinel even though task 1948 migrated other call sites to `reify_types::SourceSpan::PRELUDE_SENTINEL_OFFSET`. Replace:
  - line 1449: `let sentinel = u32::MAX as usize;` → `let sentinel = reify_types::SourceSpan::PRELUDE_SENTINEL_OFFSET;`
  - line 1544: `offset_to_line_col_fast(source, &offsets, u32::MAX as usize)` → use the constant.
  This keeps the sentinel contract expressed in one canonical location so future changes track automatically.

(2) Test quality: In `offset_to_line_col_fast_matches_original_every_offset` (lines 1457-1466), the convergence block now has three assertions:
  - `fast_sentinel == orig_sentinel` (existing equality)
  - `fast_sentinel == (1, 1)` (new literal pin)
  - `orig_sentinel == (1, 1)` (new literal pin, redundant)
The third is redundant by transitivity: once `fast_sentinel == (1,1)` and `fast_sentinel == orig_sentinel` both hold, `orig_sentinel == (1,1)` follows. Collapse to one new assertion:
`assert_eq!(fast_sentinel, (1, 1), "sentinel must be (1,1) to match the no-location fallback");`

Acceptance:
- No remaining `u32::MAX as usize` literal in `engine_tests.rs` at the two cited sentinel sites.
- Convergence test retains `fast_sentinel == orig_sentinel` + a single `(1,1)` pin assertion.
- `cargo test -p reify-gui-tauri` (or the GUI test crate) green.

tasks.title: Robustness polish for reify-test-support helpers (esc-1973-144 S2/S3)
tasks.description: Two low-priority robustness improvements to `crates/reify-test-support/src/helpers.rs` identified by reviewer_comprehensive on esc-1973-144.
tasks.details:
**S2 — Document substring-matching trade-off in `assert_no_type_cascade` (helpers.rs:564)**
The function uses `msg.contains(fragment)` on diagnostic messages. A fragment like `"ambiguous"` or `"duplicate"` could match unrelated future diagnostic wording, silently weakening the whitelist. Reviewer rated this low-impact (call sites use specific phrases) and explicitly said "not worth blocking on."

Action: add a short rustdoc paragraph noting the trade-off — callers should prefer specific phrases over common English words. Alternative (heavier): accept a predicate instead of `&[&str]`; defer unless a real collision surfaces.

**S3 — Make `test_get_let_expr_in_panics_on_missing_default_expr` self-sufficient (helpers.rs:1175-1187 → actually ~1188-1199)**
The test relies on `TopologyTemplateBuilder::auto_param` producing a value cell with `default_expr: None`, which is an implementation detail rather than a documented invariant. `#[should_panic(expected = "has no default expr")]` would catch a regression, but an explicit guard makes the intent clearer.

Action: either (a) add `assert!(cell.default_expr.is_none());` before the `get_let_expr_in` call, or (b) construct the `ValueCell` directly with `default_expr: None` to remove the builder dependency.

Risk: low; both are mechanical one-line edits.

Source: esc-1973-144 review suggestions S2 and S3. S1 from the same escalation (constructor_hash_tests.rs duplicate) is already covered by task #2061.

tasks.title: esc-1746-amend-1 residual suggestions S4/S5/S6: metaKey guard test, autofocus assertion, transactional userViewOrder invariant
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 1746 amend-1 review (non-blocking suggestions not folded during /unblock because source files weren't touched by the blocker fix).

**S4 — metaKey modifier guard test** (`gui/src/__tests__/useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts`)
Existing tests cover ctrlKey/shiftKey/altKey but not metaKey. Implementation at useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:103 guards on all four. Add `Meta+1 does NOT call onSwitchViewByIndex` test mirroring existing ctrl/shift/alt cases.

**S5 — weak autofocus assertion** (`gui/src/__tests__/ViewManageModal.test.tsx:55-63`)
Test titled "first focusable element receives autofocus" only asserts `focusable` is truthy — not that it received focus. After the `queueMicrotask` await, assert `expect(document.activeElement).toBe(focusable)` (or `dialog.contains(document.activeElement)` if jsdom focus is flaky; document the softening in that case).

**S6 — transactional userViewOrder invariant** (`gui/src/stores/viewStateStore.ts`, `gui/src/panels/ViewSelector.tsx`)
Currently ViewSelector's `orderedViews` memo dev-warns when `userViewOrder` references an id missing from `state.views`, but silently filters in prod. Stronger fix: make `deleteView` and `reorderUserViews` transactionally consistent (they already are), then remove the defensive filter so any future bug surfaces loudly instead of being masked. Alternatively, assert invariant in the store and panic (dev) / log (prod).

tasks.title: Close fd 9 on child exec in cargo-test-occt-gated.sh to prevent sccache daemon lock leak
tasks.description: scripts/cargo-test-occt-gated.sh opens fd 9 on the OCCT flock and then `exec timeout ... cargo ...`. Cargo fork-execs sccache as a detached daemon that inherits fd 9, daemonizes, and outlives cargo — so the flock persists forever after the wrapper exits. On 2026-04-20 this wedged the orchestrator merge queue: PID 2847997 (dead) held the lock via sccache PID 3082658, blocking seven queued verify retries. Fix: replace `exec timeout ... "$@"` with `timeout ... "$@" 9<&-` so cargo and its descendants do not inherit fd 9; parent bash continues to hold fd 9 (and thus the flock) until cargo exits.
tasks.details:
File: scripts/cargo-test-occt-gated.sh, lines 92-109.

Two changes:
1. Replace `exec timeout --kill-after=60 "$TEST_TIMEOUT" "$@"` with `timeout --kill-after=60 "$TEST_TIMEOUT" "$@" 9<&-`. The `9<&-` redirect closes fd 9 for the child (and all its descendants, including sccache). Dropping `exec` keeps the wrapper bash alive holding fd 9; when cargo exits, bash exits, fd 9 closes, flock releases.
2. Update the design comment at lines 92-98 to document the new invariant: parent bash holds the lock via fd 9; children do NOT inherit the lock fd, to prevent fd-leak into daemons that outlive cargo (sccache being the concrete case).

Regression test: append Test 18 to tests/infra/test_occt_flock_gate.sh. Spawn the wrapper on a command that forks a `setsid sleep 1000 &` daemon and exits; then verify `flock -n -x $LOCK true` succeeds after the wrapper returns. If fd 9 leaked into the daemon, the second flock would fail.

Existing tests 1-17 must continue to pass. Serialization (Test 8) still holds because parent bash still owns fd 9. Exit-code propagation (Test 9) preserved via bash's last-command exit semantics. `exec` substring guard (Test 7) still satisfied by the surviving `exec 9>>"$LOCK"`.

memory_hints: search("OCCT flock sccache"), search("cargo-test-occt-gated"), get_entity("reify-kernel-occt").

tasks.title: Fix orchestrator scheduler task-fetch ENOENT error
tasks.description: The reify orchestrator scheduler is logging 'ERROR Failed to fetch tasks: [Errno 2] No such file or directory' on a ~15-second loop. This is unrelated to the fd-9 flock leak fix shipped in Task 2064. The error fires continuously and may be causing the double-restart pattern observed on 2026-04-20.
tasks.details:
## Investigation

1. Identify where in the orchestrator scheduler the tasks fetch occurs — likely `scheduler.py` or `task_fetcher.py` in dark-factory. The error is "[Errno 2] No such file or directory", which means a file path is being opened that doesn't exist.

2. Check whether the error is in the reify-specific path (`/home/leo/src/reify/.taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json`) or a dark-factory path.

3. Common causes:
   - Stale `project_root` config pointing at wrong path
   - Missing `.taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json` (verify it exists)
   - Scheduler reading a config path (e.g., `orchestrator.yaml`) that doesn't exist in the reify project root

4. Verify tasks.json exists: `ls -la /home/leo/src/reify/.taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json`

5. Check orchestrator config in reify for correct `project_root` / `tasks_path` settings.

## memory_hints
- search: "orchestrator scheduler task fetch path config"
- search: "tasks.json path project_root configuration"
- entity: "reify orchestrator config"

Reify config was already correct (orchestrator.yaml project_root absolute, tasks.json present). Post-fix run orchestrator-20260421T084219Z.log shows zero 'Failed to fetch tasks' errors. No reify-side commit warranted; follows cross-repo precedent from tasks 1675/1905/1906.

tasks.title: Follow-up to task 1921: three missed silent Type::Real fallbacks in expr.rs (index/quantifier/method)
tasks.description:
Task 1921 completed the Type::Error sentinel audit but missed three specific silent `_ => Type::Real` fallbacks in crates/reify-compiler/src/expr.rs. These are NOT error-producer branches — they are type-inference fallbacks that swallow logic errors without emitting diagnostics, violating the "errors must be noisy" rule (briefing convention, task-117 origin).

Three sites to fix:

1. **expr.rs:1323-1328** — `index_access` compiles `obj[idx]`; when `obj.result_type` is neither `Type::List(_)` nor `Type::Map(_,_)` nor `Type::Error`, the result silently defaults to `Type::Real` with no diagnostic. Reachable from user code e.g. `let x = 5; let y = x[0]`.

2. **expr.rs:1635-1642** — quantifier (`forall`/`exists`) compiles `∀x ∈ collection. P(x)`; when `collection.result_type` is neither `Type::List(_)` nor `Type::Set(_)` nor `Type::Error`, `elem_type` silently defaults to `Type::Real` with no diagnostic. Reachable from user code e.g. `forall x in 5 { x > 0 }`.

3. **expr.rs:1152-1167** — inside `if COLLECTION_AGGREGATION_MEMBERS.contains(&member.as_str())` guard, the trailing `_ => Type::Real` catch-all is unreachable (the guard restricts to count/sum/keys/values) but is still coded as a silent default. Should be `unreachable!()`.

Fixes:
- (1) and (2): push `Diagnostic::error(...)` with a span, then use `make_poison_type(diagnostics, n)` to return `Type::Error` (same pattern as range-no-bounds, match-no-arms).
- (3): replace with `unreachable!("COLLECTION_AGGREGATION_MEMBERS restricts member to count/sum/keys/values")`.

Add regression tests to crates/reify-compiler/tests/silent_defaults_tests.rs:
- `index_into_non_collection_emits_diagnostic`
- `quantifier_over_non_collection_emits_diagnostic`

No test needed for (3) since it's purely a defensive-coding style change (unreachable assertion).

Related: task 1921 (parent audit, done), task 117 (silent-defaults test infrastructure), task 448 (anti-cascade parent).

queries: Type::Real silent fallback expr.rs index quantifier method
expr.rs collection_sub_member_types defensive fallback unreachable

tasks.title: reapply_user_overrides: replace defensive clear with debug_assert invariants
tasks.description:
Post-merge review follow-up on task 2038. Reviewer argues that two defensive patterns introduced in `crates/reify-cli/src/mcp_context.rs::reapply_user_overrides` guard states that are unreachable through public API and should be replaced with type-enforced invariants or `debug_assert!` that fails loudly when the assumption breaks.

Scope:

1. **Early-return branch (lines ~204-211).** Currently calls `state.clear_overrides()` when `user_overrides` is empty to prune any stale `warned_overrides`. The reviewer observes that no public mutation path can leave `warned_overrides` non-empty while `user_overrides` is empty: `load_file`/`open_file` call `clear_overrides()`, `set_parameter` only pushes, the `Ok` arm removes the cell-specific warn entry, and mismatch arms keep entries in `user_overrides`. Consider one of:
   - Revert to bare `return;` and rely on `clear_overrides()` as the single invariant-enforcing entry point.
   - Keep `return;` but add `debug_assert!(state.warned_overrides.is_empty())` so violations fail loudly in tests.

2. **Paired test (lines ~1096-1142).** `reapply_user_overrides_maintains_warned_subset_invariant` reaches into `ctx.lock_state()` and calls `state.user_overrides.clear()` directly to fabricate an otherwise-unreachable state. This ossifies the private `CliState` field layout. If the defensive branch is removed, delete this test. If kept as a `debug_assert!`, rewrite the test to trigger the assertion under pre-fix conditions rather than behaviorally verify the silent mutation.

3. **NotInitialized docstring (lines ~193-203).** The docstring asserts `NotInitialized` is unreachable because `reapply_user_overrides` runs immediately after `ensure_engine(...).eval(...)`. This is an unenforced calling convention. Consider `debug_assert!(!matches!(err, EngineError::NotInitialized), "reapply called before eval")` when classifying the variant, or reword the docstring to mark this as an unenforced precondition rather than an invariant.

Keep all three changes in one commit; they share a theme ("prefer loud invariant failures over silent defensive cleanup").

tasks.title: Refactor arc-sharing characterization tests: extract helper, add SafetyFactor coverage
tasks.description:
Two related test-quality improvements landed alongside the Value::Field::lambda Box→Arc change (task 1629).

(1) Extract a shared helper in crates/reify-expr/tests/field_calculus_tests.rs (~lines 4284-4485). The four tests `gradient_result_arc_shares_source_lambda`, `divergence_result_arc_shares_source_lambda`, `curl_result_arc_shares_source_lambda`, `laplacian_result_arc_shares_source_lambda` are near-identical ~30-line copy-paste, differing only in the operator name and the source field's codomain (scalar vs vec3). Extract `assert_unary_op_shares_source_lambda(op_name: &str, codomain: Type, lambda: Value)` and call it four times. Reduces ~120 lines → ~40 and makes adding a fifth unary field operator a one-liner.

(2) Add SafetyFactor coverage to crates/reify-types/tests/field_lambda_arc_sharing.rs. The current characterization test only covers `FieldSourceKind::Analytical` with an `Undef`-free Lambda payload. The `lambda` slot's docstring in `crates/reify-types/src/value.rs` notes the payload shape varies by `FieldSourceKind` (Field for Gradient/Divergence/Curl/Laplacian, List for SafetyFactor, Undef for Sampled/Imported). Add a single ptr_eq case constructing `lambda: Arc::new(Value::List(vec![...]))` with `source: FieldSourceKind::SafetyFactor` to guard against a future refactor that accidentally deep-clones through the list path. Reviewer notes this is not required for correctness — pure tighter coverage.

Verification: `cargo test -p reify-expr field_calculus` and `cargo test -p reify-types field_lambda_arc_sharing`.

Source: post-merge review suggestions on task 1629 (reviewer_comprehensive).

tasks.title: Harden stdlib fallback in module_dag.rs: all-or-nothing overlay + doc placement
tasks.description:
Address three review suggestions on the stdlib fs/embedded fallback in crates/reify-compiler/src/module_dag.rs:

1. (robustness, lines 156-185) Per-module fs-vs-embedded fallback can silently mix sources. Example: if stdlib_root has units.ri but not materials_mechanical.ri, `import std.units` compiles from fs, but `import std.materials.mechanical` falls back to embedded stdlib whose own std.units was compiled against the embedded copy. Downstream consumers then see two CompiledModules under `std.units` semantics with potentially differing types, producing hard-to-diagnose trait/type mismatches.

   Fix: either (a) make the fallback all-or-nothing per import session — if ANY std.* came from fs, require all to come from fs and fail with a clear diagnostic, OR (b) document the partial-overlay semantics explicitly and add a pinning test. At minimum add an assertion/diagnostic when fallback is about to insert a stdlib prefix module that would shadow an already-present fs version with differing content.

2. (architecture, lines 22-28) The precedence-fallback doc block is attached to `ModuleResolver`, but `ModuleResolver` itself has no fallback behaviour — the fallback lives in `ModuleDag::compile_module`. Move the precedence block onto `ModuleDag::compile_module` (or `ModuleDag`); keep the resolver doc focused on filesystem resolution only.

3. (performance, lines 171-177) The fallback clones every CompiledModule in the stdlib[..=idx] prefix into self.modules. For sequential compile_module calls over multiple std paths, each call re-walks the prefix (the contains_key guard skips inserts but not iteration). Either short-circuit once an already-present module is observed at the end of the prefix (the insert-prefix invariant means earlier indices must also be present), or add a one-line comment documenting the O(idx) cost as intentional.

Memory hints: search "stdlib embedded fallback module_dag"; review `crates/reify-compiler/src/stdlib_loader.rs` and the test `compile_project_stdlib_unit_collision_mentions_stdlib` which pins filesystem-precedence behavior.

tasks.title: Tighten ModulePath::from_dotted contract: Result or hard assert, fix docstring
tasks.description:
Address review suggestion on crates/reify-types/src/identity.rs:28-36 (`ModulePath::from_dotted`).

Problem: the function uses `debug_assert!` for emptiness and empty-segment checks, but the docstring's guidance ("Add a `debug_assert` at call sites that accept user-provided strings") inverts responsibility — callers shouldn't duplicate invariants the constructor owns. In release builds, bad inputs ("" or "a..b") silently produce `[""]` or `["a", "", "b"]`, which then flow into module identity keys. Since `from_dotted` is called on `module_path` strings originating from import declarations in `ModuleDag::compile_module` (crates/reify-compiler/src/module_dag.rs), a malformed parse/import can silently reach this in release.

Fix: either
- (preferred) change the signature to `from_dotted(dotted: &str) -> Result<Self, ParseError>` and update the (few) callers to handle the error, OR
- convert the `debug_assert!`s to hard `assert!`s since this is not a hot path.

Also: remove the misleading "add a `debug_assert` at call sites" note from the docstring — the invariant is the constructor's responsibility.

Memory hints: grep `from_dotted` for the full caller set before choosing Result vs assert; callers include `module_dag.rs:160`, `module_dag.rs:197`, and likely the parser/test-support crates.

tasks.title: Follow-up to merged 1062: tighten orient diagnostic arm and add pos_ok non-zero y/z
tasks.description: Post-merge review follow-up. Task 1062 shipped (merge commit 4cfc63bd7) with two small residual gaps in crates/reify-stdlib/src/orientation.rs tests. 1062 is DONE — this is net-new work, not a duplicate.
tasks.details:
Residual gaps after 1062 merged:

1. orient_per_component_diagnostics (orientation.rs lines 292-303): the loop uses `catch_unwind(case).expect_err("expected ... to panic")`. If a case silently passes (e.g. future regression where the macro stops checking one component), `expect_err` aborts with a label-less message. Replace with explicit match: `match catch_unwind(case) { Err(e) => /* existing label check */, Ok(_) => failures.push(format!("label {label:?}: case did not panic")) }`. Preserves the collect-all-mismatches invariant 1062 introduced.

2. sign_insensitive_macro_fully_populated_quaternion (orientation.rs lines 437-455): currently only exercises neg_ok (actual = -0.5s vs expected = +0.5s). The existing sign_insensitive_macro_positive test uses (1,0,0,0) so pos_ok's `y - $ey` / `z - $ez` comparisons only ever run against zero operands. Add a companion positive-form assertion: actual `Value::Orientation { w: 0.5, x: 0.5, y: 0.5, z: 0.5 }` vs expected `(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5)` under `sign_insensitive = 1e-10`. This was originally in 1062's revised deliverable 3 but was dropped in the amend commit 49d1c4a7c as "redundant" — the reviewer pushed back and the reasoning above shows why it is not redundant.

Source: post-merge reviewer_comprehensive suggestions on esc-1062-3.

tasks.title: Fix deferred-commit overlay escape in module_dag compile_module
tasks.description:
Close a latent overlay-invariant escape in the `StdlibMode` deferred-commit logic (crates/reify-compiler/src/module_dag.rs, approximately lines 189–344 of the current file).

The issue: in the `Ok(path) if is_std_path` arm, when `stdlib_mode` is still `None`, `commit_fs_mode = true` is set but the actual write to `self.stdlib_mode` is deferred until after the module body (and its transitive imports) successfully compile. During the recursive `compile_module` calls for this module's own `import std.Y` statements, the `Err(fs_err) if is_std_path` arm can legitimately find Y only in the embedded stdlib (if Y is missing from `stdlib_root`) and commit `stdlib_mode = Some(Embedded)`. When the outer call returns, the unconditional `if commit_fs_mode { self.stdlib_mode = Some(FileSystem); }` overwrites the Embedded commit — silently mixing a filesystem-loaded outer module with an embedded-loaded inner dependency. This is the exact partial-overlay scenario the new invariant is meant to reject.

Not reachable through the bundled stdlib today (none of `stdlib/*.ri` have `import` statements), but a user-supplied `stdlib_root` whose `.ri` files import other std.* modules will trip it, defeating the invariant.

Two viable fixes (pick one or combine):
(a) Commit `stdlib_mode = Some(FileSystem)` eagerly *before* recursing into imports so the inner embedded-fallback arm sees `Some(FileSystem)` and emits the overlay diagnostic. Trade-off: a parse/compile failure on the outer module would taint mode for subsequent calls; guard this with revert-on-error if that matters.
(b) After the inner recursion returns, re-check `self.stdlib_mode` and emit the partial-overlay diagnostic when `commit_fs_mode == true` but a descendant already switched mode to `Embedded`. Don't silently overwrite.

Add a regression test in `crates/reify-compiler/tests/module_dag_tests.rs` that constructs a user-supplied stdlib dir with one `.ri` file that `import`s another std.* module missing from the dir, and asserts the partial-overlay diagnostic fires.

Memory hints: search "stdlib embedded fallback module_dag StdlibMode partial overlay"; review the two existing overlay tests `partial_stdlib_overlay_errors_when_fs_missing_later_module` and `partial_stdlib_overlay_errors_when_embedded_first_then_fs` in module_dag_tests.rs for setup patterns.

tasks.title: De-hardcode stdlib order in sequential_embedded_fallback test
tasks.description:
Make `sequential_embedded_fallback_no_duplicates_in_topo_order` (crates/reify-compiler/tests/module_dag_tests.rs, approximately lines 1410–1486) robust to stdlib growth.

The test currently hardcodes both the expected stdlib module count (6) and the full name list ("std.units", "std.si_units", "std.materials.mechanical", "std.structural.physical", "std.analysis", "std.tolerancing") at specific positions. The prose comments even reference specific indices ("stdlib index 2", "stdlib index 5"). Any future reordering or extension of the embedded stdlib in `stdlib_loader::load_stdlib()` will break this test in ways unrelated to the backward-walk short-circuit invariant it actually pins.

Fix: derive the expected prefix from `stdlib_loader::load_stdlib()` at test time. Pick two target modules by their index in the loaded stdlib (e.g. position 2 and length-1), call `compile_module` for each, and assert `dag.topo_order` equals the prefix's dotted paths with no duplicates and no gaps. Keeps the actual invariant (insert-prefix + no-dup + no-gap after two overlapping calls) explicit while surviving stdlib reorderings and additions.

Update the prose comments to drop the hardcoded index references.

Memory hints: review `crates/reify-compiler/src/stdlib_loader.rs` for the load_stdlib API; the test itself lives in `crates/reify-compiler/tests/module_dag_tests.rs`.

tasks.title: Tighten diagnostic-substring assertions and pin Display-variant table coverage
tasks.description: Two post-merge polish items from task 1188 review: (a) tighten fragile substring assertions in geometry diagnostic tests; (b) guard the kind_display_tests table against silently-missing variants.
tasks.details:
Follow-up to task 1188 (bundle landing ExtrudeSymmetric per-side threshold + Display impls for geometry op kinds). Two independent test-quality items:

### Item A — Tighten diagnostic substring assertions
Location: `crates/reify-eval/tests/geometry_error_handling.rs` and any tests added in the 1188 bundle that use `diagnostics[0].message.contains("linear")`, `contains("extrude")`, etc.

Problem: short lowercase tokens like `linear` and `extrude` are prefixes of longer op names (`linear_2d`, `extrude_symmetric`). A future refactor that emits the wrong-but-similar op name in the diagnostic would not be caught — the assertion would still pass.

Fix: assert on the full op token with a delimiter. Use `contains("for linear ")` (trailing space), `contains("for extrude ")`, `contains("for extrude_symmetric")`, etc. — or anchor on the full Display output from `PrimitiveKind`/`ModifyKind` to pin the exact op name.

### Item B — Pin Display-variant table against new variants
Location: `crates/reify-compiler/src/types.rs` around the `kind_display_tests` block (roughly lines 745-838 as of task 1188 landing).

Problem: the table-driven Display round-trip tests pin a real contract but do not flag a new enum variant that lacks a Display arm. Rust's match exhaustiveness catches a missing arm in the `impl Display`, but the test table will silently skip any new variant added without updating both sides.

Fix (pick one):
1. Add a `// NOTE: extend this table when adding a new variant to PrimitiveKind/ModifyKind/...` comment above each `check(&[...])` array. Cheap, no new deps.
2. (Preferred if dep cost is acceptable) Thread the variant list through `strum::EnumIter` / `strum::IntoEnumIterator` so the test iterates all variants automatically and fails at compile time when a new one is added.

Check `Cargo.toml` for existing `strum` usage before adding a new dep; if already depended on transitively, option 2 is nearly free.

### Verification
- `cargo test -p reify-eval --test geometry_error_handling`
- `cargo test -p reify-compiler --lib kind_display`
- Add a negative test (feature-gated or doc-test) that would fail if a new variant is added without a Display arm — optional but demonstrates the guard works.

Memory hints:
- search: "diagnostic substring assertion op token delimiter"
- search: "Display variant table strum EnumIter"
- entities: PrimitiveKind, ModifyKind, kind_display_tests, geometry_error_handling.rs

tasks.title: Polish compile_builder: retarget stale comments, factor is_first_def + build_constraint_def_registry helpers
tasks.description:
Post-merge cleanup for task 2035 (compile_builder extraction). Three related items in `crates/reify-compiler/src/compile_builder/`:

1. **Stale `above`/`below` comments in `entities_phase.rs` (L88-145).** The passthrough match arms carry over lib.rs-era text: "Already compiled in pre-pass above", "Compiled in dedicated purpose pass below", etc. After the refactor those phases live in sibling modules — retarget each comment at its module (e.g. `// Already compiled by functions_phase::phase_functions.`, `// Handled later by post_passes::phase_purposes.`).

2. **Duplicate-filter in `entities_phase::compile_entity_decl` (L178-183).** `is_first_def` re-derives duplicate-entity filtering from `seen_entity_names` via span-equality, duplicating pre_pass's detection logic. If the tracker's value shape ever changes (e.g. stops storing first span), Structure/Occurrence duplicates silently double-push or all-skip. Lift into an explicit predicate on `CompilationCtx`, e.g. `fn is_first_entity_def(&self, name: &str, span: SourceSpan) -> bool`, and call it from both pre_pass and entities_phase so the cross-module contract has one source of truth.

3. **Extract `build_constraint_def_registry` helper (entities_phase.rs L54-66).** The prelude-first / local-override rebuild currently inlines in `phase_entities` while `build_trait_registry` is already factored out. Mirror the pattern — `fn build_constraint_def_registry(local: &[CompiledConstraintDef], prelude: &[&CompiledModule]) -> HashMap<String, &CompiledConstraintDef>` — and call it from `phase_entities` (and from `phase_pending_bound_checks` if it grows to need it).

No behavior change expected. `cargo test -p reify-compiler` must pass unchanged.

tasks.title: Document or soften ConstraintChecker labeled-diagnostic drift assert
tasks.description:
The drift debug_assert in labeled_diagnostics (crates/reify-eval/src/engine_constraints.rs:230-245) imposes an implicit contract that any Error-severity diagnostic emitted for a labeled constraint must embed `id.to_string()` verbatim so label substitution has something to replace. Third-party `ConstraintChecker` implementations that emit domain-specific error text (e.g. "wall thickness below minimum") without the raw ConstraintNodeId will panic in debug builds even though runtime output is correct.

Two options: (a) add a doc comment on `ConstraintChecker::check` making the embedding requirement an explicit trait invariant, or (b) demote the assert to `tracing::warn!`/`debug!` so it remains observable but non-fatal for external checkers.

Memory hints: search "labeled_diagnostics drift assert", search "ConstraintChecker trait contract", entities: ConstraintChecker, labeled_diagnostics

tasks.title: Restore positional predicate-order assertion in constraint_inst_label_multi_predicate
tasks.description:
`constraint_inst_label_multi_predicate` (crates/reify-compiler/tests/constraint_inst_tests.rs:413-444) was switched to label-based `.find()` lookups in task 848.2 to make it order-independent across instantiations. However, predicate order WITHIN a single instantiation is a meaningful contract — pred_idx must match source-order of predicates in the def — and the new test no longer exercises that.

Add a supplementary assertion that for a single instantiation the predicates appear in source order (e.g. filter to one inst_idx, verify pred_idx is monotonically increasing starting at 0). Keeps the cross-instantiation order-independence while restoring the intra-instantiation ordering guard.

tasks.title: edit_source: detect guarded-group role flips when cell content_hash is unchanged
tasks.description:
In engine_edit.rs edit_source Phase 1 (lines 1333-1341), `has_dirty_guards` fires on (a) structure_controlling in dirty cone, (b) structure_controlling in changed_set, or (c) an `added` cell that is a current member/else_member. It does NOT fire when a cell's ROLE within an existing guarded group flips — e.g., a source edit moves `let foo` from the `where` branch to the `else` branch — while the cell's `content_hash` is unchanged. In that case the cell is not in `changed` (hash equal), not in `added` (still present), and the guard_cell isn't dirty. Step 12 won't re-evaluate it (not in dirty_cone), and Phase 1 won't run to route it through the deactivate path. The preserved old snapshot value survives on the wrong branch, diverging from cold eval.

Fix options:
1. Compute a per-cell `old_role_in_any_group` vs `new_role_in_any_group` diff using old/new `guarded_groups`, and include the affected member ids in `changed_set` or a trigger condition.
2. Alternatively, make guarded-group membership contribute to ValueCellNode `content_hash` so a role swap surfaces as `changed` naturally.

Add a cross-check test in tests/edit_source.rs that performs exactly this edit (move a let between where/else branches without changing its expr) and compares incremental result to cold eval.

- search: "guarded_groups structure_controlling Phase 1"
- entity: "Engine"

tasks.title: Def-preview activation: invalidate in-flight fetches on external state changes
tasks.description: Close two race-condition gaps in the def-preview activation path, plus a small test-comment cleanup, surfaced by post-merge review of task 1748.
tasks.details:
## Scope

Three suggestions from the reviewer on task 1748's merged work. All three are in the def-preview activation path (`gui/src/stores/defPreviewStore.ts`, `gui/src/hooks/useDefPreviewActivation.ts`, and the related test in `gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx`).

### 1. defPreviewStore.clearPreview() does not invalidate in-flight loadPreview (defPreviewStore.ts:49)

Scenario: user lands inside definition A → activation hook calls `loadPreview('A', slowFetch)` (token=1, awaiting); user moves the cursor out of any definition → activation hook calls `defPreviewStore.clearPreview()` (state.defName ← null); the slow fetch for A then resolves → the token guard still passes (token 1 === latestLoadToken 1) → `applyPreview` re-sets `state.defName = 'A'` and re-populates meshes, even though the user has already left the definition. The hook's own token guard only protects against its own repeated calls, not against an external `clearPreview` while a load is in flight.

**Fix:** Have `clearPreview()` bump `latestLoadToken` (e.g. `++latestLoadToken` before the `setState`) so any in-flight fetch resolves into the stale-branch.

**Test:** Add a regression test that starts a slow `loadPreview`, then calls `clearPreview()`, then resolves the fetch, and asserts `state.defName` remains `null`.

### 2. useDefPreviewActivation token incremented inside setTimeout, not on cursor change (useDefPreviewActivation.ts:75-109)

`latestRequestToken` is incremented inside the `setTimeout` callback (line 93), not when the cursor changes. If the cursor moves *after* a fetch has already started awaiting `getContainingDefinition` but before the next debounce elapses, the in-flight fetch for the previous position will still have `token === latestRequestToken` when it resolves and will be applied (`setDefInfo` + `loadPreview`) even though the user has already moved. A subsequent fetch then applies the fresh result, but there is a brief window where an obsolete def is shown/loaded.

**Fix:** Bump `latestRequestToken` synchronously in the `createEffect` body on every cursor change (e.g. `latestRequestToken++` right after clearing `timerId`). Then compare against the captured token after the await — this invalidates any in-flight request as soon as the cursor moves, not only when a newer timer fires.

**Test:** Add a race test where the cursor moves mid-flight (between the timer firing and `getContainingDefinition` resolving), and assert the stale result is discarded.

### 3. Misleading test comment (App.test.tsx:3055-3074)

The inline comment warns that `advanceTimersByTimeAsync(250)` will also fire the `savePanelLayout` 300ms debounce at `App.tsx:176`, but (a) 300ms > 250ms so it wouldn't fire anyway and (b) that `setTimeout` was scheduled with the real-timer `setTimeout` before `vi.useFakeTimers()` was called, so it is not owned by the fake clock and will not fire on `advanceTimersByTimeAsync` regardless.

**Fix:** Either drop the comment, or replace it with a precise statement: "`vi.useFakeTimers()` is installed after mount, so only timers created post-mount (the activation hook's 200ms debounce) are affected by `advanceTimersByTimeAsync`."

### memory_hints
- search: "defPreviewStore loadPreview token race"
- search: "useDefPreviewActivation debounce"

tasks.title: edit_source Phase 4: invalidate cache for removed collection scoped cells and entirely-removed collection_subs
tasks.description:
In engine_edit.rs edit_source Phase 4 (lines 1690-1817), the code removes collection-instance scoped cells via `new_snapshot.graph.value_cells.remove(&scoped_id)` and `values.remove(&scoped_id)`, but does NOT invalidate `self.cache` entries keyed by `NodeId::Value(scoped_id)`. A subsequent edit that re-adds a collection instance at the same index would find a stale cache entry from the previous incarnation.

Also: a `collection_sub` that is entirely REMOVED by the edit (no longer present in `new_snapshot.graph.collection_subs`) never has its scoped cells visited by Phase 4 at all — their cache entries are never invalidated. Latent because `Snapshot::from_compiled_module` rebuilds `graph.value_cells` from scratch, but `self.cache` is retained across edits.

Fix:
1. In Phase 4's remove loop, call `self.cache.invalidate(&NodeId::Value(scoped_id))` for each removed scoped cell.
2. Add a separate sweep that diffs old/new `graph.collection_subs`; for each entirely-removed collection_sub, enumerate its scoped_ids in the OLD graph and invalidate them.

Regression test (tests/edit_source.rs): two-edit sequence grow collection → shrink → re-grow, assert the re-grown instance's value comes from a fresh evaluation (not a resurrected cache entry).

tasks.title: edit_source: close test coverage gaps (collection count, module-level table refreshes, removed-constraint, realization diffing)
tasks.description:
The tests in crates/reify-eval/tests/edit_source.rs exercise identical-module noop, modified let, added/removed cell, modified/added constraint, param-override preservation and purge, mixed bracket cross-check, guard-expr flip, and added active/inactive members. Gaps to close with cross-check-vs-cold-eval tests:

1. Phase 4 collection count re-elaboration (no edit that changes `count(n) of …` from n to m).
2. Step-11 module-level table refreshes — function added/removed/modified, objective change, purpose change, meta change. A regression in any of the four refreshes (self.functions / compiled_purposes / meta_map / objectives) would ship silently.
3. Removed value cell whose dependents' expressions remain shape-compatible with a fallback branch — exercises the `// skipping it would miss dependents` arm in step 6 at engine_edit.rs:1002-1017.
4. Removed constraint (existing tests cover modified + added only).
5. Realization diffing at any granularity (added/removed/modified) — symmetric with constraint tests.

Each test should call both `engine.edit_source(&new_module)` and a fresh `engine.eval(&new_module)` from a new Engine instance, and assert matching EvalResult values and diagnostics.

tasks.title: edit_source Phases 1 & 3: skip guarded groups whose guard value did not change
tasks.description:
In crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs, Phase 1 (lines 1333-1420, inside `if has_dirty_guards`) and Phase 3 (lines 1611-1676, inside `if guard_changed`) both iterate EVERY guarded group and re-evaluate every member once the outer trigger fires. On a module with N unrelated guarded groups where a single guard resolved differently, each phase does O(N · avg_members) redundant expression evaluations. This matches edit_param so it's not a new regression, but the duplicated work compounds because edit_source may run both Phase 1 AND Phase 3 on the same edit.

Fix: inside the `for group in ...` loop in each phase, compare the freshly-computed (or current-in-`values`) guard value to the pre-edit snapshot value for that SPECIFIC group and `continue` if unchanged. Keep the outer trigger as an early-exit but let the inner loop skip unaffected groups.

Also consider applying the same per-group filtering in edit_param for symmetry.

No new tests strictly required (cross-check tests already guard correctness), but add a perf-oriented assertion: for a module with 10 guarded groups and an edit to 1 guard, count evaluated expressions and assert only the affected group's members were re-evaluated.

- search: "guarded_groups Phase 1 Phase 3 redundant eval"

tasks.title: compile_builder polish: anchor duplicate-diagnostic wire format in tests + clarify constraint-def registry doc
tasks.description:
Two post-merge polish follow-ups from task 2079 review (compile_builder extraction).

1. **Anchor duplicate-diagnostic wire format** — `crates/reify-compiler/src/compile_builder/ctx.rs` test `record_or_report_duplicate_inserts_and_deduplicates` asserts `diagnostics.len() == 1` on the duplicate branch but does not verify the diagnostic's message text or label ordering. This helper is now the single source of truth for the `duplicate entity definition '<name>'` wire format (previously replicated verbatim across four pre_pass branches), so a silent message regression would only be caught by distant integration tests. Extend the test to assert:
   - `diagnostics[0].message` contains `duplicate entity definition 'Widget'`
   - Two labels present in order: first label span == `span_b` with kind `"structure"` (second def), second label span == `span_a` with kind referencing `"first defined as structure here"` (or the exact format).
   This anchors behaviour that was previously enforced by duplication.

2. **Clarify `build_constraint_def_registry` doc comment** — `crates/reify-compiler/src/compile_builder/entities_phase.rs:32-40`. Current doc says local defs "override on name collision" but does not call out that *all* local defs (pub or not) are inserted, only that prelude defs are filtered to pub. Extend the comment: e.g. "All local constraint defs (pub or not) are inserted; non-pub local defs are only reachable within the current module but still shadow prelude defs of the same name." No behaviour change.

Cold-path micro-opt in `record_or_report_duplicate` (the redundant `.get().expect()` after `is_first_entity_def`) was also suggested but dismissed — not hot, and readability cost of `entry()` API rewrite outweighs the saved lookup.

`cargo test -p reify-compiler` must pass.

tasks.title: Harden constraint_def_eval tests: dedupe MinWall fixture, tighten drift target_prefix, add positive-case asserts
tasks.description:
Three review follow-ups for `crates/reify-eval/tests/constraint_def_eval.rs` from task 2082:

1. **Dedupe shared source fixture (code_reuse).** The `constraint def MinWall { param wall: Length; wall > 2 } structure S { param thickness: Length = 1; constraint MinWall(wall: thickness) }` source literal is now copy-pasted in three tests (lines 20–30 `violated_constraint_def_produces_labeled_diagnostic`, 547–560 `non_embedding_checker_does_not_panic_on_labeled_constraint`, 604–613 `drift_signal_fires_for_non_embedding_checker`). Hoist to a module-level `const MIN_WALL_SOURCE: &str = ...` (or `fn min_wall_source() -> &'static str`) and consume from all three tests so the structure name / params can't desync.

2. **Tighten drift test target filter (test_robustness).** `drift_signal_fires_for_non_embedding_checker` (line 596–598) uses `.target_prefix("reify_eval")`, which would start counting spuriously if any unrelated `tracing::debug!` is added elsewhere in the `reify-eval` crate. Replace with the more specific `.target_prefix("reify_eval::engine_constraints")` so the assertion pins the drift branch specifically.

3. **Add positive-case asserts in non-panic test (test_coverage).** `non_embedding_checker_does_not_panic_on_labeled_constraint` (lines 568–579) verifies the domain message pass-through but doesn't assert the ConstraintCheckEntry itself. Add: `assert_eq!(result.constraint_results.len(), 1)`, `assert_eq!(entry.satisfaction, Satisfaction::Violated)`, and `assert_eq!(entry.label, Some("MinWall#0[0]".to_string()))`. This directly asserts the trait-docstring guarantee that third-party checkers still get the friendly label attached even when their message omits the raw id.

tasks.title: edit_source: add symmetric inactive→active role-flip test (reverse of task 2084 lock)
tasks.description:
Task 2084's lock `edit_source_role_flipped_guard_member_matches_cold_eval` in crates/reify-eval/tests/edit_source.rs only covers the active→inactive direction of the role flip (a member that was Determined becoming Undef). The symmetric case — a member previously suppressed to Undef on the inactive branch becoming a live Determined value on the active branch — is not pinned.

Since the fix relies on Phase 1 running the full activation/deactivation pass for the whole group, the symmetric case exercises a different path in `deactivate_if_not_auto` / activation that is worth a dedicated lock.

Add a companion test where `moving` starts on the else-branch (Undef under `use_thick=true`), then in module B moves to the where-branch. Assert cold and incremental agree on the now-Determined value. Reuse `parse_and_compile` / `fresh_engine` for minimal extra code. Mirror the dual-assertion pattern (relative + positive anchor) from the existing test so a "both wrong" regression cannot slip through.

tasks.title: edit_source: debug_assert role-map uniqueness in role-flip probe
tasks.description:
In crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs around lines 1371-1380, the role-flip probe builds `old_roles: HashMap<ValueCellId, (ValueCellId, u8)>` via `old_roles.insert(mid.clone(), ...)`, which silently overwrites if a ValueCellId ever appears in multiple groups/branches in the old graph. The invariant that a cell belongs to at most one (group, branch) is presumably enforced elsewhere, but if it were ever violated the role map would be silently wrong and Phase 1 could skip firing. Blast radius is small but the invariant is currently implicit at this call site.

Fix: wrap the two `insert` calls (members and else_members) with `debug_assert!(old_roles.insert(mid.clone(), ...).is_none(), "ValueCellId {:?} appeared in multiple guarded-group roles", mid);` (or equivalent) so invariant breaks surface during development with no release runtime cost.

tasks.title: edit_source: defer role-flip probe HashMap build behind short-circuit
tasks.description:
In crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs around lines 1367-1407, `has_role_flipped_guard_member` is computed eagerly before the `||` chain at line 1404-1407, so the HashMap build + walk over old/new guarded_groups runs on every edit even when the `structure_controlling` dirtiness branch or `has_added_guard_member` would already set `has_dirty_guards=true`. On models with many guarded-group members this is wasted work on the hot edit path.

Fix: defer the role-flip probe to run only when earlier triggers did not fire. Restructure so the probe runs via an inline closure/IIFE after the cheaper `structure_controlling_dirty || has_added_guard_member` check short-circuits. Preserves correctness; task 2084's lock `edit_source_role_flipped_guard_member_matches_cold_eval` still pins behavior.

Scope note: distinct from task 2088 (which filters inside the Phase 1/3 loops by per-group guard value); this is about the outer trigger evaluation only.

tasks.title: Pipe kernel: orient profile to path start-tangent (or reject non-Z paths)
tasks.description: Follow-up from task 324 review. The Pipe kernel arm always creates its circular profile face in the XY plane at z=0 (+Z normal). Any path whose start-tangent is not aligned with +Z silently produces a degenerate zero-volume solid — execute() returns Ok with no diagnostic. `pipe(arc(...), r)` or `pipe(bezier(...), r)` produce invalid output with zero user feedback. The kernel_pipe_x_axis_path_degenerate_solid test currently pins this broken behavior as intended, turning a real correctness bug into a locked-in contract.
tasks.details:
## Fix options

(a) **Correct fix**: Reorient the profile face to the path's start-tangent frame inside the Pipe kernel arm (crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/lib.rs:570-582). The existing docstring acknowledges this as the eventual direction.

(b) **Interim fix**: Inspect the path's start-tangent before swinging and return `GeometryError::OperationFailed("pipe currently only supports paths whose start-tangent is +Z")` for non-Z-aligned paths. This at least surfaces a diagnostic instead of silent garbage geometry.

## Test follow-up

Update `kernel_pipe_x_axis_path_degenerate_solid` in crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/lib.rs (~line 4330) from an Ok-with-zero-volume pin into a `should_panic`/error-expectation test, so the silent-failure path is not enshrined as desired behavior.

## Doc

Update `GeometryOp::Pipe` doc in crates/reify-types/src/geometry.rs to reflect the final constraint (either general orientation supported, or Z-only with explicit error).

## Why this matters

pipe() is a user-facing surface-language primitive (registered in units.rs, compile arm, e2e test). Silent garbage geometry from non-Z paths is a meaningful UX/correctness hazard that downstream consumers (STEP export, tessellation) happily accept.

## memory_hints
- search: "Pipe kernel arm orientation start-tangent"
- entity: GeometryOp::Pipe

Introduce a small private helper — e.g. `fn take_trace(traces: &mut HashMap<NodeId, DependencyTrace>, node_id: &NodeId) -> DependencyTrace` — shared between engine_eval.rs and unfold.rs (or an inline closure) so the remove+expect and its rationale live in one place.

tasks.title: engine_edit: fix role-flip probe count mismatch with intra-group duplicates
tasks.description:
The role-flip probe in `Engine::edit_source` (crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs around line 1435) ends with `flipped || new_total != old_roles.len()`, where `new_total` is a raw per-member iteration count (no dedup) while `old_roles.len()` reflects a deduped HashMap. When both old and new guarded-groups contain the *same* intra-group duplicate (same ValueCellId in both members and else_members of one group — an affirmatively-supported shape per `build_old_role_map`'s docstring), `new_total` counts it twice but `old_roles.len()` counts it once, so the probe falsely reports a role-flip and forces an unnecessary guard re-eval.

Spotted by reviewer_comprehensive after task 2092's intra-group-duplicate test (`build_old_role_map_intra_group_duplicate_last_write_wins`) pinned the helper's last-write-wins contract — that test documents the helper but sidesteps this callsite mismatch.

Fix options (pick one):
  (a) Track a HashSet<ValueCellId> of seen IDs during the new-groups walk and compare `seen.len() != old_roles.len()` after the loop (apples-to-apples dedup).
  (b) Have `build_old_role_map` return `(map, pre_dedup_insert_count)` so the probe compares raw-vs-raw counts.

Add a regression test that constructs old and new graphs each containing the identical intra-group duplicate and asserts the probe does NOT report a role-flip (currently it would).

Fix options: (a) track HashSet of seen IDs during new-groups walk, compare seen.len() vs old_roles.len(); or (b) have build_old_role_map return (map, pre_dedup_count) so probe compares raw-vs-raw.

Add regression test with identical intra-group duplicate in both old and new graphs; assert probe does NOT report role-flip.

(b) **Document as intentional stable FFI.** Update the header comment on `make_polyline_wire` to state explicitly that it is part of the stable FFI surface for future polyline use cases, not just CompCurve testing — the current comment only justifies it for the tests.

tasks.title: Pipe start-tangent guard follow-ups: NaN rejection, tolerance in error, -Z test case
tasks.description: Three polish fixes co-located around the task-2095 Pipe start-tangent guard in crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/lib.rs. All strictly tighten the rejection contract; no behavior change for accepted (+Z) paths.
tasks.details:
Post-merge review of task 2095 (reviewer_comprehensive) flagged three related cleanups on the Pipe start-tangent guard:

1. **NaN rejection (lib.rs:588-591).** The guard `if t.z < 1.0 - 1e-6` silently accepts NaN components because any comparison with NaN is false. `wire_start_tangent` already errors on zero magnitude and OCCT is unlikely to emit NaN, but defense-in-depth: flip to `!(t.z >= 1.0 - 1e-6)` (rejects NaN) or add an explicit `t.x.is_finite() && t.y.is_finite() && t.z.is_finite()` precheck before the tangent comparison.

2. **Include tolerance in the runtime error (lib.rs:593-597).** The docstring says the constraint is "+Z (within 1e-6)" but the runtime error string drops the tolerance. Include it so callers hitting FP noise can tell whether their path is close-but-rejected vs. far-off (e.g. extend the message with "expected tangent.z > 0.999999").

3. **Add -Z case to kernel_pipe_non_z_start_tangent_returns_error (lib.rs:4343-4368).** The test currently covers +X, +Y, and an XY-plane arc. A start-tangent ≈ (0,0,-1) also fails the `t.z > 1-1e-6` check and is worth pinning: -Z is the single case where a user might reasonably expect pipe() to "just work" (profile plane still aligned, direction flipped). Pinning makes the contract explicit; if -Z is ever accepted in a future orientation-generalization pass, this test becomes the forcing function.

Triage context preserved in memory 0bb8ede0-45ea-426f-bbf6-903f7a1a7850 (observations_and_summaries, agent_id=claude-task-2095-steward).

tasks.title: Document/test duplicate let-binding shadowing semantics in named_steps
tasks.description:
`crates/reify-eval/src/engine_build.rs:332-345` (in `execute_realization_ops`) — two `let` bindings with the same name within a single structure will silently overwrite each other in `named_steps` (HashMap::insert); any subsequent `GeomRef::Sub(name)` then resolves to the latest binding. This may be intended shadowing semantics, but (a) it is not documented and (b) it is not tested — a regression flipping to first-write-wins would go unnoticed.

Fix: either (1) add a one-line comment noting that duplicate names here are impossible because the compiler rejects them earlier (cite the rejection site); or (2) if shadowing IS allowed at compile time, add a test that two `let body = …` bindings resolve `Sub("body")` to the second one, and document the decision in the doc comment on `execute_realization_ops`.

tasks.title: Thread realization span into execute_realization_ops so per-op diagnostics carry source spans
tasks.description:
`crates/reify-eval/src/geometry_ops.rs:253-257` — on an unknown `Sub(name)` lookup the error message is `unresolvable GeomRef::Sub('{name}') — no such named sub-reference in scope`. This is surfaced via `Diagnostic::error(format!("failed to compile geometry operation: {}", err))` with no source span (the caller uses `SourceSpan::new(0,0)` everywhere upstream), so a user with a typo in `difference(body, hoel)` gets no indication of which file/line.

Not specific to `Sub(name)` — all surrounding diagnostics share the same span-less shape. Fix: thread the realization's `span` into `execute_realization_ops` so per-op diagnostics can carry a span. Self-acknowledged follow-up from Task 2011 review (reviewer explicitly marked it out of scope).

tasks.title: Pipe start-tangent helper polish: drop wording-pin test, dedupe doc, optional upper-bound guard
tasks.description: Three small follow-ups from the post-merge review of task 2106 in crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/lib.rs.
tasks.details:
Post-merge review (reviewer_comprehensive) of task-2106's changes to validate_pipe_start_tangent flagged three small polish items:

1. **Delete the wording-pin test** (lib.rs:4444-4460, `validate_pipe_start_tangent_error_mentions_tolerance`). Its sole assertion is `msg.contains("tolerance")`. It exercises no behavior beyond message prose — no tolerance value, no branch selection, no coordinate reporting. Rewording the message (e.g. "accepted margin %e") would break the test without changing any contract; conversely a real correctness regression (wrong comparator/branch) would slip past it. The adjacent `validate_pipe_start_tangent_rejects_negative_z` test already covers the not-+Z branch with a stronger assertion. Action: delete the test.

2. **Trim doc duplication** (lib.rs:98-103 + 106-109 + 602-609). The unit-vector residual derivation now lives on `PIPE_START_TANGENT_Z_EPSILON`'s doc comment, the helper's rustdoc, and the inline comment at the Pipe arm call site. Keep the residual derivation on the constant only; let the helper's rustdoc describe behavior; trim the call-site inline comment to a one-line pointer (e.g. "Reject paths whose start-tangent is not approximately +Z; see validate_pipe_start_tangent."). Low priority.

3. **Optional upper-bound guard** (lib.rs:108-124, `validate_pipe_start_tangent`). After filtering NaN/Inf, the comparator `t.z < 1.0 - ε` accepts arbitrarily large finite `t.z` (e.g. 1e100) and does not enforce the unit-vector invariant the doc rationale depends on. Cheap defense against upstream FFI bugs in `wire_start_tangent`: add `|| t.z > 1.0 + PIPE_START_TANGENT_Z_EPSILON` to the guard, or assert `(t.x*t.x + t.y*t.y + t.z*t.z - 1.0).abs() < 1e-3` defensively. Not blocking; pre-refactor code had the same gap.

All three are co-located polish on the helper landed in commits 5be841671..d087ef970 (task 2106). No behavior change for accepted (+Z) paths.

tasks.description: Polish refactor in crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs: extract the duplicated guard re-elaboration loop into a helper, and add a borrow-scope comment on the hoisted eval_state binding.
tasks.details:
Two follow-ups from task 471's review (esc-471-63, suggestions 2 and 3 from reviewer_comprehensive):

**(A) Extract `reelaborate_guarded_group` helper** (engine_edit.rs ~452-483 and ~693-723):
After task 471's consolidation, the two members/else_members re-elaboration blocks are now structurally identical save for `graph` vs `new_snapshot.graph` and small wording differences — one in the Phase 1 guard re-elaboration (~line 452), one in the post-resolution phase (~line 693). Extract a private helper with signature roughly:
```
fn reelaborate_guarded_group(
    graph: &Graph,
    group: &GuardedGroup,
    guard_val: &Value,
    values: &mut ValueMap,
    snapshot_values: &mut SnapshotValues,
    ctx: &ReelaborateCtx,  // meta_map, functions, etc.
)
```
Both call sites then reduce to one line. Verify by diffing the two blocks and confirming behavior is preserved (eval_expr call, values.insert, deactivate_if_not_auto branch, DeterminacyState::Determined for active cells).

**(B) Borrow-scope comment at engine_edit.rs:933**:
Task 471 hoisted `let eval_state = self.eval_state.as_ref().unwrap();` out of the step-8 scope. The code relies on Rust's disjoint-field borrow checking to coexist with later `self.param_overrides.retain(...)` (~1132) and `self.cache.invalidate(...)` (~1148+). This works today but is non-obvious. Add a brief comment at line 933 noting that `eval_state` is a disjoint-field borrow of `self.eval_state`, used only for reads into old snapshot state, and that the disjoint-field rule lets later mutable borrows of sibling fields coexist.

Consider doing (A) first so the post-helper call sites are cleaner before adding the comment in (B).

tasks.title: Add Rust-side drift test for layered make_line_wire length floor
tasks.description: Guard the Rust vs C++ layered minimum-length floor in make_line_wire against future drift: add a test that fails if the Rust-layer floor becomes >= the C++ floor.
tasks.details:
Context: task 383 S2 set a C++ degeneracy floor of 1e-10 m² in crates/reify-kernel-occt/cpp/occt_wrapper.cpp (see comment at ~lines 124-132), while Rust applies a stricter 1e-12 m² floor upstream. The layered design means the Rust guard must stay strictly smaller than the C++ guard; if a future edit loosens the Rust guard above 1e-10, the C++ guard becomes the effective limit silently with no regression signal.

Work:
- Expose CPP_LINE_WIRE_MIN_LENGTH_SQ to Rust via a C-exported const (preferred) or mirror it in Rust with a comment pointing to the exact C++ source location.
- Add a unit test in crates/reify-kernel-occt that asserts the Rust-layer floor is strictly smaller than the C++ floor (e.g. `assert!(RUST_MIN_LEN_SQ < cpp_min_len_sq())`).
- Update the C++ comment to reference the test by name so future edits on either side know where to look.

Modules: crates/reify-kernel-occt/cpp/occt_wrapper.cpp, crates/reify-kernel-occt/cpp/occt_wrapper.h, crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/ffi.rs, crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/lib.rs

tasks.title: DualViewport internal Splitter has no-op onResize — drags are silent
tasks.description:
In gui/src/viewport/DualViewport.tsx:130, the horizontal Splitter rendered between the def-preview and design-main viewports passes `onResize={() => {}}`. The Splitter component (gui/src/components/Splitter.tsx:32,57) calls `props.onResize(delta)` on both mousedown-drag and ArrowDown/ArrowUp keypresses; with a no-op handler the splitter looks draggable (cursor changes, role=separator, focusable) but does nothing.

Every other Splitter in the app wires a real handler (App.tsx:770 handleLeftResize, App.tsx:791 handleRightResize, App.tsx:816 handleSideResize). The DualViewport one is the only no-op, which creates a UX inconsistency: a user grabbing it gets the drag cursor and accomplishes nothing.

Two reasonable fixes:
1. Wire a real handler that adjusts the def-preview / design-main row split (probably storing the ratio in viewportStore) so the splitter actually resizes.
2. If the dual-viewport row split is intentionally fixed for v0.1, hide or visually disable the Splitter (drop role=separator, drop tabindex, drop the cursor-change CSS) so it does not advertise interactivity.

Modules: gui/src/viewport/DualViewport.tsx, gui/src/viewport, gui/src/components/Splitter.tsx, gui/src/stores/viewportStore.ts

tasks.title: engine_edit: extend reelaborate_guarded_group tests for no-default_expr and absent-cell branches
tasks.description: Add test coverage for two explicitly documented but currently untested branches of reelaborate_guarded_group in crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs.
tasks.details:

  - active member with default_expr
  - inactive non-Auto member (→ Undef/Undetermined)
  - inactive Auto member (→ absent)

The docstring states: "Cells without a default_expr (or absent from the graph) are left unchanged". Neither currently has an assertion. A regression that replaced `if let Some(node)` with an unconditional insert would escape the tests.

2. **Active member absent from graph.value_cells** — include an id in `members` that is NOT inserted into graph.value_cells, assert both maps return None for it.

Both cases exercise the early-exit path where reelaborate_guarded_group must be a no-op on the cell.

tasks.title: Harden has_depth_tagged assertion in recursive_unfold.rs against diagnostic rewording
tasks.description:
From task 461 review — the `has_depth_tagged` assertion in crates/reify-eval/tests/recursive_unfold.rs:2310-2320 pins the specific wording prefix `recursive sub "oops" in "`. Because engine_eval.rs also emits a root-level diagnostic for `oops`, the test relies on subtle wording differences between two message paths to distinguish Phase-2 from root-level emission. Any benign rewording (single-quote variant, "within" vs. "in") could silently flip the test to pass for the wrong reason.

Fix: anchor the assertion on a structural invariant unique to the Phase-2 path — the `at depth N` (N≥1) substring, which cannot be emitted by the engine_eval root-level path. Suggested form:

    d.message.contains("at depth 1") && d.message.contains("oops") && d.message.contains("Nonexistent")

Reviewer: reviewer_comprehensive (task 461 review).

tasks.title: Unify make_line_wire floor constants as single-source Rust const with compile-time drift check
tasks.description: Replace the runtime FFI drift-guard test for make_line_wire floors with a compile-time invariant, eliminating the test-only FFI accessor leaking into production binaries.
tasks.details:
Context: Task 2123 added a runtime drift-guard test (`rust_line_wire_floor_strictly_below_cpp_floor`) that reads the C++ `CPP_LINE_WIRE_MIN_LENGTH_SQ` through a new FFI accessor `cpp_line_wire_min_length_sq()`. Reviewer flagged two concerns:

1. (ffi.rs:271) The accessor is declared in the production cxx-bridge with no `#[cfg(test)]` gate — comment-only enforcement of TEST-ONLY tends to drift, and the symbol is linked into release binaries.
2. (occt_wrapper.cpp:136 + lib.rs:80) Both floor constants are duplicated across the language boundary, and a whole FFI shim + runtime test exists purely to detect drift. A single-source-of-truth design removes the drift class entirely.

Preferred redesign:
- Promote `CPP_LINE_WIRE_MIN_LENGTH_SQ` to a Rust `const` alongside `RUST_LINE_WIRE_MIN_LENGTH_SQ`.
- Pass the C++ floor value to the C++ layer via one of: (a) generated header written by build.rs, (b) `extern "Rust"` fn that C++ calls at startup to init a static, or (c) compile-time cxx constant export if supported in the project's cxx version.
- Replace the runtime test with `const _: () = assert!(RUST_LINE_WIRE_MIN_LENGTH_SQ < CPP_LINE_WIRE_MIN_LENGTH_SQ);`.
- Remove the `cpp_line_wire_min_length_sq()` FFI accessor (both the bridge decl in `src/ffi.rs:~271` and the C++ impl in `cpp/occt_wrapper.cpp:~140`).
- Update comments in `occt_wrapper.cpp:~132-135` and `lib.rs:~68-80` to reflect the new single-source design.

Keep the existing behavioral tests (`ffi_make_line_wire_rejects_sub_epsilon_length`) intact.

tasks.title: Harden DualViewport tests and add containerRef null guard
tasks.description:
Post-merge robustness follow-ups from task 2124 review. Four related tweaks to DualViewport.tsx and DualViewport.test.tsx:

1. **Test (l) jsdom flex-shorthand coupling** — `gui/src/__tests__/viewport/DualViewport.test.tsx:613` asserts on the `flex` shorthand string (`'0.3 0 0%'`). jsdom's CSSOM has been inconsistent about parsing/round-tripping the `flex` shorthand; if it normalizes the basis (`0%` → `0px`) or drops tokens, this test will flake. Switch to asserting on `flexGrow` directly: `expect(defWrapper.style.flexGrow).toBe('0.3')` and similar for design. Keep a separate assertion that the style prop is non-empty to cover the single-viewport fallback.

2. **Test mock fidelity for setSplitRatio** — `gui/src/__tests__/viewport/DualViewport.test.tsx:114-119` mock's `setSplitRatio` clamps via Math.min/Math.max but skips the real store's `Number.isFinite` guard. If a future DualViewport change ever produces NaN (e.g. 0/0 when container height is measured as NaN) the mock silently stores NaN while the real store no-ops — tests pass, production breaks. Add `if (!Number.isFinite(ratio)) return false;` at the top of the mock, matching viewportStore.ts:188. (Alt: import the real `createViewportStore` and spy on it instead of re-implementing.)

3. **handleDualResize containerRef null guard** — `gui/src/viewport/DualViewport.tsx:92-97` reads `containerRef.clientHeight` and checks `h <= 0` but not `containerRef` itself. Safe today because Solid assigns `let containerRef!` during JSX render before any splitter event, but fragile under deferred callbacks in tests or SSR. Change to `if (!containerRef || containerRef.clientHeight <= 0) return;` — same cost, explicit null coverage.

4. **Clamp end-to-end coverage** — `gui/src/__tests__/viewport/DualViewport.test.tsx:476-501` has no test exercising an end-to-end clamp through the real store. Add a test that imports the real `createViewportStore`, passes `onResize(1000)` with h=400, and asserts the resulting `state.splitRatio` is clamped to 0.9. (Naturally composes with change #2 above — if you switch to the real store there, this test is almost free.)

These are all tiny, scoped to the DualViewport module. Suggest handling together since #2 and #4 interact.

tasks.title: engine_edit tests: rename run_active_case and add symmetric guard=false coverage
tasks.description: Follow-up from task 2125 review (esc-2125-73). Two related test-quality improvements to the reelaborate_guarded_group tests in crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs.
tasks.details:
Two small, independent improvements surfaced in the task 2125 post-merge review:

1. **Rename helper `run_active_case` → `run_with_guard_true`.**
   Location: crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs:1968 (definition) and all call sites.
   Rationale: the helper hardcodes `guard_val = Value::Bool(true)` and is used by tests that exercise *both* the active branch (members) and the inactive branch (else_members under guard=true), e.g. `reelaborate_guarded_group_inactive_else_member_without_default_expr_is_deactivated`. The current name implies "active branch" which is only half the story. A name that describes the input (`guard_val`) rather than the narrative is more accurate.

2. **Add symmetric guard=false coverage** for the four new no-op / deactivation tests added in task 2125.
   The four new tests at lines ~2243–2410 cover only `guard_val = Bool(true)`:
     - active member with default_expr=None
     - active member absent from graph
     - inactive else_member with default_expr=None
     - inactive else_member absent from graph
   Under `guard_val = Bool(false)` the roles flip (`members` → inactive, `else_members` → active). A regression that diverged the two halves of the shared `for (cells, is_active) in [(&group.members, is_true), (&group.else_members, is_false)]` loop would not be caught by the current tests — the general guard=false test `reelaborate_guarded_group_activates_else_members_when_guard_false` only covers the happy path.

   Either: (a) add four mirror tests covering guard=false × {active member missing default_expr, active member absent, inactive member no default_expr, inactive member absent}; (b) parameterize the eight cases via a helper that takes `guard_val` + role assignments; or (c) add a short comment at the top of the `guard_val = Bool(true)` test cluster explaining that symmetry is enforced by the shared loop and hence only one direction is pinned, and dismiss the coverage gap deliberately.

tasks.title: Refactor DualViewport test mock viewportStore to wrap real createViewportStore
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 2130 review. `gui/src/__tests__/viewport/DualViewport.test.tsx:114-124` defines `makeViewportStore` with a hand-rolled `setSplitRatio` that now duplicates the real store's `Number.isFinite` guard and `[0.1, 0.9]` clamp. This duplication is a drift risk: if `viewportStore.ts:188` grows additional validation (e.g. rejects negatives under a new policy, changes clamp bounds), the mock will silently diverge and tests that rely on the mock for store behavior will pass while production behavior differs.

Two possible fixes (reviewer_comprehensive):
(a) Wrap the real `createViewportStore` in `makeViewportStore` and only spy on its methods — single source of truth for store semantics.
(b) Keep the hand-rolled mock but add a direct equivalence test that asserts the mock's `setSplitRatio` behaves identically to the real one for representative inputs (NaN, Infinity, clamp boundaries at 0.1/0.9, normal values).

Preferred approach: (a) — it makes the drift impossible by construction. The real store is already imported in k5/k6/k7, so the machinery is there.

Scope: only `gui/src/__tests__/viewport/DualViewport.test.tsx`. Verify full DualViewport test file still passes after refactor.
