tasks.title: Decouple make_line_wire Rust-guard test from C++ error wording via marker token
tasks.description: Replace fragile substring matching in the above-floor assertion of `make_line_wire_rust_guard_boundary` (crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/lib.rs ~4748-4766) with a stable Rust-side marker, eliminating implicit coupling to C++ layer wording.
tasks.details:
## Context
Task 2129 added a behavioral test anchoring the Rust-layer `RUST_LINE_WIRE_MIN_LENGTH_SQ` floor. The above-floor assertion uses negative-substring matching on "coincident"/"zero length" (NOT present) to verify the Rust guard did not fire. The test's own NOTE comment (lib.rs:4751-4758) documents this fragility: if the C++ rejection message in cpp/occt_wrapper.cpp:812 is ever reworded to include either phrase (e.g. "coincident endpoints"), the test reports a false negative.

Reviewer (reviewer_comprehensive) flagged this as test_robustness and recommended the Rust-side marker approach over maintaining sentinel-word discipline across two languages. Reviewer also noted (test_coverage) that `OCCT_AVAILABLE` currently gates the entire test, but the below-floor case is independent of OCCT (the Rust guard at lib.rs:693 returns before any FFI call) — so the primary invariant goes uncovered on OCCT-less builds.

## Work
1. Prefix the Rust-guard `OperationFailed` message at `crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/lib.rs:694-695` with a stable marker, e.g. `"[rust-guard] line_segment endpoints are coincident (zero length)"`.
2. Update the below-floor assertion in `make_line_wire_rust_guard_boundary` to assert on the `[rust-guard]` marker instead of lower-cased substrings.
3. Update the above-floor assertion to assert the `[rust-guard]` marker is absent (removes the coupling to C++ wording entirely).
4. Restructure the test so the below-floor case runs unconditionally (no `OCCT_AVAILABLE` gate) — the Rust guard returns before FFI, and the primary invariant is the Rust-side guard. Gate only the above-floor half on `OCCT_AVAILABLE`. Verify `OcctKernel::new()` is safe without OCCT; factor out guard invocation if not.
5. Remove the NOTE comment once the coupling is gone.
6. Update/remove any other callers or tests that assert on the plain-text Rust-guard message (grep for "coincident" / "zero length" across the crate).

## Acceptance
- The test no longer mentions C++ wording.
- Reworking the C++ message in occt_wrapper.cpp does not break the Rust-guard test.
- Below-floor assertion runs on OCCT-less CI.

## Dismissed reviewer suggestions
- code_duplication (lib.rs:4711,4743 — two `OcctKernel::new()` calls): cosmetic, dismissed as noise. May be folded opportunistically while making the above changes.

memory_hints: search "task 2129 make_line_wire rust-guard boundary test", search "RUST_LINE_WIRE_MIN_LENGTH_SQ marker token".

tasks.title: Refine Rust-guard marker exposure and boundary test coverage
tasks.description: Three post-merge reviewer refinements to the line_wire Rust-guard marker work landed in task 2135: expose RUST_GUARD_MARKER beyond pub(crate) so the integration test can import it, add a positive above-floor unit test that runs without OCCT, and trim an outdated doc block on rust_guard_above_floor_does_not_fire.
tasks.details:
## Context
Task 2135 introduced a `[rust-guard]` marker in `line_segment_rust_guard`'s error message to decouple boundary tests from C++ error wording. Reviewer (reviewer_comprehensive) flagged three follow-ups after merge:

## Work

1. **Expose `RUST_GUARD_MARKER` as `pub`** (code_reuse).
   - File: `crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/floor_constants.rs:41`.
   - Currently `pub(crate) const RUST_GUARD_MARKER: &str = "[rust-guard]"`. The integration test `crates/reify-kernel-occt/tests/curve_constructors_integration.rs:31` lives in an external test crate and therefore can't see `pub(crate)` items, so it hardcodes the literal `"[rust-guard]"`. The constant is now duplicated in three places (definition, in-crate tests via `use`, and the integration test literal).
   - Change the visibility to `pub` (and re-export it via the crate root as needed — note `mod floor_constants;` in `lib.rs` — so that `reify_kernel_occt::RUST_GUARD_MARKER` or `reify_kernel_occt::floor_constants::RUST_GUARD_MARKER` resolves from the integration test).
   - Update `curve_constructors_integration.rs:31` to `import` and use the constant in the `contains(...)` assertion and the error message string.
   - Confirm the `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotation is still appropriate once visibility widens.

2. **Add an `above_floor_passes` unit test** in `floor_constants.rs` (test_coverage).
   - Symmetric to the existing `below_floor_rejects_with_rust_guard_marker` (floor_constants.rs:94–117) and to the OCCT-gated `rust_guard_above_floor_does_not_fire` (lib.rs:4667).
   - Assert `line_segment_rust_guard((1.1 * RUST_LINE_WIRE_MIN_LENGTH_SQ).sqrt(), 0.0, 0.0).is_ok()`.
   - Runs unconditionally (no OCCT required); catches an inverted predicate (`>` swapped for `<`) regression.

3. **Trim the doc block** on `rust_guard_above_floor_does_not_fire` (test_coverage/style).
   - File: `crates/reify-kernel-occt/src/lib.rs:4667`.
   - The comment (lib.rs:4668–4689) spends more lines re-describing the below-floor coverage (now in the other two tests) than describing what *this* test asserts. Trim to ~4–6 lines focusing on: (a) the above-floor assertion and why Ok isn't *required* (CPP floor is 100× above Rust floor, so C++ still rejects), (b) a single-line pointer to the two below-floor tests.

## Acceptance
- `RUST_GUARD_MARKER` has a single source of truth; integration test imports it rather than duplicating the literal.
- `cargo test -p reify-kernel-occt --lib` (no OCCT) runs both `below_floor_rejects_with_rust_guard_marker` and the new `above_floor_passes`.
- `cargo test -p reify-kernel-occt` (with OCCT) continues to pass.
- The `rust_guard_above_floor_does_not_fire` doc block is concise and matches the test's scope.

## Dismissed
None — all three suggestions accepted.

tasks.title: Restrict last_guard_phase_group_evals() to test-only surface
tasks.description:
`Engine::last_guard_phase_group_evals()` (crates/reify-eval/src/engine_admin.rs:209 + field at lib.rs:215) is a pub accessor whose sole purpose is test instrumentation for the per-group skip optimisation added in task 2088. Exposing a performance counter on the public API couples callers to an internal optimisation and risks external users treating it as a supported metric.

Fix: gate the accessor behind `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-instrumentation"))]` or downgrade to `pub(crate)` with a test-only re-export, OR rename to something general (e.g. `last_guard_reelaboration_count`) and document it as a stable inspection metric rather than a skip-optimisation probe. Reviewer (reviewer_comprehensive) prefers option (a).

Affected sites: same treatment for the `last_guard_phase_group_evals: usize` field declaration and any doc-comment updates.

Source: post-merge review suggestion on task 2088 (merge commit 90804da34).

tasks.title: Tighten per-group guard-skip perf-lock: add Phase 1-only, Phase 3-only, added-member, and role-flip assertions
tasks.description:
The perf-lock tests added by task 2088 in crates/reify-eval/tests/edit_source.rs:1900-2023 and crates/reify-eval/tests/guard_eval.rs:2048-2155 only exercise the case where Phase 1 and Phase 3 BOTH fire for a single group (counter == 2). They do NOT lock the intentionally-conservative branches of the skip condition.

Add assertions for:
(a) Phase 1 firing while Phase 3 does NOT — e.g. guard expression edited but resulting value unchanged (`where u3` → `where u3 && true`); expected counter reflects Phase 1 only.
(b) Phase 3 firing while Phase 1 does NOT — edit_param on a non-guard input that propagates through a computed guard without any structure_controlling dirtiness path.
(c) Skip correctly SUPPRESSED when a member is added to a group whose guard value is unchanged (counter == 1 because `has_added_in_group` forces non-skip).
(d) Role-flip suppression path via `has_role_flipped_guard_member` — counter == 1.

These are cheap and lock the conservative clauses so a regression (e.g. dropping `has_added_in_group` or the role-flip clause, or dropping Phase-1-only reset) would be caught. Correctness tests exist elsewhere (e.g. `edit_source_role_flipped_guard_member_matches_cold_eval`), so this gap is performance coverage only.

Source: post-merge review suggestion on task 2088 (merge commit 90804da34).

tasks.title: Extract guard_value_unchanged helper to dedupe old_guard_val lookup in engine_edit
tasks.description:
The same `old_guard_val` lookup is duplicated verbatim across three sites in crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs (around lines 516, 744, and 1492/1759), each pulling `self.eval_state.as_ref().and_then(|s| s.snapshot.values.get(&group.guard_cell)).map(|(v, _)| v)` with near-identical comment blocks. Phase 3 edit_param (~744) and Phase 3 edit_source (~1759) skip logic is identical; Phase 1 edit_param and Phase 1 edit_source differ only by the added/role-flip clause.

Fix: extract a small helper like `fn guard_value_unchanged(&self, guard_cell: &ValueCellId, new_val: &Value) -> bool` on Engine (or a free fn scoped by eval_state). Call it from all three sites. Phase 1 of edit_source can layer the `has_added_in_group || has_role_flipped_guard_member` clause at the call site. Consolidate the doc-comment in one place.

Low priority — functional no-op but reduces drift risk.

Source: post-merge review suggestion on task 2088 (merge commit 90804da34).

tasks.title: Dedup Phase 3 reelaboration in edit_param against Phase 1 (or document baseline counter=2)
tasks.description:
In crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs edit_param (Phase 1 around lines 480-530, Phase 3 around lines 711-770), Phase 1 and Phase 3 can both reelaborate the SAME group when a guard value actually changes. The per-group skip (added by task 2088) does not deduplicate across phases: Phase 1 updates the guard cell in `values`, but `old_guard_val` in Phase 3 is still pulled from `self.eval_state` (pre-edit snapshot), so the comparison correctly detects a real value change and does NOT skip — Phase 3 redoes Phase 1's work.

Not a correctness bug (reelaboration is idempotent), but a missed optimisation. The current counter-baseline of 2 (recently tightened by commit 88dc7a37f) bakes this behavior in.

Choose one:
(a) Track a per-edit `phase1_reelaborated: HashSet<GuardedGroupId>` (or equivalent) populated in Phase 1, consulted in Phase 3 to skip already-processed groups. Update perf-lock tests to expect counter=1 after the dedup.
(b) Document explicitly on the `last_guard_phase_group_evals` field doc-comment (lib.rs:209-215) that Phase 1 and Phase 3 both count each affected group by design, so test authors know why counter==2 is the intended baseline.

Option (a) is the real perf win; option (b) is minimum-effort disclosure.

Source: post-merge review suggestion on task 2088 (merge commit 90804da34).

tasks.title: Clean up test-instrumentation wire-up: drop redundant surface test, document Cargo self-dev-dep
tasks.description:
Two post-merge review suggestions on task 2137 (merge commit c7a6cf62b) to tighten the test-instrumentation feature wire-up in crates/reify-eval.

1. Delete `crates/reify-eval/tests/test_instrumentation_surface.rs`. This file is a standalone compile-time lock asserting that `Engine::last_guard_phase_group_evals()` is reachable from integration tests when `test-instrumentation` is active. However, `tests/edit_source.rs:2012` and `tests/guard_eval.rs:2145` already invoke the accessor with meaningful runtime assertions — if the self-dev-dep entry in Cargo.toml is removed, both of those integration tests will fail to compile just as loudly. The new surface test adds no coverage beyond what already exists; its own module doc even notes that runtime behaviour is locked by guard_eval.rs / edit_source.rs. Removing it trims one test file and one cargo-test target.

2. Add a short `#` comment above line 19 of `crates/reify-eval/Cargo.toml` explaining why the crate depends on itself via `reify-eval = { path = ".", features = ["test-instrumentation"] }`. The self-dev-dep idiom is non-obvious and a future maintainer might try to "clean it up" by removing the apparent circular line, which would break integration tests that use the feature-gated `Engine::last_guard_phase_group_evals()` accessor. Suggested comment: `# Self-dev-dep enables `test-instrumentation`-gated accessors (e.g. Engine::last_guard_phase_group_evals) in integration tests; see src/engine_admin.rs`.

Verify with `cargo test -p reify-eval` after each change.

tasks.title: Dedup Phase 3 reelaboration in edit_source against Phase 1 (mirror of task 2140)
tasks.description:
Apply the symmetric cross-phase dedup in edit_source that task 2140 applied in edit_param. edit_source in crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs has analogous Phase 1 (~line 1448) and Phase 3 (~line 1741) guard re-elaboration logic with the same cross-phase redundancy: Phase 3's old_guard_val is pulled from self.eval_state (pre-edit snapshot), so Phase 3 re-does Phase 1's work when a guard value actually flips. The perf-lock test `edit_source_phase1_and_3_skip_unchanged_guarded_groups` in tests/edit_source.rs:1926 currently asserts counter == 2, baking in the redundancy.

Design (from esc-2140-83):
- Declare `phase1_reelaborated: HashSet<ValueCellId>` before Phase 1.
- Insert after `self.last_guard_phase_group_evals += 1;` at ~line 1498.
- In Phase 3 after the `let Some(guard_val) = ...` guard at ~line 1753, add `if phase1_reelaborated.contains(&group.guard_cell) { continue; }`.
- Update perf-lock assertion in tests/edit_source.rs:1926 from `counter == 2` to `counter == 1`.

Caveat: edit_source has additional skip conditions (added members, role-flipped guard members) that make per-group skip logic more complex than edit_param's. Account for these when wiring the HashSet population point so groups that were re-elaborated for role-flip or added-member reasons are correctly recorded as phase1_reelaborated.

Memory hints: search for "task 2140" to see the edit_param design; see engine_edit.rs:727-753 for the post-wave2 cleanup that uses the phase1_reelaborated set in edit_param.

tasks.title: Reconcile inactive-branch Auto-cell semantics between eval() and edit_param()
tasks.description:
The new post-solver pass in engine_eval.rs:819-827 and 858-866 writes (Undef, Auto) for inactive-branch Auto cells, overwriting values the solver just resolved. Meanwhile `deactivate_if_not_auto` (used by edit_param Phase 1 and the post-wave2 cleanup) PRESERVES the solver's value for Auto cells. This means initial `eval()` and `edit_param()` now produce different observable states for the same guard configuration when an Auto param appears in an inactive branch. The test fixture `edit_param_guard_group_mixed_members_via_edit_param` already had to be rewritten to chase this change from Determined/7mm to Undef/Auto.

Decide which semantics is canonical and make the two paths match, or explicitly document the asymmetry and its rationale in a central place (not just inline comments in each site).

Suggested fix: Either (a) make initial eval use `deactivate_if_not_auto` for inactive-branch Auto cells (preserve solver values, matching edit_param), or (b) update edit_param's deactivation to overwrite Auto cells to (Undef, Auto) (matching new eval). Document the decision in a module-level comment and add a test asserting `eval() → edit_param(no-op)` leaves Auto cells in identical state.

Source: post-merge review suggestion on task 2140.

tasks.title: edit_param post-wave2 cleanup misses Phase-1-skipped-but-dirty-guard groups
tasks.description:
The post-wave2 cleanup in crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs:727-753 only iterates groups that Phase 1 actually re-elaborated (i.e. added to `phase1_reelaborated`). Groups that Phase 1 fired over but skipped via the per-group unchanged-guard short-circuit (line 524) are NOT in the set. Yet wave2 will still re-evaluate any inactive member of such a group whose default_expr reads a resolved auto param — overwriting the cell's prior Undef with a concrete value. Phase 3's per-group skip will then also short-circuit (guard value unchanged), so the corruption never gets repaired.

The existing test `edit_param_wave2_does_not_corrupt_inactive_members` exercises only the Phase-1-fired-for-this-group path; the Phase-1-skipped-but-dirty-guards path is untested.

Suggested fix: Either (a) broaden the cleanup to iterate ALL guarded groups whose guard value is unchanged (re-assert inactive-branch deactivation regardless of whether Phase 1 ran for them), or (b) add a test that constructs two groups — one whose guard changes and one whose guard is unchanged but has an inactive member reading a resolved auto param — and asserts the unchanged group's inactive member stays Undef. If the intent is that this scenario cannot occur in practice, state the precondition and enforce it with a debug_assert.

Source: post-merge review suggestion on task 2140.

tasks.title: Avoid guarded_groups.clone() in edit_param post-wave2 cleanup
tasks.description:
At crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs:728 the post-wave2 cleanup does `new_snapshot.graph.guarded_groups.clone()` to iterate and compare guard_cell against a HashSet. The iteration doesn't mutate the graph, so the clone is only needed because `new_snapshot` is borrowed immutably downstream through `&new_snapshot.graph`. In a structure with many guarded groups this is a wasted allocation per edit_param.

Suggested fix: Re-scope the borrow so the iterator can borrow `&new_snapshot.graph.guarded_groups` directly, or iterate `phase1_reelaborated` itself and look up each group by guard_cell. The latter is also O(|phase1_reelaborated|) rather than O(|guarded_groups|).

Source: post-merge review suggestion on task 2140.

tasks.title: Cross-phase dedup: handle wave2 guard flip in edit_source/edit_param
tasks.description:
The `phase1_reelaborated` HashSet in both edit_param (task 2140) and edit_source (task 2142) dedup assumes Phase 1's re-elaboration is idempotent *for a given guard value*. If the guard cell itself reads a resolved auto param, wave2 can flip the guard between Phase 1 and Phase 3. `reapply_phase1_deactivations` uses the *current* guard_val to deactivate newly-inactive members (correct), but never evaluates the default_expr of newly-active else_members whose default_expr does not read a resolved auto param. Phase 3 then skips the group via `phase1_reelaborated`, leaving those cells Undef when they should be Determined.

Scope: crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs — applies symmetrically to both edit_source (lines ~1860-1868, ~1583-1590) and edit_param (task 2140 path, comment at 1442-1445).

Suggested fix options:
(a) Track guard value at insert time: replace `HashSet<ValueCellId>` with `HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>` and in Phase 3 don't dedup when the current guard value differs from the recorded one (fall through to full re-elaboration, including active-branch default_expr evaluation).
(b) Have `reapply_phase1_deactivations` detect a guard flip versus Phase 1's recorded value and, on flip, fall through to full re-elaboration rather than only re-deactivating.

Add a targeted regression test: source edit that changes a non-guard constraint input, guard reads a resolved auto param, guard flips because of the resolver, else_members' default_exprs are literals (not reading auto params). Verify the newly-active else_members transition from Undef to Determined and match cold-eval results.

Source: post-merge review suggestion on task 2142 (esc-2142-102, reviewer_comprehensive). Inherited from task 2140 — predates this diff.

tasks.title: Test coverage: role-flip/added-member interaction with edit_source cross-phase dedup
tasks.description:
In edit_source Phase 1 (crates/reify-eval/src/engine_edit.rs:1583-1590), the insert into `phase1_reelaborated` sits AFTER the `old_guard_val == Some(&guard_val) && !has_added_in_group && !has_role_flipped_guard_member` skip. Consequently groups reached via the role-flip or added-member triggers — whose guard value did not change — still land in the dedup set, and Phase 3 will skip them.

The new test added with task 2142 only exercises the guard-value-changed path (x=10mm → 3mm). The role-flip/added-member interaction with the new dedup set and the post-wave2 cleanup is not covered by this diff's tests. The existing role-flip test `edit_source_role_flipped_guard_member_matches_cold_eval` predates the dedup.

Suggested fix: Add a regression test that combines a role-flipped guard member + resolver re-evaluation (wave2) + Phase 3 trigger, asserting results remain cold-eval-equivalent. At minimum verify `last_guard_phase_group_evals` matches the expected count. A cheap alternative: extend the existing role-flip cold-eval-equivalence test to also run under a source edit that triggers wave2.

Source: post-merge review suggestion on task 2142 (esc-2142-102, reviewer_comprehensive).

tasks.title: Test coverage: role-flip/added-member + edit_source cross-phase dedup
tasks.description:
edit_source Phase 1 (engine_edit.rs:1583-1590) inserts into phase1_reelaborated AFTER the guard-unchanged skip that also checks !has_added_in_group && !has_role_flipped_guard_member. Groups reached via role-flip or added-member triggers with unchanged guard value still land in the dedup set, so Phase 3 will skip them. Task 2142's new test only exercises the guard-value-changed path; role-flip/added-member interaction with the dedup and post-wave2 cleanup is uncovered.

Fix: Add a regression test combining role-flipped guard member + resolver re-evaluation (wave2) + Phase 3 trigger, asserting cold-eval-equivalent results and expected last_guard_phase_group_evals count. Alternative: extend existing edit_source_role_flipped_guard_member_matches_cold_eval to run under a source edit that triggers wave2.

Source: esc-2142-102, reviewer_comprehensive.
